#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

#  Heating & Cooling Systems 
  1. [Air Duct Repair](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_0)
  2. [Box Fan, Window Fan, Floor Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_1)
  3. [Central Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_2)
  4. [Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_3)
  5. [Heat Pump HVAC](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_4)
  6. [Room Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_5)
  7. [Smart Thermostat](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_6)
  8. [Whole House Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_7)


  1. [Air Duct Repair](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_0)


In a typical home, about 20% of the air is lost due to leaks, holes, and an improperly connected duct system, which results in an increase on your energy bill. A well-designed and properly sealed air duct system can make your home more comfortable and energy efficient.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
Must already have a central electric heating or cooling system 
## Incentive amount 
50% of repair cost (Up to $300)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Air Duct](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27528)
##   

###   

  1. [Box Fan, Window Fan, Floor Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_1)


Any of these fan options provide cooling relief during hot and muggy weather and save on energy costs by reducing the need for turning on your central A/C.  
Additionally, these fans provide cooling in occupied rooms rather than areas not in use.
## Efficiency Criteria
Must be ETL Listed
## Incentive Amount
$20 (maximum 2)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

  

![Box Fan.2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31204)
![Window fan.1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31206)
![Oscillating fan.1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31205)
  

  

  1. [Central Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_2)


ENERGY STAR® certified central air conditioners have a higher seasonal energy efficiency ratio (SEER) and use 8% less energy than conventional new models. Replacing air conditioners that are more than 10 years old with ENERGY STAR certified units can save up to 20% on heating and cooling costs. 
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Minimum 15+ SEER
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](http://energystar.gov) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


  

## Incentive Amount 
$200 per ton
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Heating & Cooling System](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27532)
  

  

  1. [Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_3)


A mini-split heat pump provides a highly efficient heating and cooling system, where ductwork does not exist. Mini-splits help keep each room or spaces a customized temperature via remote access.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * Minimum 15+ SEER rating
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ductless_heating_cooling) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


## Incentive Amount 
$200 per Ton
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27552)
  

  

  

  

  

  

  1. [Heat Pump HVAC](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_4)


A high-efficiency heat pump transfers central air conditioning as well as heating and uses less energy than traditional electric systems. Heat pumps transfer heat in both directions, using the outside air to heat a home in winter and cool it in summer.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Minimum 15+ SEER rating
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/?s=mega) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


## Incentive Amount
$200 per Ton
## Additional Rebate Information
South Coast AQMD offers an additional Heat Pump HVAC rebate that is separate from APU's rebate. Please visit South AQMD link below for additional information and questions:
[www.aqmd.gov/gozero](http://www.aqmd.gov/gozero)
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![AC](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27529)
  

  1. [Room Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_5)


Room air conditioners cool rooms rather than the entire home and can provide cooling only where needed. ENERGY STAR certified room air conditioners can save more energy and money through improved sealing and insulation around the unit to keep you cool and comfortable.   

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-room-air-conditioners/results) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system 


## Incentive Amount
$100 (Maximum of 2)   

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Window AC](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27538)  

  

  1. [Smart Thermostat](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_6)


Unlike programmable or manual thermostats, ENERGY STAR certified smart thermostats can deliver greater convenience, control, and can help you save money on your energy bill. Smart thermostats utilize motion sensors, which recognize when you have left the home and have the potential to learn you daily schedule, making energy-saving temperature changes automatically.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/smart_thermostats)
  * Wi-Fi Enabled 
  * Must be a replacement of an existing thermostat 


## Incentive Amount 
$50 (Maximum of 2) 
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Smart Thermotat](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27534)
  

  1. [Whole House Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_7)


A whole house fan is an efficient way to reduce the use of your air conditioner and maintain a comfortable temperature within your home. A whole house fan draws in cool outside air from open windows and releases excess hot air inside the home through your attic.  

##    
​Installation/Efficiency Criteria  

Permanently mounted in attic  
Central air conditioning existing  

  

## ​Incentive Amount  

$100  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Whole House](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27537)

  7. A/C Tune Up


#  A/C Tune-Up 
The Anaheim Public Utilities A/C Tune-Up Rebate Program provides residential customers a rebate of up to a $100 ($150 for income-qualified customers) for getting an A/C tune-up. Customers are only eligible to participate in this Program once every twelve months and applications must be submitted within 1 year of A/C tune-up service.
### How the Program Works
1. Schedule an A/C tune-up with a licensed HVAC contractor  
2. Apply [online](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/) OR Mail in the following documents:
  * A/C Tune-Up Rebate Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56308/AC-Tune-Up-Application-8_6_24_FILLABLE_ENGLISH) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56309/AC-Tune-Up-Application-8_6_24_FILLABLE_SPANISH)
  * Invoice that details the job performed, amount paid, and serviced date
  * Mail documents to:


Anaheim Public Utilities  
Attn: A/C Tune-Up  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd., Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
Rebates are limited and subject to available funding. Incentive amount not to exceed purchase price.
  

### Qualifying Income Levels
[*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).](https://www.anaheim.net/6423)
### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)

#  Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting (Residential) 
## The Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting Program offers high-efficiency outdoor lighting to keep your home bright and safe.
### **Increase visibility with dusk-to-dawn and security lights.**
![20W Res Security Light w. Motion Sensor](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28841)
![D2D Greenlite Light Bulb](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=25051)
![115w PaR38](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28852)
Outdoor security light with dusk-to-dawn function and motion detection. 
LED A19 bulb with dusk to dawn features allows any light fixture to turn on at night and off in the morning. 
LED Par 38 bulb with dusk-to-dawn features allows a celling or can light fixture to turn on at night and off in the morning. 
###  **Program Criteria**  
  

  * Participants must be Anaheim Public Utilities electric customers with current and non-delinquent accounts.
  * Lights must be installed within the 30-day period. For new hardwired fixtures, it is recommended that participants obtain multiple quotes from licensed electricians and also obtain a permit from the building department for installation.
  * Lights must be maintained and used for a minimum of five years from installation.
  * Participants must provide access for inspection of the installed fixture(s).
  * Participants are eligible to receive up to two (2) free light fixtures for their residential home.


Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. This program is subject to available funding.
To view multi-family and commercial property Dusk-to-Dawn lighting options, please visit our [Commercial Dusk-to-Dawn Program webpage](https://anaheim.net/2628/Dusk-to-Dawn-Lighting).
###  [**Qualifying Income Levels**](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)  
  

Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).
### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.

Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.   

[![A/C Tune-up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28494)](https://www.anaheim.net/3436/)

#  Home Appliance & Fixtures 
  1. [Air Purifier](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_0)
  2. [Ceiling Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_1)
  3. [Dishwasher](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_2)
  4. [Dusk-to-Dawn Security Light](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_3)
  5. [Heat Pump Dryer](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_4)
  6. [Heat Pump Water heater](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_5)
  7. [Refrigerator](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_6)


  1. [Air Purifier](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_0)


Room air purifiers are portable electric appliances that remove fine particles, such as dust and pollen, from indoor air. ENERGY STAR® certified room air purifiers are 40% more energy efficient than standard models, saving customers about $30 annually on energy costs. Using ENERGY STAR portable purifiers is a great way to improve indoor air quality in a single room or area while using energy efficiently.
### Incentive Amount
### $30 (Maximum of 2)
### $50 (Maximum of 2) **with UV light**
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/air_cleaners) Certified
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/air_cleaners) Certified **with UV light**


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-848939714](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27169)
  

  1. [Ceiling Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_1)


ENERGY STAR® certified ceiling fans are 40% more efficient than conventional fan/light units. These high-quality, innovative fans use improved motors and blade design, and the latest in energy efficient technology.
### Incentive Amount
### $20 (Maximum of 3)
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ceiling_fans)[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ceiling_fans) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1059752578](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27474)
  

  1. [Dishwasher](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_2)


A standard-sized ENERGY STAR® certified dishwasher costs about $35 per year to run and saves an average of 3,870 gallons of water over the lifetime of the device. 
### Incentive Amount 
### $50
###   

### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/dishwashers) Certified


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![istockphoto-1077858850-1024x1024](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27480)
  

  

  1. [Dusk-to-Dawn Security Light](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_3)


Keep your home bright and safe with high efficiency outdoor lighting that detects natural light and turns off during the day and on at night. Customers can now purchase a new LED fixture and apply for a rebate to offset the cost.
### Incentive Amount
### $20 (Maximum of 2)
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * ### LED fixture only


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
###    
  
![20W Res Security Light w. Motion Sensor](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28985)  
  
  
  
  

  1. [Heat Pump Dryer](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_4)


Heat pump dryers take in ambient air, heat it, and recirculate it in the dryer to maintain the temperature without using much energy. This dryer passes humid air in the dryer drum through a condenser to remove the moisture without losing too much heat. Heat Pump dryers can save 20-60 percent more than conventional clothes dryers. 
### Incentive Amount
### $200
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-clothes-dryers/results) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1092362272](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27018)
###   

  1. [Heat Pump Water heater](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_5)


ENERGY STAR® certified electric storage water heaters use a highly efficient heat pump to transfer heat from the surrounding air to the water. Heat pump water heaters save a family of four approximately $330 per year on their electric bill. While a certified heat pump water heater costs more upfront, the savings will pay back the difference in approximately two years.
### Incentive Amount
### $400  
  

### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-water-heaters/results) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1023034238](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27520)
  

  

  1. [Refrigerator](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_6)


Recent improvements in insulation and compressors have made today’s refrigerators use much less energy than older models. ENERGY STAR® certified refrigerators are about 9% more energy efficient than models that meet the federal minimum standard.
## Incentive Amount
### $50
For standard rebate: Apply Online OR Download Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50765/Home-Incentives-App-872023) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50770/Home-Incentives-App-SPANISH-872023)
### $200
For income-qualified customers submit the following: 
-[ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50765/Home-Incentives-App-872023) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50770/Home-Incentives-App-SPANISH-872023)  
AND  
- Income-Qualified Rebate Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50763/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_2023)[ ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50763/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_2023)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50764/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_SPANISH)  
**-**[**Qualifying Income Levels**](https://www.anaheim.net/6423/)
### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ### Replacement of an existing refrigerator
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-residential-refrigerators/results) Certified
  * ### Must be greater than 7.75 cubic feet


###  [Guidelines and how to apply](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)  
  

### Receive an additional $50 when you recycle your Refrigerator/Freezer[](http://www.anaheim.net/937/Refrigerator-Recycling-Program)[**

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### For any questions or to learn more about the program, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.

  7. Rebate Guidelines & Application


#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)


### Contact Us
  1. ## **Rebate Processing**
  
714-408-4161 or Email

  7. Leak Repair Rebate Program


#  Leak Repair Rebate Program 
## Program Overview
This program offers rebates to customers who repair water leaks in and around their residences, including but not limited to, slab leaks, irrigation systems, and water faucet leaks. This program provides single-family customers with a water account a rebate of up to $500 for income-qualified residents, or $250 for non-income-qualified residents for the repair of water leaks, including materials and labor. 
## How It Works
To be eligible for the $500 rebate, Anaheim Public Utilities customers must be at or below the 80% Orange County median level income or customers must be a current participant in an income-qualified program with Anaheim Public Utilities. Qualifying customers will be eligible for reimbursement. All customers can request rebates for multiple leak repairs at the same single-family residence, and a customer can file subsequent applications on future water leak repairs, subject to a total program cap of $500 for income qualified residents or $250 for non-income qualified residents. 
## How To Apply
1. Complete [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/31624/Leak-Repair-Application) in full and attach the following required documents
  * Copy of your most recent Anaheim Public Utilities water bill 
  * Copies of all corresponding invoices, receipts, checks or other documents showing expenditures of leak repair 
  * Documentation of leak - e.g. spike in water bill, report after inspection of leak by APU staff, or invoice documenting leak 


2. Submit Application and supporting documents to:  
  

  

## [Qualifying Income Levels](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).

#  Water Rebates 
Anaheim Public Utilities, in collaboration with the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California’s SoCal Water$mart Program, offers incentives and programs for indoor and outdoor water-saving devices. Replacing inefficient devices can provide water savings and a reduced water bill.
[![Efficient Clothes Washer Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29160)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/clothes-washers/)
[![Leak Repair Rebate Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38395)](https://www.anaheim.net/5525/)
[![Household Flow Sensors Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38383)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/flow-monitoring-device-pilot-program/)
[![High-Efficiency Toilet Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28506)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/toilets/)
[![Rain Barrel & Cistern Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28508)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/rain-barrels-cisterns/)
[![Rotating Sprinkler Nozzles Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28509)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/sprinkler-nozzles/)
[![Soil Sensor Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28511)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/soil-moisture-sensors/)
[![Smart Meter Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28510)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/irrigation-controllers/)
# Water Programs  

Participate in one of our water-saving programs to help minimize your reliance on water usage inside and outside of your home. We can perform an in-home evaluation of your water and electric usage and even help with the costs associated with a landscape transformation project.  

[![Home Utility Check-Up Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28513)](https://www.anaheim.net/475/)
[![TreePower Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28514)](https://www.anaheim.net/763/)
[![Turf Replacement Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28515)](https://www.anaheim.net/1062/)
[![Zero Turf Landscape Design Brochure](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=55456)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66230/Landscape-Brochure-2024-v03)

#  Insulation & Building Projects 
  1. [Attic Fan (Solar/Standard)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_0)
  2. [Exterior Wall Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_1)
  3. [High Performance Windows](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_2)
  4. [Window Film](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_3)
  5. [Fire Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_4)
  6. [Fire Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_5)


  1. [Attic Fan (Solar/Standard)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_0)


Solar or standard attic fans run on sun power and pull superheated air out of your attic to be replaced by cooler outside air. This reduces the amount of work your HVAC system has to do to cool your upstairs living area, which is especially useful if you have an upstairs bonus room or bedroom that gets uncomfortably hot in summer. 
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Solar: permanently mounted on the roof or in the attic
  * Standard: permanently mounted on a roof or in the attic with a smart thermostat


##    
Incentive Amount
Both: $30 
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Attic Fan ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28990)
  

  

  1. [Exterior Wall Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_1)


Exterior wall insulation reduces the transfer of cold or warm air by filling the space between your walls with a layer of fiber material. Many older homes have little to no exterior wall insulation, resulting in unnecessarily high heating and cooling energy costs. Retrofitting exterior wall insulation can yield significant energy savings as well as enhanced comfort.   

##    
​Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Adding a minimum of R-13+

  

## ​Incentive Amount   

$0.10 per sq. ft.  
  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![iStock-155447087](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27523)  

  1. [High Performance Windows](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_2)


Heat gain and loss through windows account for up to 50% of a home’s heating and cooling needs. Many technological improvements have been made in recent years that have advanced the insulating quality of windows which can help lower your energy costs.
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/residential_windows_doors_and_skylights/key_product_criteria#:~:text=With%20the%20equivalent%20energy%20performance,than%20or%20equal%20to%200.42)® certified
  * U-Factor of 0.28 or less 
  * Solar Heat Gain Coefficient of 0.23 or less 


  

## Incentive Amount 
$1 per sq. ft.
  

  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Insulation & Building Projects](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27524)
  

  1. [Window Film](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_3)


There are many different grades, shades, colors, and thicknesses of window films built to offer solutions to a variety of challenges including heat and glare reduction, thermal insulation, UV filtration and even safety and security.   
  

## ​Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Solar Heat Gain Coefficient of 0.39 or less
  * Only single-pane, exterior windows qualify. North-facing windows excluded

  

## ​Incentive Amount  

$1.00 per sq. ft.  
  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![iStock-135374538](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27525)  
  

  1. [Fire Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_4)


Fire-resistant attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance.   
  
​
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  

  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating   
(Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater  



## ​Incentive Amount   

$0.35 per sq. ft.  
Rebate cap of $750  
  

  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  

![iStock-183779679](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27526)  

  

  1. [Fire Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_5)


These vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires
  

Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is Recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas ](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


## Incentive Amount
Up to $350 rebate 
  

  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/) for more information about fire safety.
  

![Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28987)
  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

#  Manage Your Utilities Online 
  * [ LOG IN ](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150)


  * [ PAY MY BILL ](https://apu51.ez-pay.io/validate.aspx?BillerId=VmicfoDAU6)


## What is MY ACCOUNT?
MY ACCOUNT is a convenient way to manage your Anaheim Public Utilities account online. Among other great features, it allows you to:
  * Pay your bill
  * Request a payment extension
  * Request a duplicate bill
  * Enroll in paperless billing
  * Enroll recurring payments 
  * Update your mailing address 
  * View billing and payment details 
  * View usage history


Find out more by visiting the [MY ACCOUNT FAQ](https://anaheim.net/faq.aspx?TID=25) page.

  7. Fire Safety 


#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *

#  Pool Equipment 
  1. [Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/Pool-Equipment#tabd630a06f-79d3-47c4-9271-77e577b22677_0)


  1. [Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/Pool-Equipment#tabd630a06f-79d3-47c4-9271-77e577b22677_0)


Variable speed (VS) motors can use 65% less energy than standard pool pumps and can get the same job done. Unlike single speed motors that only operate at maximum flow rates, variable speed pool pumps use efficient motors and advanced hydraulic designs to deliver the appropriate flow for water filtration and cleaning.
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Variable speed pool pumps and motors
  * Must be a replacement of an existing pool pump


  

## Incentive Amount 
$300 per pump
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27542)
  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

  7. Battery Storage


#  Battery Storage 
Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
##    
Installation/Efficiency Guidelines 
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be hardwired and part of an existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Developmental-Schedule-D-TOU-2-050119) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920/myPower-Savings) for at least 12 months
  * Please see program application for additional program guidelines 


## Rebate Amount
Up to $3,000 per household   
Guidelines on how to [ap](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)[ply](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24).  
  

## Customer Owned Solar Energy
Anaheim has supported more than 3,500 customers installing solar throughout the City. Beginning January 1, 2021, Anaheim Public Utilities will be transitioning to Net Energy Metering (NEM) 2.0, which is designed to offer the most benefit to customers who also install an energy storage system or are able to change their daily energy use to align with the needs of the grid. NEM programs are designed to credit electric customers for excess energy produced by their solar panels and delivered back on the electric grid. For more information about Anaheim's NEM 2.0 and customer owned solar, please [click here](https://www.anaheim.net/636)
  

![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34164)

#  Back-Up Power 
  1. [Uninterruptible Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_0)
  2. [Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_1)


  1. [Uninterruptible Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_0)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines. 
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
[ENERGY STAR® ](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-uninterruptible-power-supplies/results)Certified (for standard UPS only)
## Incentive Amount
$50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
$200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving [Medical Lifeline Rate](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=) (Maximum of 2)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27539)
  

  

  

  1. [Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_1)


A portable power station is a rechargeable battery-powered generator that can supply power to multiple devices at the same time. In addition, medical portable power stations are also available to help keep your equipment on during any loss of power. Best of all, you may be eligible to receive up to two (2) rebates for purchasing eligible Portable Power Stations, per residential address.   
  

## ​Product Criteria   

Battery Powered (minimum 100watt AC output)  
  

## ​Incentive Amount   

$50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)  
$200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving [Medical Lifeline Rate](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=) (Maximum of 2)  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34153)  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

  5. Bill Assistance


#  Bill Assistance 
We understand that some of our customers may experience financial setbacks or hardships.
We offer several options to assist with bill payment and help get you back on track. We also provide the necessary documentation you may need to seek third-party assistance.  
  
Give us a call at 714-765-3300 and we'll walk you through the best options for your situation. 
  1. [Bill Deferrals](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_0)


We understand that some of our customers may experience financial setbacks or hardships. For those who have fallen behind, we offer payment extensions and will work with you to get you back on track.
## Extensions & Payment Plans
A customer may request a 10 calendar day extension if they are not able to pay by the bill due date.
For past due bills requiring a longer period of time to pay, a customer alternatively may request a payment plan. See [Deferred Payment Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2045/Deferred-Payment-Assistance-PDF?bidId=) for more information about each option.
## Monthly Billing
Monthly Billing is a monthly bill for bi-monthly billed customers. See [Monthly Billing](https://www.anaheim.net/formcenter/public-utilities-6/monthly-billing-enrollment-form-130) to read more about the billing option and submit a form for enrollment.
## Level Pay
Level Pay is a monthly bill plan where a customer may make an average payment based on their last 12 months of previous usage. See [Level Pay](https://anaheim.net/6537/Level-Pay-Plan) for more information.
  1. [EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_1)


The Anaheim Public Utilities Emergency Assistance Program provides financial assistance to qualified residential customers facing temporary financial hardship. Eligible customers may receive a credit of **$250 toward electric service and $100 for water service,** once every three (3) years as emergency payment assistance.
  * [Emergency Assistance Application](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Emergency-Assistance-Application-373)


  1. [INCOME-QUALIFIED PROGRAMS](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_2)


Customers who meet income-qualified guidelines may be eligible to participate in a number of income-qualified programs. 
Programs include senior, military veteran, and long-term disabled discounts, community-produced solar bill credit, as well as home efficiency programs. See [Income-Qualified Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/2546/Income-Qualified-Programs) for more information.
  * [Income-Qualified Programs Application (English)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25981/Income-Qualified-Energy--Water-Discount-Application--?bidId=)
  * [Income-Qualified Programs Application (Spanish)](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25982/Income-Qualified-Energy--Water-Discount-Application-Spanish?bidId=)


  1. [FEDERAL LOW INCOME ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_3)


### Electric Bill Assistance Program
Anaheim customers can receive utility payment assistance on their **electric bill** through the federally-funded **Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)** administered through the Community Action Partnership of Orange County (CAPOC).   
  
Funding is based on monthly income. Customers may view income qualifications and apply online or call CAPOC at (714) 839-6199 for more information. Once approved, a bill credit will be **directly applied to your account**.
  * [Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)](https://www.caliheapapply.com/)


  1. [MEDICAL ALLOWANCES](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_4)


If you or someone who lives in your home uses **life-support equipment** , you may be eligible to pay a lower rate for some of your electricity. View the application below for details and a list of qualifying medical equipment. 
  * [Medical Lifeline Allowance Application](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369)


  1. [THIRD PARTY ASSISTANCE](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_5)


The following third-party organizations provide assistance with paying your Anaheim Public Utilities electric bill.* These organizations are not affiliated with the City of Anaheim but have been essential partners in assisting those in need.
  * [Anaheim Community Foundation (ACF)](https://anaheimcf.org/anaheim-emergency-assistance/) - offers support to help cover rent, utility bills, and household essentials through Anaheim Family Resource Centers.  
  

  * [Community Action Partnership (CAP)](https://capoc.org/)  
11870 Monarch Street, Garden Grove, CA 92841  
714-839-6199 or 800-660-4232  
  

  * [Share Our Selves (SOS)](https://www.shareourselves.org/) - offers health services, bill assistance, and other programs in Orange County.  
1550 Superior Ave., Costa Mesa, CA 92627  
949-642-3451   
  

  * [The Salvation Army](http://www.salvationarmyusa.org/) - provides various assistance programs including bill assistance, job training, and food pantries.  
1515 W. North St. Anaheim, CA 92801  
714-783-2344  
  

  * [2-1-1 Orange County](https://www.211oc.org/) - an information and referral service that connects people to programs for housing, job placement, food, and health insurance services.  
949-646-4357 (local)


*All assistance is subject to meeting each agency's eligibility requirements and is reliant upon available funding.
  1. [Third Party Water Bill](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_6)


Customers who meet program guidelines may be eligible for Anaheim Public Utilities’ [Income-Qualified Water Rebate Program](https://anaheim.net/6627/Income-Qualified-Third-Party-Water-Bill-). The program provides**u****p to a $120 rebate per year** to qualifying customers who receive a **third-party water bill**.
  * [Income Qualified Water Rebate Application (English)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58586/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application-as-of-010925_)
  * [Income Qualified Water Rebate Application (Spanish)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58640/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application_Spanish)


* * *

### Fraud Alert
Received a suspicious call?  
Hang up and call 714-765-3300 or 311 (within city limits).

Fri: 7 am - 5:30 pm

Latest News
  1. [![PR graphic Festival](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=69988)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
####  [Anaheim approves Anaheim Hills Festival apartment project ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
An extensively considered and highly debated proposal for apartments at Anaheim Hills Festival shopping center was approved Tuesday night by Anaheim’s City Council. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
  2. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
####  [Latest: Ament requests withdraw guilty plea in court with decision pending; running timeline ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
Anaheim has condemned actions documented in a federal investigation of the city's former mayor and a former Anaheim Chamber of Commerce executive that came to light in 2022. Here is what has happened, how Anaheim has responded and ongoing updates. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
  3. [![Image to go with a press release for Anaheim Contigo and information on immigration enforcement](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
####  [Anaheim Contigo: go to for timely immigration enforcement information ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
Anaheim Contigo is your source for timely information about federal immigration enforcement activity in our city. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
  4. [![20250514-DSC00828](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
####  [New state law covers space near crosswalks for safer streets ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
A new California law limits how close you can park near crosswalks in Anaheim neighborhoods and across the state. The state law aims to improve visibility and make streets safer for kids, families and everyone who walks, bikes or drives. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
  5. [![center greens ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
####  [Center Greens Park debuts downtown ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
Center Greens Park debuted downtown Saturday, transforming a grassy, informal space into an iconic park with a new skate park, walking paths, butterfly garden and more for community to enjoy. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
  6. [![news flash La Palma groundbreaking](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
####  [La Palma Park Way Promenade Groundbreaking ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
Steeped in history, La Palma Park promenade project starts multiyear transformation of iconic park [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
  7. [![two volunteers building a rock climbing wall for new playground at little people's park ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
####  [Iconic downtown Anaheim park gets new kid-designed, volunteer-built, donated playground ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
One of Anaheim’s most iconic parks got a new playground designed by neighborhood kids and built in a day by more than 100 volunteers from The CarMax Foundation, KABOOM!, Anaheim Family YMCA and the city of Anaheim. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
  8. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
####  [Anaheim shares state audit, supports recommendations with adoption underway ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
Anaheim has shared a state audit critical of contract and funding oversight involving Visit Anaheim and the Anaheim Chamber of Commerce and welcomes the report’s recommendations with adoption already underway. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)

  5. About the Utilities


#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  2. [About Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)

#  Departments 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
The City of Anaheim is a City Council/City Manager form of government.
  2. ####  [City Attorney](https://www.anaheim.net/439/City-Attorney)
View the services the City Attorney provides the City of Anaheim.
  3. ####  [Boards & Commissions](https://www.anaheim.net/176/Boards-Commissions)
The City of Anaheim has a number of boards and commissions on which citizens serve and make recommendations to the City Council in an advisory capacity. 
  4. ####  [City Clerk](https://www.anaheim.net/218/City-Clerk)
The City Clerk is responsible for elections, records, and passport services.
  5. ####  [City Council](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)
The City of Anaheim aims to continually reinvent itself to be a better place for all who live, do business in, or visit our great city.
  6. ####  [Community Services](https://www.anaheim.net/6094/Community-Services)
The goal of the Community Services Department is to enrich individuals, families, and the community through the provision of services, facilities, and programs which improve the quality of life in Anaheim.
  7. ####  [Convention, Sports & Entertainment](https://www.anaheim.net/2595/Convention-Sports-Entertainment)
The Anaheim Convention Center, Sports and Entertainment consists of entertainment facilities such as Angel Stadium of Anaheim, the Honda Center, the Anaheim Convention Center, the National Grove of Anaheim. It also includes the transportation facility known as ARTIC.
  8. ####  [Economic Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6280/Economic-Development)
Find a list of services the Economic Development Department provided.
  9. ####  [Finance](https://www.anaheim.net/479/Finance)
The Finance Department assists other departments in the city in meeting their service objectives by allocating and monitoring the city's financial resources, processing payroll and other financial transactions and procuring goods and services.
  10. ####  [Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/6096/Fire-Rescue)
Access the various fire and emergency services performed by Anaheim Fire & Rescue.
  11. ####  [Housing and Community Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6363/Housing-and-Community-Development)
The Community Development Department implements a variety of programs to foster a better quality of life. These programs create new residential opportunities, revitalize neighborhoods and create job training opportunities.
  12. ####  [Human Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/401/Human-Resources)
Human Resources partners with city departments to hire, compensate, support and create a workforce dedicated to delivering high-quality services, which makes Anaheim a special place to live, work and play.
  13. ####  [Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/490/Planning-Building)
Find information about permits, building, licenses, code enforcement, economic development, historic preservation, and planning and zoning.
  14. ####  [Police Department](https://pd.anaheim.net/)
Access information about the Police Department's programs, what initiatives it's focusing on and more information about its different divisions.
  15. ####  [Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)
Find more information about the Public Utilities Department, how to receive rebates and incentives and general information about city utilities.
  16. ####  [Public Works](https://www.anaheim.net/229/Public-Works)
Public Works provides a myriad of comprehensive services to meet a variety of the city's infrastructure, construction, and development needs.

  2. Disability & Civil Rights Program


#  Disability & Civil Rights Program 
Accessibility  
In accordance with the requirements of Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), and ADA Amendments Act of 2008, the City of Anaheim (city) does not discriminate against qualified individuals with disabilities on the basis of disability in its services, programs, or activities.  
  
The City of Anaheim under Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 as amended in 1972, 1978, and 1992, shall not, on the ground of race, color, or national origin, exclude any person from participation in, deny any person the benefits of, or subject any person to discrimination under any program or activity receiving Federal financial assistance.  
  
Purpose  
The City of Anaheim is committed to ensuring that people with disabilities are able to take part in, and benefit from, the whole range of public programs, services, and activities offered by the city. Further, the city is committed to ensuring that people served shall not be denied the benefits of, or be subjected to discriminate on the basis of race, color, sex, age, disability, or national origin. The city continues to modify its facilities, programs, policies, or practices, as necessary to ensure such access is provided.  
  
Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act and Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 requires that public entities adopt and publish grievance procedures to assure the prompt and equitable resolution of complaints. The purpose of the grievance procedure is to resolve as promptly as possible any problems, complaints, or conflicts related to the city's compliance without the need for the complainant to resort to other remedies available under the law.  
  
Alternate Format  
Any person who requires a disability-related modification or accommodation, including auxiliary aids or services, in order to participate in the public meeting may request, at least 72 hours, in advance such modification, accommodation, aid or service by contacting the department or program. Click on Department Liaison list to select the appropriate department to make your request. To enable us to respond in a manner that will be of most help to you, please indicate the nature of the accessibility need, your preferred format (electronic format, large print, etc.) the web address to send or respond to regarding the requested material, and your full contact information so we can reach you if questions arise while fulfilling your request.

#  Anaheim Public Utilities 
## Proposals (RFP) and Bids for:
  * [Capital Project Advertisements](https://www.anaheim.net/309/)
  * [Bids & Requests for Proposals](https://www.anaheim.net/630/Bids-Requests-for-Proposals-RFPs)


## LSE Commercial Interest Allocation Points - California Independent System Operator (CAISO)
On September 30, 2024, FERC approved the CAISO’s Track 2 proposal for Phase 1 of the Interconnection Process Enhancements 2023 (IPE) initiative. The IPE proposal aims to streamline and reduce the number of projects backlogged in the interconnection queue process, all while coordinating resource procurement, interconnection, resource planning, and transmission planning.
CAISO has developed heightened requirements to complete an interconnection request that necessitates a greater level of project readiness to advance to the interconnection study process. In addition to the new readiness requirements required in FERC Order No. 2023, CAISO is implementing specific criteria to score and prioritize interconnection requests. The main criteria are commercial interest, project viability, and system need. Commercial interest evaluations will incorporate preliminary, non-binding feedback on specific projects from load-serving entities, such as Anaheim Public Utilities (APU).
APU is bound by APU Department Order No. 350 for all procurement of long-term energy-related resources by APU, including awarding of LSE Commercial Interest Points. For more information on APU ‘s selection criteria and consideration factors for awarding Commercial Interest Points please click [here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12740/350-Long-Term-Energy-Resources-?bidId=).
### LSE Commercial Interest Points Contact
714-765-4471 or Email

  5. Who Governs the Utilities


#  Who Governs the Utilities? 
**The[City Council ](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)represents residents and businesses in Anaheim and is made up of Anaheim's mayor, who is elected at large, and six City Council members elected by voters in their districts. **
**The Anaheim City Council****appoints****a 7 member** [**Public Utilities Board**](https://www.anaheim.net/1585/Public-Utilities-Board)**to****makes recommendations to the City Council concerning the operation of electric, water, and other public utilities under the management of the Public Utilities Department.**
## Public Utilities Board Responsibilities
  * Make recommendations concerning the establishment of rates, rules, and regulations for the operation of the electrical and water utilities of the City.
  * Make recommendations concerning the acquisition, construction, improvement, extension, enlargement, diminution, or curtailment of all or any part of the electric, water and any other public utilities under the operation and/or management of the Public Utilities Department.
  * Recommend financing, including the issuance of bonds for the electric and water utilities.
  * Review annual budget of the department and make recommendations accordingly.
  * Establish a Public Utilities Hearing Board composed of 3 current members of the Public Utilities Board.
  * Perform other related duties as outlined in the Municipal Code and such additional duties and functions as may be required from time to time by specific action and direction of the City Council.


## Compensation
There is no compensation for being a member of the Public Utilities Board.
## Term
Members serve for 2 consecutive 4-year terms and may not concurrently serve on 2 or more City boards and commissions.
## Conflict Disclosure
Members are required to complete and file, with the City Clerk, an annual Statement of Economic Interest, Form 700.
## Application/Selection Process
Scheduled and unscheduled vacancies are advertised by the City Clerk. Applicants must complete and submit their application online at [City of Anaheim: Board Application](https://anaheim.granicus.com/boards/forms/283/apply/4616798?code=f76c0456-7461-47b9-9f11-ccc6b9518933). 
Applicants will be forwarded to Council for their consideration. At an agenized Council meeting, nominations are made from the Council floor and voted on by Council.
## References
  * Anaheim City Charter §900
  * Municipal Code §1.04.700-730
  * Ordinance Nos. 3557(§1); 5397(§1)
  * 5774(§24). [5851 and 6066]


![awt.jpg](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=15676)

![Anaheim Anytime logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35594)
**Anaheim Anytime** provides our residents, businesses and visitors the ability to **share problems, issues or other concerns they see in their neighborhood or around town** with the city 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
Anaheim Anytime is our **p****artnership with our community to address issue and maintain neighborhoods**. Use it to report **graffiti, abandoned items, streetlight outages** and other issues. It's the most effective way to bring issues to our attention, with every Anaheim Anytime inquiry **assigned and followed up on**. 
**Please note:** Requests are monitored during business hours. If you have an **emergency, please call 911**. If you have a **non-emergency police issue** , question or request, please call (714) 765-1900.
