#  Rates, Rules & Regulations 
## Electric Utility
The electric tariff schedules have been approved by resolution by the City Council of the City of Anaheim and are the effective rates and rules of the Electric Utility. The City Council may amend or cancel these rates and rules by resolution. No official or employee of the City of Anaheim or the Electric Utility has any authority to waive, alter, or amend these tariff schedules in any respect, except in the manner provided by the tariff schedules.
  * [Title Page](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1963/)
  * [Introduction](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1960/)
  * [Table of Contents](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1962/)
  * [Electric Utility Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1961/)
  * [Rate Schedules](https://www.anaheim.net/653/)
  * [Rules](https://www.anaheim.net/883/)


## Water Utility
The water tariff schedules have been approved by resolution by the City Council of the City of Anaheim and are the effective rates and rules of the Water Utility. The City Council may amend or cancel these rates and rules by resolution. No official or employee of the City of Anaheim or the Water Utility has any authority to waive, alter, or amend these tariff schedules in any respect, except in the manner provided by the tariff schedules.
  * [Title Page](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1967/)
  * [Introduction](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1964/)
  * [Table of Contents](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1966/)
  * [Water Utility Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1965/)
  * [Rates Schedules](https://www.anaheim.net/891/)
  * [Rules](https://www.anaheim.net/894/)

#  Income-Qualified Programs 
You may be eligible for one or more of our income-qualified programs if your household income meets eligibility guidelines. Click on the pictures below for more information and how to apply.

#  How-To Guides 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [How to Get Started – California Friendly Garden ](http://www.bewaterwise.com/conservation_Tips.shtml)
Wondering how to get started to make your garden more California Friendly® ? You don’t have to do a major renovation. Here are some ideas that will save water, improve plant health, and reduce run-off – all California Friendly® .
  2. ####  [How to Select California Friendly Plants Video ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eYPqqj-tc7s)
Dr. Darren Haver explains how to select beautiful California-friendly, drought-tolerant plants for your landscape.
  3. ####  [How to Adjust Your Sprinklers at Home Video ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TbKB0yOLGf4)
Learn how to save water and eliminate runoff by adjusting your sprinklers at home.
  4. ####  [How to Eliminate Runoff from Your Sprinklers Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DKiI8M3OGkc)
Learn how to save water and eliminate runoff by adjusting your sprinklers at home.
  5. ####  [How to Choose Weather Based Irrigation Controller](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Smart_Controller_Brochure_2014.pdf)
A smart sprinkler controller automatically adjusts irrigation schedules in response to changing weather conditions. It monitors information such as rain, wind, temperature, humidity, soil moisture, the amount of time it takes for water to evaporate and plant type to calculate the amount of water needed to keep plants healthy.
  6. ####  [How to Choose Water-Efficient Sprinkler Nozzles](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Rotating_Nozzles_Brochure_2014.pdf)
The latest innovation in sprinkler technology are nozzles that turn a standard pop-up spray head into a precision device that can save water in your landscape. In addition, water jetting from these nozzles is more resistant to wind, less likely to mist, and significantly reduces run-off onto streets and sidewalks. 
  7. ####  [How to Choose a Water-Efficient Clothes Washer](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Choosing_Washer_Brochure_2014.pdf)
A simple rule of thumb is to look for a front-loading washer instead of a typical top-loading one. frontloading washers use about half of the water to wash a full load of laundry, compared to traditional top loaders. 
  8. ####  [How to Save Water Outdoors and Indoors ](http://www.bewaterwise.com/tips.shtml)
Up to 70% of your annual water use is outside your home … watering your lawn and garden, and keeping your property clean. Landscaping with native and drought-tolerant plants, and following these simple tips will help you use water more efficiently and save money on your water bill.


[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  7. La Palma Water Complex


#  La Palma Water Complex 
![La Palma 2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23304)
![La Palma 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23305)
The La Palma Complex serves the residences and businesses of Central and West Anaheim providing water supply and peak pumping capacity to fight fires and meet maximum day water demands. The facilities include the followings:
  * (1) 4 million gallon (4 MG) water storage reservoir that stores water
  * (1) well that pumps groundwater to fill reservoir
  * (1) pump Station that pumps water from reservoir to water distribution system


The dedication for the La Palma Water Complex was held on July 31, 2018.

  5. [Save Water](https://www.anaheim.net/717/Save-Water)
  6. WaterSmart Landscape Award


#  WaterSmart Landscape Award 
## Show Off Your Landscape
Anaheim Public Utilities hosts the annual WaterSmart Landscape Award program. The contest is open to Anaheim residents and businesses that have implemented California-Friendly landscapes that excel in sustainable landscape design, plant selection, water use efficiency, and creativity. Winners will receive a WaterSmart Landscaping Care Kit, WaterSmart Landscape Awardee Yard Sign, and be recognized at an Anaheim Public Utilities Board Meeting! As well as:
  * **Residential landscape winners will receive a $250 gift card to Home Depot or Lowes**
  * **Commercial landscape winners will receive a $500 gift card to Home Depot or Lowes**


### **Award application will reopen in Spring 2026.**
## Ways To Nominate: 
  1. Complete the [ONLINE FORM](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/WaterSmart-Landscape-Award-Nominations-172)
  2. Email entries to akanzler@anaheim.net
  3. Call in entries to 714-765-4218


### Provide detailed information, i.e.: 
  * Name 
  * Address 
  * Short description explaining what you liked about the landscape 


### Past WaterSmart Landscape Award Winners
  1. ![2025-WSLA-Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66289)
  2. ![2025-WSLA-Res-Runner-Up](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66291)

## Judging Criteria
###    
1. Appropriate Plant Selection
  * California Friendly®/native plants


###  2. Project Creativity
  * Creative and innovative landscape design


###  3. Landscape Design
  * Plant color, textures, form, repetition, organization, and unity will be considered


###  4. Irrigation System
  * Designed to reduce runoff, use of water-efficient irrigation (drip system, micro spray, rotating nozzles, etc.)


Note: Only front yard, parkways, and common areas are eligible. Artificial turf projects are ineligible.
## Program Guidelines  
  

### Participating nominees will need to submit the following:
  * Short description of your project (include plant list)
  * Submit 4 color photos of landscaped area (include 2 wide-angle view photos) 
  * Provide a signed waiver to release photos to the public 
  * Copy of your water bill
    * Must have an active Anaheim Public Utilities water account


### Winners will be notified directly by phone.
### Contact Us
  1. ## Save Water
  
714-765-4250 or Email

Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

![2015 WaterSmart Landscape Awardee](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=11096)
##   

## Turf Replacement Program
For a limited time, the Turf Replacement Program rebate is $4 per square foot of turf removed (up to 5,000 sq. ft. for residential and 50,000 sq. ft. for commercial customers). Turf must be replaced with California Friendly® plants. Please visit [socalwatersmart.com](http://socalwatersmart.com) to view additional program requirements and begin the application process.

#  Experiencing an Outage? 
## Please call 911 to report a fire or medical emergency. DO NOT approach [downed power lines](https://www.anaheim.net/4754/Power-Outage-Safety).
**_View outage maps below for status on current outages._**
[![report an outage Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34455)](https://www.anaheim.net/2671/Anaheim-Anytime-Request?id=5804)
[![electric outage map](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34458)](https://www.anaheim.net/2829)
[![water outage map](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34457)](https://www.anaheim.net/3145)
[![sign up Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34456)](http://www.anaheimalert.net/)
## Why is my power off?
Power outages may occur due to a number of different causes, from regional, large-scale blackouts, to local problems on power lines serving your home or business. Some examples of power outage causes include:  

  * Non-payment
  * Cars hitting power poles
  * Mylar balloons tangled in power lines
  * Equipment issues
  * Extreme weather
  * Trees falling on overhead power lines
  * Transmission lines damaged from wildfires


## Why is my water off?
Water service may be disrupted by a variety of causes, including:  

  * Non-payment
  * Water main break
  * Service line leak
  * Equipment failure 
  * Planned shutdown for maintenance
  * Construction activities 
  * Water facilities damaged from natural disasters (earthquake, wildland fire, etc.) 
  * Water quality concerns


## When Power Goes Off
  
If one of these problems should occur, Anaheim’s local grid is designed to detect most outages and quickly restore power to as many customers as possible. Automated equipment and computer monitoring systems will inform utility operators of large outages, who will dispatch field staff to investigate the cause.  
  
Anaheim’s electric operations center is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, to dispatch a troubleshooter to respond quickly to power outages. However, if significant repairs are required after normal business hours, additional staffing for crews will require employees to report in, assemble materials, and make the necessary repairs.  
  
In the meantime, [here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/10532/Outage-Prep?bidId=) is some helpful information to ensure that you and your family are prepared.   
  
Anaheim’s staff is trained to work on high voltage electric systems to restore power in a safe manner to all customers. Please know that we are making every effort to restore your power as soon as possible.   
  
If you were less than satisfied with our response to a power outage, or if you have questions or suggestions that you would like considered by executive management, please [complete this form](http://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Share-with-the-General-Manager-48) to send a message to the Anaheim Public Utilities General Manager.  

[![Outage Alert Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37500)](https://member.everbridge.net/892807736725456/login)

#  Ways to Save 
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers more than 45 rebates and incentive programs to assist Anaheim businesses, residents, income-qualified customers and schools save water, energy and money. Whether you are making capital improvements, replacing inefficient equipment or looking for free services on ways to help you save, we have a program that’s right for you!  

[![Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44141)](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/)
### Single Family & Multi-Family Residents
[![Commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44140)](https://www.anaheim.net/2533/)

  8. Recycled Water Fill Station


#  Recycled Water Fill Station 
The Recycled Water Fill Station is a part of the Anaheim Public Utilities’ ongoing effort to provide programs and services to help conserve water.  

The Fill Station will be used by the City to fill mobile water containers with recycled water for cleaning the City’s parking structures and local streets. Potable water will be conserved by using recycled water to pressure wash the City’s parking lot structures. The Fill Station is located at a block east of the City Hall parking structure at 200 South Anaheim Blvd. 
![Filling Station](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=16788)

#  Departments 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
The City of Anaheim is a City Council/City Manager form of government.
  2. ####  [City Attorney](https://www.anaheim.net/439/City-Attorney)
View the services the City Attorney provides the City of Anaheim.
  3. ####  [Boards & Commissions](https://www.anaheim.net/176/Boards-Commissions)
The City of Anaheim has a number of boards and commissions on which citizens serve and make recommendations to the City Council in an advisory capacity. 
  4. ####  [City Clerk](https://www.anaheim.net/218/City-Clerk)
The City Clerk is responsible for elections, records, and passport services.
  5. ####  [City Council](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)
The City of Anaheim aims to continually reinvent itself to be a better place for all who live, do business in, or visit our great city.
  6. ####  [Community Services](https://www.anaheim.net/6094/Community-Services)
The goal of the Community Services Department is to enrich individuals, families, and the community through the provision of services, facilities, and programs which improve the quality of life in Anaheim.
  7. ####  [Convention, Sports & Entertainment](https://www.anaheim.net/2595/Convention-Sports-Entertainment)
The Anaheim Convention Center, Sports and Entertainment consists of entertainment facilities such as Angel Stadium of Anaheim, the Honda Center, the Anaheim Convention Center, the National Grove of Anaheim. It also includes the transportation facility known as ARTIC.
  8. ####  [Economic Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6280/Economic-Development)
Find a list of services the Economic Development Department provided.
  9. ####  [Finance](https://www.anaheim.net/479/Finance)
The Finance Department assists other departments in the city in meeting their service objectives by allocating and monitoring the city's financial resources, processing payroll and other financial transactions and procuring goods and services.
  10. ####  [Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/6096/Fire-Rescue)
Access the various fire and emergency services performed by Anaheim Fire & Rescue.
  11. ####  [Housing and Community Development](https://www.anaheim.net/6363/Housing-and-Community-Development)
The Community Development Department implements a variety of programs to foster a better quality of life. These programs create new residential opportunities, revitalize neighborhoods and create job training opportunities.
  12. ####  [Human Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/401/Human-Resources)
Human Resources partners with city departments to hire, compensate, support and create a workforce dedicated to delivering high-quality services, which makes Anaheim a special place to live, work and play.
  13. ####  [Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/490/Planning-Building)
Find information about permits, building, licenses, code enforcement, economic development, historic preservation, and planning and zoning.
  14. ####  [Police Department](https://pd.anaheim.net/)
Access information about the Police Department's programs, what initiatives it's focusing on and more information about its different divisions.
  15. ####  [Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/6099/Public-Utilities)
Find more information about the Public Utilities Department, how to receive rebates and incentives and general information about city utilities.
  16. ####  [Public Works](https://www.anaheim.net/229/Public-Works)
Public Works provides a myriad of comprehensive services to meet a variety of the city's infrastructure, construction, and development needs.

#  Residential 
Anaheim Public Utilities provides a wide array of programs and services for Anaheim residents. Select one or more of the options below for the programs that best meet your needs!  

[![Energy Rebates Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28474)](https://www.anaheim.net/936/)
[![Water Rebates Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28476)](https://www.anaheim.net/945/)
[![Income Qualified Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28475)](https://www.anaheim.net/2546/)
[![Multi Family Residence Programs Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=52223)](https://www.anaheim.net/6475)
* * *
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

    11. [Home Utility Check-Up ](https://www.anaheim.net/475/Home-Utility-Check-Up)
    12. [Medical Lifeline](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=)
    13. [MyPower Savings](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16359)
    14. [Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/Pool-Equipment)

    16. [Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program](https://www.anaheim.net/937/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program)
    17. [TreePower Program](https://www.anaheim.net/763/)
    18. [Weatherization Program](https://www.anaheim.net/946/)
  2. [Water Rebates & Programs ](https://www.anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Programs) [](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/Residential "Expand Water Rebates & Programs  Submenu")
    1. [High-Efficiency Clothes Washer](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/clothes-washers/)
    2. [Home Utility Checkup](http://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/475/Home-Utility-Checkup)
    3. [Household Flow Sensors](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/flow-monitoring-device-pilot-program/)
    4. [Leak Repair Rebate](https://www.anaheim.net/5525/)
    5. [Premium High-Efficiency Toilets](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/toilets/)
    6. [Rain Barrels & Cistern](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/rain-barrels-cisterns/)
    7. [Rotating Sprinkler Nozzles](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/sprinkler-nozzles/)
    8. [Soil Moisture Sensors](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/soil-moisture-sensors/)
    9. [TreePower Program](https://www.anaheim.net/763/)
    10. [Turf Replacement Program](https://www.anaheim.net/1062/Turf-Removal-Program)
    11. [Weather Based Irrigation Controllers](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/irrigation-controllers/)
    12. [Trees for Turf](https://www.anaheim.net/6702/Trees-for-Turf)

Latest News
  1. [![PR graphic Festival](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=69988)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
####  [Anaheim approves Anaheim Hills Festival apartment project ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
An extensively considered and highly debated proposal for apartments at Anaheim Hills Festival shopping center was approved Tuesday night by Anaheim’s City Council. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3189)
  2. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
####  [Latest: Ament requests withdraw guilty plea in court with decision pending; running timeline ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
Anaheim has condemned actions documented in a federal investigation of the city's former mayor and a former Anaheim Chamber of Commerce executive that came to light in 2022. Here is what has happened, how Anaheim has responded and ongoing updates. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2731)
  3. [![Image to go with a press release for Anaheim Contigo and information on immigration enforcement](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
####  [Anaheim Contigo: go to for timely immigration enforcement information ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
Anaheim Contigo is your source for timely information about federal immigration enforcement activity in our city. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3137)
  4. [![20250514-DSC00828](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
####  [New state law covers space near crosswalks for safer streets ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
A new California law limits how close you can park near crosswalks in Anaheim neighborhoods and across the state. The state law aims to improve visibility and make streets safer for kids, families and everyone who walks, bikes or drives. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=3086)
  5. [![center greens ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
####  [Center Greens Park debuts downtown ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
Center Greens Park debuted downtown Saturday, transforming a grassy, informal space into an iconic park with a new skate park, walking paths, butterfly garden and more for community to enjoy. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2996)
  6. [![news flash La Palma groundbreaking](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
####  [La Palma Park Way Promenade Groundbreaking ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
Steeped in history, La Palma Park promenade project starts multiyear transformation of iconic park [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2984)
  7. [![two volunteers building a rock climbing wall for new playground at little people's park ](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
####  [Iconic downtown Anaheim park gets new kid-designed, volunteer-built, donated playground ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
One of Anaheim’s most iconic parks got a new playground designed by neighborhood kids and built in a day by more than 100 volunteers from The CarMax Foundation, KABOOM!, Anaheim Family YMCA and the city of Anaheim. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2924)
  8. [![city news graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/)](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
####  [Anaheim shares state audit, supports recommendations with adoption underway ](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)
Anaheim has shared a state audit critical of contract and funding oversight involving Visit Anaheim and the Anaheim Chamber of Commerce and welcomes the report’s recommendations with adoption already underway. [Read on...](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?AID=2847)


[View All News](https://www.anaheim.net/CivicAlerts.aspx?CID=58)

#  Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting (Residential) 
## The Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting Program offers high-efficiency outdoor lighting to keep your home bright and safe.
### **Increase visibility with dusk-to-dawn and security lights.**
![20W Res Security Light w. Motion Sensor](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28841)
![D2D Greenlite Light Bulb](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=25051)
![115w PaR38](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28852)
Outdoor security light with dusk-to-dawn function and motion detection. 
LED A19 bulb with dusk to dawn features allows any light fixture to turn on at night and off in the morning. 
LED Par 38 bulb with dusk-to-dawn features allows a celling or can light fixture to turn on at night and off in the morning. 
###  **Program Criteria**  
  

  * Participants must be Anaheim Public Utilities electric customers with current and non-delinquent accounts.
  * Lights must be installed within the 30-day period. For new hardwired fixtures, it is recommended that participants obtain multiple quotes from licensed electricians and also obtain a permit from the building department for installation.
  * Lights must be maintained and used for a minimum of five years from installation.
  * Participants must provide access for inspection of the installed fixture(s).
  * Participants are eligible to receive up to two (2) free light fixtures for their residential home.


Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. This program is subject to available funding.
To view multi-family and commercial property Dusk-to-Dawn lighting options, please visit our [Commercial Dusk-to-Dawn Program webpage](https://anaheim.net/2628/Dusk-to-Dawn-Lighting).
###  [**Qualifying Income Levels**](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)  
  

Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).

#  Energy Rebates 
Make your home more energy efficient by receiving a rebate to help offset the costs of purchasing qualified ENERGY STAR® appliances and home products.   

[![Appliance & Fixtures](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28489)](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/)
[![Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35178)](http://www.anaheim.net/5730/Battery-Storage)
[![Building Products](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28490)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/)
[![Heating & Cooling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28491)](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/)
[![backup power](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45136)](https://www.anaheim.net/5243)
[![Pool Equipment](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28493)](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/)
[![Fire Safety](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28861)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/)
# Energy Programs  

Participate in one of our programs to help you live a cleaner and greener lifestyle. We can help you recycle your old, operational refrigerator, perform an in-home evaluation of your electric and water usage, and even help you install a free shade tree for your home.

#  Rebate Guidelines & Application 
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a wide variety of rebates to assist our residential customers with energy savings. Please feel free to read through our application process and program guidelines below before completing the online [application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/). If you prefer to apply using a paper application, please contact us and we would be happy to send you one.  

  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)
  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)
  3. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


  1. [Application Process](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_0)


After purchasing a qualifying conservation measure or ENERGY STAR® certified replacement appliance, please follow the steps below:
  1. Collect all appropriate supporting documents such as original, paid sales receipts or invoices, which must include the product’s brand name, the manufacturer model number, the purchase price and date, and, if available, the product’s efficiency information.
  2. Apply online by clicking the link and creating an account.
  3. Follow the instructions within the online rebate processing tool.
  4. Once you have submitted your application online, you can check its status by logging into your rebate account. Please note that applications will be denied if no return communication is received from the customer after three outreach attempts and/or within one year from the submission date.
  5. Paper applications may be requested by contacting by phone, email, or downloading online, [ENGLISH ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64054/Home-Incentives-Application_April_2025)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64053/Home-Incentives-Application-SPANISH_April_2025)
  6. An Anaheim Public Utilities representative may call to schedule a physical inspection of your installed appliance or conservation measure before issuing a rebate.
  7. We ask that you try to keep your inspection appointment to ensure that your application is processed in a timely manner. If you need to reschedule, please contact Anaheim Public Utilities at 714-408-4161.
  8. Rebate checks are normally mailed within 30 days from the date of approval.


*If you are applying for ENERGY STAR certified window rebates, you must also include a [window incentive worksheet (PDF)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/3723) (completed by your contractor) and the stickers that display the actual ultraviolet (UV) value and solar heat gain coefficient (SHGC).
  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_1)


  * **Residential Incentive** : Home Incentive Programs provide customers with rebates to help lower the cost of investing in high efficiency products and measures. The incentive offer is limited to residential customer for residential use. The appliance must be installed in a residential dwelling within Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) service area. A residential dwelling is defined a single family home, condominium, apartment, or mobile home permanently located within APU service area. The dwelling must be fully constructed and occupied (no new construction).
  * **Required documentation** : This application and a paid sales receipt for each product must be returned to the Rebate Processing Center office listed on the bottom of the application. Online purchases require submittal of the delivery slip. To expedite your application, please upload a copy of your APU bill. Window rebates require submittal of a window incentive worksheet.
  * **Limitations** : In order to qualify for the Program, Applicant must be an APU electric service customer with any and all APU accounts in good standing. The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by APU. Maximum of one application per measure type per account service address every 5 years, unless approved by APU. The incentive amount will not exceed the purchase price. The incentive check will be mailed in approximately 30 days following application approval.
  * **Installation verification** : Applicant understands and agrees that APU or its representative may inspect the installed product for installation verification before the incentive is paid.
  * **Delinquent accounts** : Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the APU’s Rates, Rules and Regulations) utility bill(s) for which the Applicant is responsible whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant understands APU may withhold payment of incentive until all of the conditions listed herein are met.
  * APU does not warrant, endorse, or assume liability for the quality, performance, or safety of the contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the applicants responsibility.
  * Applicant is responsible for meeting all program requirements and for checking with state/county/city governments and homeowner’s association (if any) in the area regarding local conditions, restrictions, codes, ordinances, rules, and regulations prior to installation. 
  * Applicant must submit application within 1 year of purchase of the product.
  * Note that insufficient or incorrect data may delay your rebate check.
  * The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-serve basis and is effective until funding is expended or the program is discontinued by Anaheim Public Utilities.


  1. [Application Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Rebate-Guidelines-Application#tab3c49f1bf-09c0-4103-a54e-7a964e9f1e1a_2)


For assistance with your application, or more information on qualifying criteria or the status of your rebate application, please call our rebate processing team at 714-408-4161, which is open Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m., excluding holidays.
### More Information
Visit the [Environmental Protection Agency's ENERGY STAR® website](http://www.energystar.gov/) for more information.
  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

#  Refrigerator Recycling Rebate Program 
## Recycle Your Refrigerator/Freezer & Receive $50
Anaheim Public Utilities customers can receive $50 for recycling their old spare refrigerator or freezer.
## How the Program Works
  1. Recycle your refrigerator or freezer (you will need to arrange an independent pickup service).
  2. Complete the [program application](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53447/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-Application-English-and-Spanish-0224) or [apply online](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program-334) to confirm program participation eligibility.
  3. You will receive your incentive check within six (6) weeks of application receipt and approval.


## Requirements
  * Must be Anaheim Public Utilities residential electric customers.
  * Only one operating unit may be recycled for incentive every five (5) years.
  * Recycled unit must be at minimum 10 cubic feet and a maximum 32 cubic feet. Mini refrigerators, such as wine coolers or dorm coolers, and commercial-sized refrigerators do not qualify.


Customers seeking information on bulky item collection services can be found [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6276/Bulky-Item-Collection). 
_*Program is subject to available funding._
![Refrigerator Recycling](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27141)

  8. Forms & Applications


#  Forms & Applications 
### **Short-Term Rental Conveyance Applications**
Anaheim Municipal Code Section 4.05.180.010.0102 (a), allows a CC&R Restricted Short-Term Rental to relocate to a new location with an approved Administrative Permit or a Minor Conditional Use Permit (MCUP). Administrative Permit applications will be processed in 10 business days and MCUP requests are generally processed in 30 to 60 days. If you are requesting a relocation, you are required to submit the items below along with the applicable fee. 
  * [Short-Term Rental Conveyance Submittal Checklist](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27339/0Short-Term-Rental-Conveyance-Submittal-Checklist)
    * [Conveyance Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27340/1Short-Term-Rental-Conveyance-Application)
    * [Local Contact and Property Management Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27334/2Local-Contact-and-Property-Management-Form)
    * [Property Details Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27335/3Property-Details-Form)
    * [Ownership Information Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27336/4Ownership-Information-Form)
    * [Neighbor Consent Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27337/5Neighbor-consent-form)


A limited number of "Qualifying Pre-Moratorium Short-Term Rental" properties may request a relocation based on compatibility issues pursuant to Anaheim Municipal Code Section 4.05.180.010.0103, please provide the following information for consideration by the Planning Director.
  * [Compatibility Conveyance Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27338/6Compatibility-Conveyance-Requirements)


### Change of Ownership
If you are requesting a change of ownership, you are required to submit a completed application along with the $498 fee. A new application must be filled out and submitted within 14 days of such a change. 
  * Change of Ownership Application and the following forms:
    * [Ownership Change Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28528/Ownership-Change-Application)
    * [TOT Contact Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/33274)
    * [Affidavit of Notice](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28530/Affidavit-of-Notice)
    * [Indemnification Agreement (Owner)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28529/Indemnification-Agreement-Owner)
    * [Indemnification Agreement (Lessee - If Applicable)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28526/Indemnification-Agreement-Lessee)
    * [Self-Certification Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28527/Self-Certification-Form)
    * [Authorized Agent Appointment form](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1083) (If owner is not managing STR)[](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1083)
    * Fee: $498 


If you have any questions regarding the application process, please contact the City of Anaheim’s Business License staff at 714-765-5194.
### Renewals
Short-Term rental permit renewals are due annually on or before July 31st. Please contact the City of Anaheim’s Business License staff at 714-765-5194.

#  Scholarship Opportunity & Optional Paid Internship 
### As a community-owned, not-for-profit utility, Anaheim Public Utilities supports the educational efforts of Anaheim's youth through scholarships.  
  

### To honor outstanding high school seniors who are interested in pursuing a degree in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics, Anaheim Public Utilities is offering an opportunity for a $5,000 scholarship to be used towards college expenses as well as an optional paid internship.  
  

Utilities are influenced by the technological advancements and discoveries coming from the STEM community (science, technology, engineering, and mathematics). Although largely operating in the background of everyday life, utilities are part of the community, providing reliable, sustainable water and power. Similarly, how would you positively impact your community through a STEM-related career?
##  [Apply Now](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/2026-Scholarship-Application-for-High-Sc-398)!  
  

## The deadline to submit an application is:
##  Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 11:59 p.m.
### Eligibility:
  * Student must submit an application and answer all 3 essay questions using correct spelling, punctuation, and grammar conventions. 
  * Student must be pursuing post-secondary education or a degree in a STEM related field.
  * Student must live in Anaheim and receive electric and/or water service from Anaheim Public Utilities. 
  * Student must be enrolled as a high school senior at the time student's application is submitted.
  * Student will be enrolled as a full time student in a two or four year accredited post-secondary institution or trade-school in the upcoming academic year.
  * Student’s parents or legal guardians cannot be employed by the City of Anaheim.
  * Optional paid six-week internship with Anaheim Public Utilities to take place during the months of June and July.

#  Administrative Permits - Application Submittal Instructions 
## Overview
Administrative permit applications are reviewed by City Departments through the [**_GoPost_**](https://anaheim.eplansoftreview.com/#/login?portal=AnaheimPlanning)[** _GoPost_**](https://anaheim.eplansoftreview.com/#/login?portal=AnaheimPlanning) to submit your online application. Application materials may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Staff will upload all plans and supporting documents for interdepartmental review of application materials. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.
## Applications Accepted
This portal accepts the following list of administrative applications:
### Administrative Permits
  * [Administrative Adjustment](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)
  * [Delay of Enforcement - Accessory Dwelling Unit](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58139/)
  * [Outdoor Dining Parking Permit](https://anaheim.net/5538/)
  * [Mural Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58156/)
  * [Recycling Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5347/)
  * [Residential Wayfinding Sign Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)
  * [Shared Parking Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)
  * [Specimen Tree Removal Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2001/)
  * [Telecommunication Antenna](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)
  * [Urban Lot Split](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/43325/)


### Zoning Plan Review
  * [Administrative Substantial Conformance](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Compact to Standard Parking Conversion](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Exterior Alterations to Mills Act Properties](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Fence and Wall Zoning Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/)
  * [Major Plan Review](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Minor Plan Review](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Parking Lot Re-Striping](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)
  * [Vehicle to Bicycle Parking Conversion](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16598/)


### Regulatory Permits
  * [Community Care Facilities](https://www.anaheim.net/5754)
  * [Entertainment Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1997/)
  * [Massage Establishment Permit Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/14663/)
  * [Sex-Oriented Business Permit Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28083/)
  * [Smoking Lounge](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1997/)
  * [Tobacco Retail Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66429/) ([Frequently Asked Questions](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/66428/))
  * [Wholesale Automotive Sales Office](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1997/)


### Outdoor & Promotional Event
  * [Banner/Balloons and Grand Opening Banner](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5352/)
  * [Carnival & Circus](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5351/)
  * [Outdoor Event (Special Event)](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5352/)
  * [Tree Lot & Pumpkin Patch](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5351/)
  * [Special Circumstance Waiver](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)


If you do not see your application type listed above, please visit our [Forms & Applications](http://anaheim.net/879/Forms-Applications) webpage to find the right online portal for your application or contact us at (714) 765-5139.
## Payment Required
Payment is required by the  [filing deadline](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/29092/) before an application is accepted. After online or in-person submittal is completed through  _**GoPost**_ , payment will be requested. Once payment is received, your application will be routed for review.
### Online Option
1. Request an email with the amount due and a link to pay online through a  [Business Solutions Specialist](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=215) assigned to your project.
### Drop Off Option - Anaheim City Hall
1. Make a check payable to “**City of Anaheim** ” with the applicable fee.
2. Include a copy of the Administrative Application Form.
3. Drop off the check and copy of the Administrative Application Form at the Planning Services Division public counter located on the first floor of City Hall (200 S. Anaheim Blvd, Anaheim, CA 92805).
## Online and In-Person Plan Submittal Requirements
All plans submitted online or in-person must adhere to the following digital requirements: 
  1. [E-Plan Submittal Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/35289/EPR-submittal-requirements)
  2. [](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/35290/Sheet-Numbering-Guidelines)[Sheet Numbering Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/35290/Sheet-Numbering-Guidelines)


## Online Submittal Tutorial Video
All plans submitted online or in-person must be uploaded to the _**GoPost**_ online portal in order for plans and supporting documents to be reviewed by all City Departments. The tutorial video below illustrates the application submittal process through the _**GoPost**_ online portal. Click the link below the video to access the **_GoPost_** online portal. 
# [**Click Here to**](https://anaheim.eplansoftreview.com/#/login?portal=AnaheimPlanning)
# [**Submit Your Online Application or**](https://anaheim.eplansoftreview.com/#/login?portal=AnaheimPlanning)
# [**Access Your GoPost Account**](https://anaheim.eplansoftreview.com/#/login?portal=AnaheimPlanning)
_***For the best experience, we recommend using an updated version of**_** _Chrome_** _**or**_** _Edge_** _**.***_

Anaheim, CA 92805

  7. Leak Repair Rebate Program


#  Leak Repair Rebate Program 
## Program Overview
This program offers rebates to customers who repair water leaks in and around their residences, including but not limited to, slab leaks, irrigation systems, and water faucet leaks. This program provides single-family customers with a water account a rebate of up to $500 for income-qualified residents, or $250 for non-income-qualified residents for the repair of water leaks, including materials and labor. 
## How It Works
To be eligible for the $500 rebate, Anaheim Public Utilities customers must be at or below the 80% Orange County median level income or customers must be a current participant in an income-qualified program with Anaheim Public Utilities. Qualifying customers will be eligible for reimbursement. All customers can request rebates for multiple leak repairs at the same single-family residence, and a customer can file subsequent applications on future water leak repairs, subject to a total program cap of $500 for income qualified residents or $250 for non-income qualified residents. 
## How To Apply
1. Complete [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/31624/Leak-Repair-Application) in full and attach the following required documents
  * Copy of your most recent Anaheim Public Utilities water bill 
  * Copies of all corresponding invoices, receipts, checks or other documents showing expenditures of leak repair 
  * Documentation of leak - e.g. spike in water bill, report after inspection of leak by APU staff, or invoice documenting leak 


2. Submit Application and supporting documents to:  
  

  

## [Qualifying Income Levels](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).

#  Grants 
Listed below are state and federal grant websites designed to assist customers in locating additional funding sources. Anaheim Public Utilities does not administer any of the grants or incentives below.
### Electric vehicle and fleet grant resources: 
  * [Fast Charge California Project](https://calevip.org/fast-charge-california-project)
  * [Communities in Charge](https://thecommunitiesincharge.org/)
  * [2025 EV Tax Credit Information](https://pluginamerica.org/learn/federal-ev-tax-credits/2024-info/)
  * [Advanced Vehicle Technology and Infrastructure - Funding Finder Tool ](https://fundingfindertool.org/)
  * [Alternative Fuel Federal Incentives](https://afdc.energy.gov/laws/fed_summary)  
[](https://afdc.energy.gov/laws/state_summary?state=ca)
  * [Alternative Fuel State Incentives](https://afdc.energy.gov/laws/state_summary?state=ca)  
[](https://cleanvehiclerebate.org/en/ev/technology/fueling/electric)
  * [California Clean Vehicle Rebate Project: Clean Vehicle Rebate](https://cleanvehiclerebate.org/en/ev/technology/fueling/electric)  
[](https://californiahvip.org/)
  * [California HVIP Incentives for Clean Trucks and Buses](https://californiahvip.org/)
  * [Driving Clean Assistance Program](https://drivingcleanca.org/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=govdelivery)


### Additional grant resources:
  * [CARB approves incentive funding plan](https://ww2.arb.ca.gov/our-work/programs/low-carbon-transportation-investments-and-air-quality-improvement-program?utm_medium=email&utm_source=govdelivery)
  * [AQMD Incentives](http://www.aqmd.gov/home/programs)
  * [California Air Resources Board funding search](https://fundingwizard.arb.ca.gov/web/)[](https://www.energy.gov/search/site?keywords=business+tax+incentives)
  * [DSIRE: Database of state incentives for renewables and efficiency](https://www.dsireusa.org/)
  * [ENERGY STAR Tax deductions for commercial buildings](https://www.energystar.gov/about/federal_tax_credits/tax_credits_commercial_buildings)
  * [ENERGY STAR Tax deductions for homeowners](https://www.energystar.gov/about/federal_tax_credits/federal_tax_credit_archives/2022_tax_credit_information)
  * [Federal grant search](https://www.grants.gov/web/grants/search-grants.html)

  6. Protective Fencing Rebate


#  Protective Fencing Rebate 
Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) is offering a rebate **up to $1000 towards the installation of protective fencing** around existing electrical equipment on private property to protect against vandalism and theft of wire.
In addition, to assist with building permit costs, APU offers **up to $200 towards obtaining a permit** for the fence installation. 
![fencing](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57371)
The approval process for new construction is a **coordinated effort between the Planning and Building Department, APU, and the customer's contractor**. 
### Program Steps:  
  

**1. Review** the [Protective Fencing Rebate Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57373/Protective-Fencing-Rebate-Program-Guidelines) (page 1) and [Utility Equipment Screening Manual (UESM)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57372/Utility-Equipment-Screening-Manual-UESM), including but not limited to Fencing – Section 3.02.
**2. Contact Electrical Engineering** at 714-765-5156 to inquire about Screening Equipment* requirements, such as necessary clearances to allow for operation and maintenance of equipment.
**3. Obtain a**[**permit for installation**](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)
  * **A site plan** for the new construction will need to be submitted by the customer's contractor, with concurrence from the property owner to the Planning and Building Department including elevation details and landscape plans showing screening methods and how the utility equipment will fit within the area.


If you have **questions about the permitting process** , please call Planning Services at 714-765-5139.  
  

**4. Install your new fencing** in accordance with the building permit. 
  * After approval and during installation, a utility inspector will check for compliance with the UESM to ensure it meets the required clearances.
  * Request a building inspection according to your permit


**5.****Complete** the [Protective Fencing Rebate Program Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57373/Protective-Fencing-Rebate-Program) **and submit** supporting documents
Supporting documents include:
    * Completed rebate application
    * Copy of Planning Department permit card showing final sign-off
    * All fencing purchase and installation receipts
    * A current utility bill for the same address as fencing (optional)


**6. Send** or **Email** the completed rebate application, permit, paid invoice, and supporting documentation to: 
Protective Fencing Rebate Program  
201 S Anaheim Blvd., Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
* * *
**Additional Resources:**
[Utilities Electric Standards and Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/512/Electric-Standards-Guidelines)[](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)
[Fence and Wall Zoning Permit Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/5557/Fence-and-Wall-Zoning-Permit)[](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-51668)
[Anaheim Municipal Code](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-51668)
*Coverage of Utilities' equipment with materials including fencing, landscaping, graffiti-resistant vinyl wrap, and protective bollards may be referred to as **screening.**
### Contact Us
  1. ### Electrical Engineering
  
714-765-5156 or Email  
  
ACH/Hearing Impaired   
TDD: 714-765-5125  
  

### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

#  Water Rebates 
Anaheim Public Utilities, in collaboration with the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California’s SoCal Water$mart Program, offers incentives and programs for indoor and outdoor water-saving devices. Replacing inefficient devices can provide water savings and a reduced water bill.
[![Efficient Clothes Washer Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29160)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/clothes-washers/)
[![Leak Repair Rebate Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38395)](https://www.anaheim.net/5525/)
[![Household Flow Sensors Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38383)](https://socalwatersmart.com/en/residential/rebates/available-rebates/flow-monitoring-device-pilot-program/)

# Water Programs  

Participate in one of our water-saving programs to help minimize your reliance on water usage inside and outside of your home. We can perform an in-home evaluation of your water and electric usage and even help with the costs associated with a landscape transformation project.  

[![Home Utility Check-Up Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28513)](https://www.anaheim.net/475/)

  6. CII and HOA Landscape Design Assistance Program (Pilot)


#  CII and HOA Landscape Design Assistance Program (Pilot) 
Anaheim Public Utilities is now offering a pilot program to assist Commercial Industrial and Institutional (CII) and Home Owners Associations (HOA) customers with the development of representational concept planting designs for their specific landscapes for areas where turf will be removed and replaced with low water using plants.
Customers will receive a free landscape design consultation and a representative landscape planting design concept from our designated consultant.
The consultant will meet with you for one hour and assist with your design needs before you begin your turf removal project.
For more details please call 714-765-4218 or submit an [application ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/59075/Landscape-Design-Assistance-Program---Application-and-Questionnaire)to savewater@anaheim.net
[![Zero Turf Landscape Design Templates](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=55661)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/59076/Landscape-Design-Templates)
* * *
# State Rules on Non-Functional Turf in Commercial and HOA Landscapes
In October 2023, the California State Legislature passed [Assembly Bill 1572](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id=202320240AB1572), which phases in a ban on decorative lawn grass watering in commercial, industrial, and institutional areas permanently. This includes government sites and common areas of Home-Owner’s Associations (HOAs).
The new law prohibits public agencies, restaurants, corporate campuses, industrial parks, HOAs and certain other property owners from watering “non-functional turf” using potable water. 
AB 1572 gradually phases out the use of potable water for irrigation of nonfunctional turf. The law’s effective dates are as follows:
  * **January 1, 2027** - Properties owned by the California Department of General Services and properties owned by local government agencies
  * **January 1, 2028** – Commercial, industrial, and institutional properties
  * **January 1, 2029** – Common areas of homeowners’ associations, common interest developments, and community service organizations or similar entities
  * **January 1, 2031** – Local government facilities in disadvantaged communities (contingent upon availability of State financial assistance)


The law does not impose restrictions on residential yards, cemeteries, parks, golf courses, and sports fields, and permits the use of potable water to the extent it is necessary to ensure the health of trees or other perennial non-turf plants.
The State Water Board is directed to create a form for compliance self-certification, and require owners of commercial, industrial, or institutional irrigated parcels larger than 5,000 square feet to self-certify compliance to the State Water Board beginning in 2030.
If you are unsure if your turf grass qualifies as ‘nonfunctional’ feel free to contact us at 714-765-4218.
* * *

#  Forms & Applications 
## Discretionary Permits

(Including Conditional Use Permit, Minor Conditional Use Permit, Variance, Tentative Map, Reclassification, General Plan Amendment, Density Bonus, Code Amendment, Discretionary Tree Specimen Removal and Specific Plan Amendment)

[![Development Applications Submittal Instructions Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=35131)](https://www.anaheim.net/5755/Discretionary-Permits-Online-Submittal)
To submit online, visit the [Submittal Instructions website](https://www.anaheim.net/5755/). Discretionary permit applications may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.

Administrative permit applications may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.

To submit online, visit the [Submittal Instructions website](https://www.anaheim.net/5756/). Zoning Plan Review applications may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.

Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.
## Outdoor, Promotional, and Special Event Permits
  * [Banner/Balloons and Grand Opening Banner](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5352/Special-Event-Permit)
  * [Carnival & Circus](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5351/)
  * [Outdoor Event](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5352/Special-Event-Permit?bidId=)
  * [Special Circumstances Waiver](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1992/)
  * [Tree Lot & Pumpkin Patch](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5351/Carnival-Application?bidId=)


[![Outdoor and Promotional Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=35371)](https://www.anaheim.net/5756/Administrative-Permits-Online-Submittal)
To submit online, visit the [Submittal Instructions website](https://www.anaheim.net/5756/). Outdoor & Promotional Event permit applications may also be submitted in-person by appointment at the Planning Services Division Counter at City Hall. Digital format of application materials are required for in-person submittal. Contact Eibet Olmedo at EOlmedo@anaheim.net or 714-765-5139 for questions or to schedule a submittal appointment.

#  Employment Assistance 
## ​Workforce Development Board
  
The City of Anaheim, in collaboration with the [Anaheim Workforce Development Board](https://www.anaheim.net/199/Workforce-Investment-Board) offers free business services to all businesses with an emphasis on Anaheim companies. Services are customized to the needs of the business. These economic incentives include:  

  * Recruitment services for employment vacancies
  * On-the-job training and youth development programs
  * A liaison with City of Anaheim departments
  * Research assistance on topics such as labor market information, wages, and job classifications
  * Assistance during downsizing/company closures.


## Workforce Development Division
  
[Anaheim Workforce Connection](http://anaheimjobs.com/) is our one stop employment center that offers a full array of business and job seeker resources.   
  

## Businesses
  
Anaheim Workforce Connection offers a full array of business amenities such as:  

  * Access to Labor Market Information
  * Job Hire Incentives thru our On-The-Job-Training Program
  * Online Job Postings at Anaheim Workforce Connection
  * Onsite Employee Recruitment Services
  * Outplacement Assistance and Layoff Aversion


Job Seekers  
Anaheim Workforce Connection is here to help you get on the right track to employment. The services available are numerous, here’s an overview to get you started:  

  * Free Vocational Training and Educational Providers
  * Job Listings and Job Fair Information
  * One-Stop Resource Center
  * Resume Assistance and Workshops

  5. Rapid Business Assistance


#  Rapid Business Assistance 
Anaheim businesses facing the difficult prospect of downsizing, closing or relocating can get timely, urgent help from our Rapid Economic Development Team. 
Our RED Team can assist with a group of specialists from across the city of Anaheim who are trained to provide emergency business support.
Our RED Team incudes our Economic Development Department, Workforce Connection, Anaheim's employment services group, Anaheim Public Utilities, Planning & Building and other key departments committed to helping businesses thrive in Anaheim.
We'll meet with business owners and key executives on ways to stabilize existing operations and identify services available to address business challenges.
To work with our RED Team, call Business Development Manager Shant Boodanian at (714) 765-4285 or email him at sboodanian@anaheim.net.
![Canyon aerial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=56271)

#  Home Appliance & Fixtures 
  1. [Air Purifier](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_0)
  2. [Ceiling Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_1)
  3. [Dishwasher](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_2)
  4. [Dusk-to-Dawn Security Light](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_3)
  5. [Heat Pump Dryer](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_4)
  6. [Heat Pump Water heater](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_5)
  7. [Refrigerator](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_6)


  1. [Air Purifier](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_0)


Room air purifiers are portable electric appliances that remove fine particles, such as dust and pollen, from indoor air. ENERGY STAR® certified room air purifiers are 40% more energy efficient than standard models, saving customers about $30 annually on energy costs. Using ENERGY STAR portable purifiers is a great way to improve indoor air quality in a single room or area while using energy efficiently.
### Incentive Amount
### $30 (Maximum of 2)
### $50 (Maximum of 2) **with UV light**
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/air_cleaners) Certified
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/air_cleaners) Certified **with UV light**


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-848939714](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27169)
  

  1. [Ceiling Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_1)


ENERGY STAR® certified ceiling fans are 40% more efficient than conventional fan/light units. These high-quality, innovative fans use improved motors and blade design, and the latest in energy efficient technology.
### Incentive Amount
### $20 (Maximum of 3)
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ceiling_fans)[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ceiling_fans) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1059752578](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27474)
  

  1. [Dishwasher](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_2)


A standard-sized ENERGY STAR® certified dishwasher costs about $35 per year to run and saves an average of 3,870 gallons of water over the lifetime of the device. 
### Incentive Amount 
### $50
###   

### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/dishwashers) Certified


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![istockphoto-1077858850-1024x1024](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27480)
  

  

  1. [Dusk-to-Dawn Security Light](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_3)


Keep your home bright and safe with high efficiency outdoor lighting that detects natural light and turns off during the day and on at night. Customers can now purchase a new LED fixture and apply for a rebate to offset the cost.
### Incentive Amount
### $20 (Maximum of 2)
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * ### LED fixture only


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
###    
  
![20W Res Security Light w. Motion Sensor](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28985)  
  
  
  
  

  1. [Heat Pump Dryer](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_4)


Heat pump dryers take in ambient air, heat it, and recirculate it in the dryer to maintain the temperature without using much energy. This dryer passes humid air in the dryer drum through a condenser to remove the moisture without losing too much heat. Heat Pump dryers can save 20-60 percent more than conventional clothes dryers. 
### Incentive Amount
### $200
###    
Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-clothes-dryers/results) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1092362272](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27018)
###   

  1. [Heat Pump Water heater](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_5)


ENERGY STAR® certified electric storage water heaters use a highly efficient heat pump to transfer heat from the surrounding air to the water. Heat pump water heaters save a family of four approximately $330 per year on their electric bill. While a certified heat pump water heater costs more upfront, the savings will pay back the difference in approximately two years.
### Incentive Amount
### $400  
  

### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-water-heaters/results) Certified 


### [Guidelines and how to apply](https://anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)
  

![iStock-1023034238](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27520)
  

  

  1. [Refrigerator](https://www.anaheim.net/5241/Appliance-Fixtures#tabcda3968d-a837-4d81-8944-5e6aa37e2737_6)


Recent improvements in insulation and compressors have made today’s refrigerators use much less energy than older models. ENERGY STAR® certified refrigerators are about 9% more energy efficient than models that meet the federal minimum standard.
## Incentive Amount
### $50
For standard rebate: Apply Online OR Download Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50765/Home-Incentives-App-872023) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50770/Home-Incentives-App-SPANISH-872023)
### $200
For income-qualified customers submit the following: 
-[ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50765/Home-Incentives-App-872023) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50770/Home-Incentives-App-SPANISH-872023)  
AND  
- Income-Qualified Rebate Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50763/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_2023)[ ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50763/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_2023)or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50764/csp_res_home_incentives_refrigeratorIQ_SPANISH)  
**-**[**Qualifying Income Levels**](https://www.anaheim.net/6423/)
### Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * ### Replacement of an existing refrigerator
  * ###  [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-residential-refrigerators/results) Certified
  * ### Must be greater than 7.75 cubic feet


###  [Guidelines and how to apply](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic)  
  

### Receive an additional $50 when you recycle your Refrigerator/Freezer[](http://www.anaheim.net/937/Refrigerator-Recycling-Program)[**  
**](http://www.anaheim.net/937/Refrigerator-Recycling-Program)
  * [**For more information**](https://www.anaheim.net/937/Refrigerator-Recycling-Rebate-Program)

#  Home Utility Check-Up 
### For any questions or to learn more about the program, please call 714-765-4274.
* * *
Want to know how you can lower your utility bill? 
By participating in our **FREE Home Utility Check-Up** , our contracted staff will conduct a personalized analysis of your home energy and water use, and help you determine what actions you can take to be more efficient in your utility use.
**Please note that this is a FREE program and participation is always initiated by you, the customer.**
Staff will never actively visit your home or call you to solicit your participation, nor will they ever try to sell you additional services. 
If you have questions about someone offering a service claiming to be working in collaboration with Anaheim Public Utilities, please call 714-765-3300 to verify.
Program participation is limited to once every 5 years. Subject to available funding.
![LED](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14810)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  7. A/C Tune Up


#  A/C Tune-Up 
The Anaheim Public Utilities A/C Tune-Up Rebate Program provides residential customers a rebate of up to a $100 ($150 for income-qualified customers) for getting an A/C tune-up. Customers are only eligible to participate in this Program once every twelve months and applications must be submitted within 1 year of A/C tune-up service.
### How the Program Works
1. Schedule an A/C tune-up with a licensed HVAC contractor  
2. Apply [online](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/) OR Mail in the following documents:
  * A/C Tune-Up Rebate Application: [ENGLISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56308/AC-Tune-Up-Application-8_6_24_FILLABLE_ENGLISH) or [SPANISH](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56309/AC-Tune-Up-Application-8_6_24_FILLABLE_SPANISH)
  * Invoice that details the job performed, amount paid, and serviced date
  * Mail documents to:


Anaheim Public Utilities  
Attn: A/C Tune-Up  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd., Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
Rebates are limited and subject to available funding. Incentive amount not to exceed purchase price.
  

### Qualifying Income Levels
[*Low-Income Limits as defined by the California Department of Housing and Community Development (adjusted annually).](https://www.anaheim.net/6423)
### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.

#  Ways to Save 
Anaheim Public Utilities currently offers more than 45 rebates and incentive programs to assist Anaheim businesses, residents, income-qualified customers and schools save water, energy and money. Whether you are making capital improvements, replacing inefficient equipment or looking for free services on ways to help you save, we have a program that’s right for you!  

[![Residential](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44141)](https://www.anaheim.net/2532/)
### Single Family & Multi-Family Residents
[![Commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44140)](https://www.anaheim.net/2533/)
### Businesses, School/Educational Facilities, Homeowners Associations (HOA), Multi-Family Common Areas
