#  Heating & Cooling Systems 
  1. [Air Duct Repair](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_0)
  2. [Box Fan, Window Fan, Floor Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_1)
  3. [Central Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_2)
  4. [Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_3)
  5. [Heat Pump HVAC](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_4)
  6. [Room Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_5)
  7. [Smart Thermostat](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_6)
  8. [Whole House Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_7)


  1. [Air Duct Repair](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_0)


In a typical home, about 20% of the air is lost due to leaks, holes, and an improperly connected duct system, which results in an increase on your energy bill. A well-designed and properly sealed air duct system can make your home more comfortable and energy efficient.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
Must already have a central electric heating or cooling system 
## Incentive amount 
50% of repair cost (Up to $300)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Air Duct](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27528)
##   

###   

  1. [Box Fan, Window Fan, Floor Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_1)


Any of these fan options provide cooling relief during hot and muggy weather and save on energy costs by reducing the need for turning on your central A/C.  
Additionally, these fans provide cooling in occupied rooms rather than areas not in use.
## Efficiency Criteria
Must be ETL Listed
## Incentive Amount
$20 (maximum 2)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

  

![Box Fan.2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31204)
![Window fan.1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31206)
![Oscillating fan.1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31205)
  

  

  1. [Central Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_2)


ENERGY STAR® certified central air conditioners have a higher seasonal energy efficiency ratio (SEER) and use 8% less energy than conventional new models. Replacing air conditioners that are more than 10 years old with ENERGY STAR certified units can save up to 20% on heating and cooling costs. 
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Minimum 15+ SEER
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](http://energystar.gov) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


  

## Incentive Amount 
$200 per ton
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Heating & Cooling System](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27532)
  

  

  1. [Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_3)


A mini-split heat pump provides a highly efficient heating and cooling system, where ductwork does not exist. Mini-splits help keep each room or spaces a customized temperature via remote access.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * Minimum 15+ SEER rating
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/ductless_heating_cooling) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


## Incentive Amount 
$200 per Ton
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Heat Pump Ductless Mini Split](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27552)
  

  

  

  

  

  

  1. [Heat Pump HVAC](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_4)


A high-efficiency heat pump transfers central air conditioning as well as heating and uses less energy than traditional electric systems. Heat pumps transfer heat in both directions, using the outside air to heat a home in winter and cool it in summer.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Minimum 15+ SEER rating
  * Minimum 12.5 EER rating
  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/?s=mega) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system


## Incentive Amount
$200 per Ton
## Additional Rebate Information
South Coast AQMD offers an additional Heat Pump HVAC rebate that is separate from APU's rebate. Please visit South AQMD link below for additional information and questions:
[www.aqmd.gov/gozero](http://www.aqmd.gov/gozero)
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![AC](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27529)
  

  1. [Room Air Conditioner](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_5)


Room air conditioners cool rooms rather than the entire home and can provide cooling only where needed. ENERGY STAR certified room air conditioners can save more energy and money through improved sealing and insulation around the unit to keep you cool and comfortable.   

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-room-air-conditioners/results) Certified
  * Must be a replacement heating/cooling system 


## Incentive Amount
$100 (Maximum of 2)   

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Window AC](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27538)  

  

  1. [Smart Thermostat](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_6)


Unlike programmable or manual thermostats, ENERGY STAR certified smart thermostats can deliver greater convenience, control, and can help you save money on your energy bill. Smart thermostats utilize motion sensors, which recognize when you have left the home and have the potential to learn you daily schedule, making energy-saving temperature changes automatically.
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
[ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/smart_thermostats)
  * Wi-Fi Enabled 
  * Must be a replacement of an existing thermostat 


## Incentive Amount 
$50 (Maximum of 2) 
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Smart Thermotat](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27534)
  

  1. [Whole House Fan](https://www.anaheim.net/2579/Heating-Cooling-Systems#tab687bcb24-e30b-4c58-9187-7a0e8b25a7f7_7)


A whole house fan is an efficient way to reduce the use of your air conditioner and maintain a comfortable temperature within your home. A whole house fan draws in cool outside air from open windows and releases excess hot air inside the home through your attic.  

##    
​Installation/Efficiency Criteria  

Permanently mounted in attic  
Central air conditioning existing  

  

## ​Incentive Amount  

$100  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Whole House](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27537)  
  

### Additional efficiency rebates may be available through [SoCalGas](https://www.socalgas.com/save-money-and-energy/rebates-and-incentives/natural-gas-appliance-rebates)

#  Private Use EV Charger Rebate 
## Receive up to $300 or $600
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities offers a rebate to customers who **install a Level 2 plug-in Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger at their home or business**. 
1. Up to $300 for a Level 2 EV charger 
2. Up to $600 for a networked charger and the customer must sign up for a time-of-use rate (TOU)
See the [TOU Rates](https://www.anaheim.net/6335/Residential-Rates) page for rates.  
  
Eligible expenses for reimbursement include:
    * Cost of the charger
    * Cost of installation (Material receipts are not eligible for reimbursement) 
    * Upgrades needed to accommodate the charger 
    * City permit fees for the EV charger


[![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)
  

  1. [Program Steps](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_0)


## Plug-In Electric Vehicle Charger Rebate Program Steps
  1. Obtain an Anaheim building permit for the installation of your new EV charger. For questions about the permitting process, call**Anaheim Building Services at 714-765-5153**


    * New residential developments may be 'EV Ready' or 'EV Capable,' meaning the space meets building code requirements for the future installation of electric vehicle supply equipment.
    * A resident can move into a new development that is 'EV Ready,' but this doesn't necessarily mean the parking space comes with the actual EV charging equipment. 
    * When the resident installs the level 2 EV charging equipment, they will need to attain a permit for the hardware. 
    * To qualify for the EV rebate for level 2 chargers, residents must attain a permit for the EV charging station. Anaheim Municipal Code Section 15.05.040 requires each EV charging station to be issued a permit.


2. Install your EV charger in accordance with building codes.
3. Complete the [online application](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/).
For questions, call 714-765-4250 or email evrebates@anaheim.net. 
**Required documents include:**
  * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
  * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
  * Copy of your Anaheim Public Utilities bill
  * Photo of the installed EV charging station
  * Photo of the serial number on your charger


  1. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/593/Private-EV-Charger-Rebate#tab1c264eb5-a871-4db3-bca5-8376bff65d25_1)


  * Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant, endorse or assume liability for the quality, performance or safety of the Customer selected contractor and/or retailer or wholesaler. Performance of any product and acceptance of materials used is solely the customer’s responsibility.
  * Applicant agrees that any incentive may be credited towards any delinquent (as currently defined in the City of Anaheim _Rates, Rules and Regulations_) utility bill(s) for which the customer is responsible, whether the account(s) is open or closed.
  * Applicant is responsible for obtaining all required permits from the City of Anaheim.
  * Applicant must receive electric service from Anaheim Public Utilities.
  * Applicant must submit all required documents listed..
  * Applicant understands and agrees that Anaheim Public Utilities may inspect the installed EV charger before the incentive is paid.
  * Maximum of 1 EV charger rebate per customer.


Prior to installation, the applicant is responsible for meeting all Program requirements and for checking with state, county, or city governments and homeowner’s associations (if any) regarding: Codes,Conditions, Ordinances, Restrictions, Rules and regulations
  * The EV charger must be installed within Anaheim Public Utilities service area.


* * *

#  Motor Incentive Program 
### Upgrading your motor system can save more money than you think.
  

Anaheim Business Energy Efficiency Solutions Program offers incentives to their customers for energy efficient upgrades for replacing existing and operating motors. One way to reduce your energy costs is to switch your standard inefficient motor with a new premium energy efficient motor.
Measure | Efficiency range | Hp Range | Incentives | Maximum Incentive  
---|---|---|---|---  
Motor/Pump | 86.5% -96.0% | 5-Hp - 200-Hp | $60 - $1,400 per unit | 25% of project cost or $50,000 whichever is less  
If you are ready to implement your motor/pump replacement project, please download the following information and contact us about your project. (All projects are subject to funding availability)
  

  

###  **Program Steps**  
  

  * Download the Program Guidelines and [Motor Incentive Table](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/26605/ABEES-Motor-Incentives-Table-FY-20-21) documents.
  * Download the [Motor Incentive Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/26606/Motors-Application-FY-20-21): (re-name file using account address and name)
  * Download and complete all required tax forms (including W-9 & 590)
  * If you have any questions or concerns about the NEMA Motor Incentive Program, call your energy representative at Anaheim Public Utilities, or fill out this[online form](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Energy-Efficiency-Solutions-61). A representative will contact you within 2-3 business days.


### [Require Forms](https://www.anaheim.net/QuickLinks.aspx?CID=412)

  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/6183)

  7. EV Fleet Charger & Infrastructure Rebate 


#  EV Fleet Charger & Infrastructure Rebate 
This program provides rebates to **commercial customers and schools to electrify their vehicle fleet**.   
  
Business fleet customers receive up to **$5,000 per charging station** and associated infrastructure costs.   
  
Schools receive up to **$10,000 per EV charger** and associated infrastructure costs.
![AESD bus charging](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=36749)
All program participants with an associated **sub-meter installation may receive up to a $5,000 rebate**. In addition, Anaheim will reimburse city plan check fees up to $1,500 and electric service connection fees up to $2,000.  
  
_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
### Program Steps:  
  

1.**Review** the Electric Vehicle Fleet Charger and Infrastructure Rebate Pilot [**Program Reservation Form**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36752/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate-Reservation-Form)**and**[**Program Agreement Terms and Conditions**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36751/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate-Program-Agreement) (pages 3-4).
2. **Complete the****Reservation Form** and email it to the program manager at EVRebates@anaheim.net. 
_Customers awarded a rebate will be emailed an approval letter and provided with a Rebate Reservation Number._
3.**Contact Electrical Engineering** at 714-765-5156 to inquire about EV charger installation requirements,  _after_ receiving the approval letter.  
  
4. **Obtain a****permit for installation** of your new charger(s) at:
200 South Anaheim Boulevard,   
First Floor, Suite 145, Counter 1  
Anaheim, CA 92805
If you have [questions about the permitting process](https://www.anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division), please call Building Services at 714-765-5153. 
5.**Install your new EV charging station** in accordance with the Electric Vehicle Fleet Charger and Infrastructure Rebate Pilot Program Agreement within one (1) year of receiving your Rebate Reservation Number. 
6. **Complete the Agreement Form****and submit supporting documents.** Please carefully review the [Electric Vehicle Fleet Charger and Infrastructure Rebate Pilot Program Agreement](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36751/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate-Program-Agreement) before completing and submitting the form with all supporting documents.
Supporting documents include:
    * EV Rebate Reservation Number
    * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
    * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
    * W-9 Form filled out by the customer receiving the rebate
    * Current utility bill for the same address as the new sub-meter
    * Documentation or proof of fleet vehicles for networked EV charging stations 


Submit documents via email (EVRebates@anaheim.net) or mail:
EV Fleet Charger & Infrastructure Rebate Pilot Program  
201 South Anaheim Boulevard, Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
7. To assign the rebate to a third party, complete the [Electric Vehicle Fleet Charger & Infrastructure Rebate Assignment Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36750/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate-Assignment-Form).
### Contact Us
  1. ## Commercial Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)[](https://www.anaheim.net/1069/)

#  Public Access EV Charger Rebates 
  * ### Receive Up to $3,500 Per Charger for Public Access Locations
  * ### Receive Up to $7,500 Per Charger for Schools, Affordable Housing, and Publicly Accessible DC Fast Plug-in locations 


_Rebates are subject to fund availability._
Anaheim Public Utilities is oﬀering Public Access Electric Vehicle Charging Station rebates to commercial, industrial, and municipal customers who**install****Level 2** (240-Volt)**or higher plug-in electric vehicle (EV) chargers** at locations **accessible to patrons, multi-family dwelling residents, commuters, and visitor****s**.   
  
Under this program, customers will be reimbursed for out-of-pocket expenses**up to $3,500** per charging station for public access locations, or **up to $7,500** for school, aﬀordable housing, or publicly accessible DC fast plug-in locations (maximum of 10 charging stations).
  

  

Anaheim Public Utilities is offering Public Access Electric Vehicle Charging Station Rebates to Commercial, Industrial, and Municipal customers who install a Level 2 (240-Volt) or higher plug-in electric vehicle (EV) chargers at locations accessible to patrons, multi-family dwelling residents, commuters and visitors. Under this program, Anaheim Public Utilities will reimburse customers for out-of-pocket expenses up to $5,000 per charging station for public access locations, or $10,000 for school, affordable housing, or publicly accessible DC fast plug-in locations (maximum of 4 charging stations). Eligible expenses include the charger purchase price and installation costs. In addition to the rebate, Anaheim will pay the City’s permit application fee for the EV charger.
  

Find out more by calling 714-765-4250 or email us.
Eligible expenses include:
  * Cost of the charger
  * Cost of installation


Program participants who choose to install an associated sub-meter may receive up to $3,500. In addition, Anaheim Public Utilities will reimburse city plan check fees up to $1,500 for such participants.
![EV Chargers](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19868)
### Program Steps:  
  

1.**Review** the Public Access EV Charging Station [**Rebate Reservation Form**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/18907)**and**[**Program Agreement Terms and Conditions**](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/18908/Public-Access-Electric-Vehicle-Charging-Station-Rebate-Program-Agreement-FILLABLE-FORM?bidId=) (pages 3-4).
2. **Complete the****Reservation Form** and email it to the program manager at EVRebates@anaheim.net. 
_Customers awarded a rebate will be emailed an approval letter and provided with a Rebate Reservation Number._
3.**Contact Electrical Engineering** at 714-765-5156 to inquire about EV charger installation requirements,  _after_ receiving the approval letter.  
  
4. **Obtain a****permit for installation** of your new charger(s) at:
200 South Anaheim Boulevard,   
First Floor, Suite 145, Counter 1  
Anaheim, CA 92805
If you have [questions about the permitting process](https://www.anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division), please call Building Services at 714-765-5153. 
5.**Install your new EV charging station** in accordance with the Public Access Electric Vehicle Charging Station Rebate Program Agreement within one (1) year of receiving your Rebate Reservation Number. 
6. **Complete the Agreement Form****and submit supporting documents.** Please carefully review the [Public Access Electric Vehicle Charging Station Rebate Program Agreement](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/18908) before completing and submitting the form with all supporting documents.
Supporting documents include:
    * EV Rebate Reservation Number
    * All charging station purchase and installation receipts
    * Copy of Building Department Permit Card showing final sign-off
    * W-9 Form filled out by the customer receiving the rebate
    * Current utility bill for the same address as the new sub-meter (if applicable)


Submit documents via email (EVRebates@anaheim.net) or mail:
Public Access EV Charging Station Rebate Program   
201 South Anaheim Boulevard, Suite 801  
Anaheim, CA 92805
7. To assign the rebate to a third party, complete the [Public Access EV Charger Rebate Assignment Form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/14112/Public-Access-EV-Charger-Rebate-Assignment-Form). 
### Contact Us
  1. ## Commercial Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email

#  Bill Assistance 
We understand that some of our customers may experience financial setbacks or hardships.
We offer several options to assist with bill payment and help get you back on track. We also provide the necessary documentation you may need to seek third-party assistance.  
  
Give us a call at 714-765-3300 and we'll walk you through the best options for your situation. 
  1. [Bill Deferrals](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_0)


We understand that some of our customers may experience financial setbacks or hardships. For those who have fallen behind, we offer payment extensions and will work with you to get you back on track.
## Extensions & Payment Plans
A customer may request a 10 calendar day extension if they are not able to pay by the bill due date.
For past due bills requiring a longer period of time to pay, a customer alternatively may request a payment plan. See [Deferred Payment Assistance](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2045/Deferred-Payment-Assistance-PDF?bidId=) for more information about each option.
## Monthly Billing
Monthly Billing is a monthly bill for bi-monthly billed customers. See [Monthly Billing](https://www.anaheim.net/formcenter/public-utilities-6/monthly-billing-enrollment-form-130) to read more about the billing option and submit a form for enrollment.
## Level Pay
Level Pay is a monthly bill plan where a customer may make an average payment based on their last 12 months of previous usage. See [Level Pay](https://anaheim.net/6537/Level-Pay-Plan) for more information.
  1. [EMERGENCY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_1)


The Anaheim Public Utilities Emergency Assistance Program provides financial assistance to qualified residential customers facing temporary financial hardship. Eligible customers may receive a credit of **$250 toward electric service and $100 for water service,** once every three (3) years as emergency payment assistance.
  * [Emergency Assistance Application](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Emergency-Assistance-Application-373)


  1. [INCOME-QUALIFIED PROGRAMS](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_2)


Customers who meet income-qualified guidelines may be eligible to participate in a number of income-qualified programs. 
Programs include senior, military veteran, and long-term disabled discounts, community-produced solar bill credit, as well as home efficiency programs. See [Income-Qualified Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/2546/Income-Qualified-Programs) for more information.
  * [Income-Qualified Programs Application (English)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25981/Income-Qualified-Energy--Water-Discount-Application--?bidId=)
  * [Income-Qualified Programs Application (Spanish)](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25982/Income-Qualified-Energy--Water-Discount-Application-Spanish?bidId=)


  1. [FEDERAL LOW INCOME ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_3)


### Electric Bill Assistance Program
Anaheim customers can receive utility payment assistance on their **electric bill** through the federally-funded **Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)** administered through the Community Action Partnership of Orange County (CAPOC).   
  
Funding is based on monthly income. Customers may view income qualifications and apply online or call CAPOC at (714) 839-6199 for more information. Once approved, a bill credit will be **directly applied to your account**.
  * [Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)](https://www.caliheapapply.com/)


  1. [MEDICAL ALLOWANCES](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_4)


If you or someone who lives in your home uses **life-support equipment** , you may be eligible to pay a lower rate for some of your electricity. View the application below for details and a list of qualifying medical equipment. 
  * [Medical Lifeline Allowance Application](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369)


  1. [THIRD PARTY ASSISTANCE](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_5)


The following third-party organizations provide assistance with paying your Anaheim Public Utilities electric bill.* These organizations are not affiliated with the City of Anaheim but have been essential partners in assisting those in need.
  * [Anaheim Community Foundation (ACF)](https://anaheimcf.org/anaheim-emergency-assistance/) - offers support to help cover rent, utility bills, and household essentials through Anaheim Family Resource Centers.  
  

  * [Community Action Partnership (CAP)](https://capoc.org/)  
11870 Monarch Street, Garden Grove, CA 92841  
714-839-6199 or 800-660-4232  
  

  * [Share Our Selves (SOS)](https://www.shareourselves.org/) - offers health services, bill assistance, and other programs in Orange County.  
1550 Superior Ave., Costa Mesa, CA 92627  
949-642-3451   
  

  * [The Salvation Army](http://www.salvationarmyusa.org/) - provides various assistance programs including bill assistance, job training, and food pantries.  
1515 W. North St. Anaheim, CA 92801  
714-783-2344  
  

  * [2-1-1 Orange County](https://www.211oc.org/) - an information and referral service that connects people to programs for housing, job placement, food, and health insurance services.  
949-646-4357 (local)


*All assistance is subject to meeting each agency's eligibility requirements and is reliant upon available funding.
  1. [Third Party Water Bill](https://www.anaheim.net/6524/Bill-Assistance#taba48ad0e0-b279-4812-8596-7b070949272b_6)


Customers who meet program guidelines may be eligible for Anaheim Public Utilities’ [Income-Qualified Water Rebate Program](https://anaheim.net/6627/Income-Qualified-Third-Party-Water-Bill-). The program provides**u****p to a $120 rebate per year** to qualifying customers who receive a **third-party water bill**.
  * [Income Qualified Water Rebate Application (English)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58586/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application-as-of-010925_)
  * [Income Qualified Water Rebate Application (Spanish)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58640/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application_Spanish)

#  Lighting Incentives Program 
## Lighting upgrades can deliver larger savings than you think. 
  
Anaheim Business Energy Efficiency Solutions (ABEES) Program offers incentives to help customers upgrade to energy-efficient LED lighting and lighting control systems. With rapid advancements in LED technology, there are now a wide variety of options to replace your existing lighting with more efficient, cost-saving solutions.
Anaheim customers can take advantage of Performance-Based energy efficiency projects, where incentives are calculated based on measurable energy savings in kilowatts (kW) or kilowatt-hours (kWh). This ensures that your investment in energy-efficient lighting not only reduces costs but also delivers tangible, measurable results.
### **_Performance Based Incentives_**
**Category** | **Measure Description** | **$ / kW Saved** | **$ / kWh annual Saved** | **LED Requirements** | **Maximum Incentive**  
---|---|---|---|---|---  
**Greater of the two** | **To qualify, measures must meet at least two (2) of the following requirements:  
** | **Performance**  
High Efficiency Lighting & Lighting Controls   
| 
  * New LED Fixture
  * LED Retrofit Kits
  * LED Bulbs
  * Lighting Controls

| $400 | $0.15 | 
  1. UL Listed
  2. ETL listed
  3. DLC listed
  4. Energy Star Certified
  5. 25,000 hours or 3 years warranty

| Limited **incentive amount** , up to **$75,000** , per customer per year  
  

  

  

  

If you are ready to implement a lighting retrofit project, please download the information below. **(All projects are subject to funding availability).**
### **Program Steps**
  * Download the Program Guidelines and [Lighting Incentive Table](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/27035/ABEES-Lighting-Incentives-Table) documents.
  * Download the [Lighting Incentive Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/26602/Lighting-Application) and all required forms (including the W-9).
  * Questions about the program details? Simply fill out our [online form](http://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Energy-Efficiency-Solutions-61). A representative will contact you within 2-3 business days.


### [Required Forms](https://www.anaheim.net/QuickLinks.aspx?CID=412)

  2. [Program Guidelines](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/6183)

#  Insulation & Building Projects 
  1. [Attic Fan (Solar/Standard)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_0)
  2. [Exterior Wall Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_1)
  3. [High Performance Windows](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_2)
  4. [Window Film](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_3)
  5. [Fire Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_4)
  6. [Fire Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_5)


  1. [Attic Fan (Solar/Standard)](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_0)


Solar or standard attic fans run on sun power and pull superheated air out of your attic to be replaced by cooler outside air. This reduces the amount of work your HVAC system has to do to cool your upstairs living area, which is especially useful if you have an upstairs bonus room or bedroom that gets uncomfortably hot in summer. 
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Solar: permanently mounted on the roof or in the attic
  * Standard: permanently mounted on a roof or in the attic with a smart thermostat


##    
Incentive Amount
Both: $30 
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Attic Fan ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28990)
  

  

  1. [Exterior Wall Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_1)


Exterior wall insulation reduces the transfer of cold or warm air by filling the space between your walls with a layer of fiber material. Many older homes have little to no exterior wall insulation, resulting in unnecessarily high heating and cooling energy costs. Retrofitting exterior wall insulation can yield significant energy savings as well as enhanced comfort.   

##    
​Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Adding a minimum of R-13+

  

## ​Incentive Amount   

$0.10 per sq. ft.  
  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![iStock-155447087](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27523)  

  1. [High Performance Windows](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_2)


Heat gain and loss through windows account for up to 50% of a home’s heating and cooling needs. Many technological improvements have been made in recent years that have advanced the insulating quality of windows which can help lower your energy costs.
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * [ENERGY STAR](https://www.energystar.gov/products/residential_windows_doors_and_skylights/key_product_criteria#:~:text=With%20the%20equivalent%20energy%20performance,than%20or%20equal%20to%200.42)® certified
  * U-Factor of 0.28 or less 
  * Solar Heat Gain Coefficient of 0.23 or less 


  

## Incentive Amount 
$1 per sq. ft.
  

  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Insulation & Building Projects](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27524)
  

  1. [Window Film](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_3)


There are many different grades, shades, colors, and thicknesses of window films built to offer solutions to a variety of challenges including heat and glare reduction, thermal insulation, UV filtration and even safety and security.   
  

## ​Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  

  * Solar Heat Gain Coefficient of 0.39 or less
  * Only single-pane, exterior windows qualify. North-facing windows excluded

  

## ​Incentive Amount  

$1.00 per sq. ft.  
  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![iStock-135374538](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27525)  
  

  1. [Fire Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_4)


Fire-resistant attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance.   
  
​
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  

  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating   
(Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater  



## ​Incentive Amount   

$0.35 per sq. ft.  
Rebate cap of $750  
  

  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  

![iStock-183779679](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27526)  

  

  1. [Fire Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5242/Building-Projects#tab2f228fbf-a365-4845-ac22-448fdd73fa72_5)


These vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires
  

Installation/Efficiency Criteria 
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is Recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas ](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


## Incentive Amount
Up to $350 rebate 
  

  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/) for more information about fire safety.
  

![Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28987)
  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

714-765-4250 or Email

#  Back-Up Power 
  1. [Uninterruptible Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_0)
  2. [Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_1)


  1. [Uninterruptible Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_0)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines. 
## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
[ENERGY STAR® ](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-uninterruptible-power-supplies/results)Certified (for standard UPS only)
## Incentive Amount
$50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
$200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving [Medical Lifeline Rate](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=) (Maximum of 2)
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27539)
  

  

  

  1. [Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/5243/Back-Up-Power#tab8f1813c3-6136-4462-af5a-49d940f367ab_1)


A portable power station is a rechargeable battery-powered generator that can supply power to multiple devices at the same time. In addition, medical portable power stations are also available to help keep your equipment on during any loss of power. Best of all, you may be eligible to receive up to two (2) rebates for purchasing eligible Portable Power Stations, per residential address.   
  

## ​Product Criteria   

Battery Powered (minimum 100watt AC output)  
  

## ​Incentive Amount   

$50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)  
$200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving [Medical Lifeline Rate](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=) (Maximum of 2)  
  
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.  
  
![Electric Portable Power Station](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34153)  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

#  Pool Equipment 
  1. [Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/Pool-Equipment#tabd630a06f-79d3-47c4-9271-77e577b22677_0)


  1. [Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/5244/Pool-Equipment#tabd630a06f-79d3-47c4-9271-77e577b22677_0)


Variable speed (VS) motors can use 65% less energy than standard pool pumps and can get the same job done. Unlike single speed motors that only operate at maximum flow rates, variable speed pool pumps use efficient motors and advanced hydraulic designs to deliver the appropriate flow for water filtration and cleaning.
  

## Installation/Efficiency Criteria
  * Variable speed pool pumps and motors
  * Must be a replacement of an existing pool pump


  

## Incentive Amount 
$300 per pump
  

[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/1481/Home-Incentives-Rebate-Guidelines-Applic) for guidelines and how to apply.
  

![Pool Pump](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27542)
  

  1. [![APPLY NOW_REBATE TOOL Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37986)](https://apu.conservationrebates.com/)

# Website Accessibility
If you use assistive technology (such as a Braille reader, or a screen reader) and the format of any material on this website interferes with your ability to access information, please [contact us](https://www.anaheim.net/contactus). To enable us to respond in a manner most helpful to you, please indicate the nature of your accessibility problem, the preferred format in which to receive the material, the web address of the requested material, and your contact information.
## Accessibility Design Guidelines
Our website has been designed with the following accessibility guidelines in mind:
  * We work to comply with [Section 508](https://www.section508.gov/) as well as [WCAG 2.0 A and AA](https://www.w3.org/WAI/intro/wcag) referring to website accessibility standards.
  * If you have difficulty accessing the site or have any comments or feedback, please do not hesitate to [contact us](https://www.anaheim.net/contactus).


## Browser Accessibility Information
Many popular browsers contain built-in accessibility tools.
  * [Chrome Accessibility Information](https://www.google.com/accessibility/all-products-features.html)
  * [Firefox Accessibility Information](https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/accessibility-features-firefox)
  * [Microsoft Edge Accessibility Information](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-edge/accessibility/)
  * [Safari Accessibility Information](https://support.apple.com/accessibility)


## Additional Plug-ins
Adobe Reader is required to view and print PDF documents that appear on this website.
  * To download this program for free, visit the [Adobe website](https://www.adobe.com/products/reader.html).
  * To read PDF documents with a screen reader, please visit the [Adobe Reader Accessibility website](https://helpx.adobe.com/reader/using/accessibility-features.html) which provides useful tools and resources.


## Supported Assistive Technology
  * Latest Version of [JAWS](https://www.freedomscientific.com/Products/Blindness/JAWS) for Windows
  * Latest Version of [NVDA](https://www.nvaccess.org) for Windows
  * Latest Version of [VoiceOver](https://www.apple.com/accessibility/vision/) for Mac OS X
  * Keyboard-Only Navigation

#  Manage Your Utilities Online 
  * [ LOG IN ](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150)


  * [ PAY MY BILL ](https://apu51.ez-pay.io/validate.aspx?BillerId=VmicfoDAU6)


## What is MY ACCOUNT?
MY ACCOUNT is a convenient way to manage your Anaheim Public Utilities account online. Among other great features, it allows you to:
  * Pay your bill
  * Request a payment extension
  * Request a duplicate bill
  * Enroll in paperless billing
  * Enroll recurring payments 
  * Update your mailing address 
  * View billing and payment details 
  * View usage history


Find out more by visiting the [MY ACCOUNT FAQ](https://anaheim.net/faq.aspx?TID=25) page.
### Contact Us

  7. Fire Safety 


#  Be Fire Safe 
Properties in or near a fire threat zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any [Anaheim Fire & Rescue](https://www.anaheim.net/5629) recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.
![Picture1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64346)
![Picture2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64347)
* * *
## FIRE SAFETY REBATES
Anaheim Public Utilities provides rebates aimed to assist residents in saving money on fire safety upgrades.
  1. [Fire-Resistant Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_0)


This type of attic insulation is a layer of fiber that reduces the risk of fires starting in the attic while at the same time decrease the energy demands of heating and cooling systems. The effectiveness of this insulation is evaluated by its Class Fire Rating and R-Value, which quantifies heat exchange resistance. 
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Class 1 (A) Fire Rating 
  * (Invoice must detail Fire Spread 0-25)
  * R-30 rating or greater


**Incentive Amount**
  * $0.35 per sq. ft.
  * Rebate cap of $750


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Attic Insulation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28826)  
**Helpful Resources**
  * [Insulation Guidelines](https://www.energystar.gov/sites/default/files/asset/document/Technical%20Bulletin%20Achieving%20Grade%201%20Insulation%20in%20Fire-Rated%20Roofs.pdf)
  * [ENERGY STAR® Certified Insulation](https://www.energystar.gov/saveathome/seal_insulate/certified_insulation)
  * [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program](https://calfire.govmotus.org/BMLSearch/Index)


  1. [Fire-Resistant Vents](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_1)


These types of vents are designed to block embers and fire from entering the home through the attic during extreme situations such as wildfires or local vegetation fires.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Installation of Fire Resistant Attic Insulation is recommended
  * Product must be approved under the [California Fire Marshal’s Building Materials Listing Program for use in Wildland Urban Interface Areas](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/fire-engineering-and-investigations/building-materials-listing)


**Incentive Amount**
  * $350 rebate


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25676/Home-Incentives-Application?bidId=)
![Ventsd](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=28827)
  1. [Uninterruptible power supply (ups)](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_2)


An uninterruptible power supply (UPS), also known as battery backup, provides emergency power to critical devices during an unforeseen power outage. An UPS provides power stored in a battery to allow for safe, orderly shutdown of connected equipment (e.g. computers). Medical device UPS systems are designed to provide an uninterrupted power source to keep your medical device running when it is supposed to such as CPAP Machines.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * ENERGY STAR® Certified (for standard UPS only)  



**Incentive Amount**
  * $50 for standard equipment (Maximum of 2)
  * $200 for medical equipment - Must presently be receiving Medical Lifeline Rate (Maximum of 2)


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/1481)
![Power Supply](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=27539)
  

  1. [Battery storage](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab4a432f92-c5bf-4ec8-ab9f-2eeea9be6ac6_3)


Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
**Installation/Efficiency Criteria**
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be standalone or part of a future existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Schedule-TOU-2-?bidId=) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920) for at least 12 months


**Incentive Amount**
  * Up to $3,000 per household 


[Program guidelines and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)
![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=34164)
* * *
## ADDITIONAL FIRE SAFETY RESOURCES
Anaheim residents can take advantage of programs designed to enhance fire safety and resilience.
  1. [Wildfire Home Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_0)


The Wildland Urban Interface is the zone of transition between unoccupied land and human development. 
Communities surrounded by wildlands are at varying degrees of risk from wildfires. Schedule a one-on-one consultation with an Anaheim Wildfire Mitigation Specialist to identify potential wildfire threats within your property and ways you can harden your home.
Schedule your FREE Wildfire Home Assessment
  1. [Fire-Resistant Shade Trees](https://www.anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety#tab53dac53d-4f32-4483-9d13-d02b14320413_1)


TreePower offers a number of FREE fire-resistant shade trees that meet fire threat zone guidelines.
![Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=28816)  

  

**ZONE 1 -****within 30 feet of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Trim trees regularly to keep branches a minimum of 10 feet from other trees
  * Remove branches that hang over your roof and keep dead branches 10 feet away from your chimney
  * Remove or prune flammable plants and shrubs near windows


**ZONE 2 -****within 30-100 ft of all structures or to****the property line:**
  * Remove vegetation and items that could catch fire from around and under decks
  * Remove fallen leaves, needles, twigs, bark, cones, and small branches
  * Create horizontal spacing between shrubs and trees
  * Eliminate opportunities for a vertical "fire ladder" by:
    * Removing branches beneath large trees for a 6-foot minimum clearance
    * Allowing a minimum clearance of 3 times height of shrub underneath a tree


[Get your FREE trees](https://www.anaheim.net/757/)  

* * *

  7. Battery Storage


#  Battery Storage 
Battery Storage provides Anaheim Public Utilities customers with the economic and environmental benefits while accelerating the adoption of renewable energy in your home by storing energy when the sun is out and using it when it's not. Battery Storage also allows a certain level of back-up power during a planned or unforeseen power outage.
##    
Installation/Efficiency Guidelines 
  * Must be UL Certified
  * Energy Storage system must have minimum of 5kWh capacity 
  * Eligible Energy Storage Unit installation must be hardwired and part of an existing solar PV system
  * Energy Storage Unit shall be capable of storing energy produced from electrical energy, such as, solar PV energy, wind energy or some other residential produced electricity
  * Customer agrees to be on a [Time of Use](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/25947/Developmental-Schedule-D-TOU-2-050119) rate and [MyPower Savings Program](https://www.anaheim.net/5920/myPower-Savings) for at least 12 months
  * Please see program application for additional program guidelines 


## Rebate Amount
Up to $3,000 per household   
Guidelines on how to [ap](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24)[ply](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55963/Battery-Storage-Rebate-Application_7_10_24).  
  

## Customer Owned Solar Energy
Anaheim has supported more than 3,500 customers installing solar throughout the City. Beginning January 1, 2021, Anaheim Public Utilities will be transitioning to Net Energy Metering (NEM) 2.0, which is designed to offer the most benefit to customers who also install an energy storage system or are able to change their daily energy use to align with the needs of the grid. NEM programs are designed to credit electric customers for excess energy produced by their solar panels and delivered back on the electric grid. For more information about Anaheim's NEM 2.0 and customer owned solar, please [click here](https://www.anaheim.net/636)
  

![Home Battery Storage ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34164)

#  Property Info 
[![Property Info](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53280)](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=d4deefb190eb407dacf7f84680c8ea21)
[Property Info](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=d4deefb190eb407dacf7f84680c8ea21) is a custom mapping application that integrates City planning data into one easy to use application.   
  
[Property Info](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=d4deefb190eb407dacf7f84680c8ea21) can help you find:
  * Zoning Designation
  * General Plan Designation
  * Parcel Size
  * Parcel Number
  * Land Use Entitlements
  * Recent Building Permits


## [Click Here to View Property Info](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=d4deefb190eb407dacf7f84680c8ea21)

#  Disneyland Resort Specific Plan 
The Disneyland Resort Specific Plan (DRSP) was adopted in 1993 and was amended in 2024 in conjunction with the DisneylandForward project. The specific plan encompasses approximately 490 acres of The Anaheim Resort and provides for the development of an international vacation destination resort (The Disneyland Resort®) that allows the development of theme park, hotel and entertainment areas, administrative office facilities, back-of-house facilities, parking facilities, an internal transportation system, and the on-going modification of Disneyland. The DRSP includes four districts: the Theme Park District, the Parking District, the Southeast District, and District A; it also includes two overlays: the C-R Overlay and the Anaheim GardenWalk Overlay.
Environmental Impact Report No. 311 (EIR311) was certified in conjunction with the original adoption of the DRSP. An addendum to EIR 311 was prepared in conjunction with a 1996 amendment to the DRSP in conjunction with the approval of Development Agreement No. 96-01 by and between the City of Anaheim and Walt Disney World Company. Subsequent Environmental Impact Report No. 352 (SEIR352) was approved in 2024 in conjunction with the DisneylandForward project and the First Amended and Restated Development Agreement No. 96-01.
[![Disneyland Resort Specific Plan 92-1 Map Image Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14539)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2532)

#  Specific Plans 
[![Specific Plans Map 01172019](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=24777)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2456/Specific-Plans-Map-)
The City of Anaheim has approved 11 specific plans which provide focused guidance and regulation in designated areas. Each specific plan includes land use, circulation and infrastructure plans along with zoning requirements and design guidelines. View our [map of the specific plans](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/2456) in the City of Anaheim.

---|---

  7. Nohl Canyon Storage Tank


#  Nohl Canyon Water Storage Tank 
Project Summary  
Anaheim Public Utilities continually works with local and regional water agencies to make sure that we all have reliable water supplies today and for future generations.  

![View from above Nohl Canyon water storage tank](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=2493)
The Nohl Canyon Water Storage Tank - a planned 10-million gallon, above-ground drinking water storage tank constructed on the site of the existing Olive Hills Reservoir - is one critically important project that improves water quality and service reliability to our community.  
  
The storage tank helps meet customer demand for water, particularly during the typically hot, dry, high-demand summer months. It also: 
  * Delivers water by gravity flow to a wide area in the event of power outages
  * Meets the high water flows required to fight fires
  * Provides emergency storage to help supply water during short-term service interruptions from Metropolitan Water District of Southern California


  1. ![Crowd sitting in chairs under tent as man talks behind podium](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Path?filePath=%2fdocuments%5cIntranet%5c69%5c286%2fNohl+Canyon+Water+Storage+Tank+Dedication+Ceremony_201502091022561867.jpg)
  2. ![Dedication sign on side of road leading to Nohl Canyon water storage tank](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Path?filePath=%2fdocuments%5cIntranet%5c69%5c286%2fNohl+Canyon+Water+Storage+Tank+Dedication+Sign_201502091022562492.jpg)
  3. ![People standing next to dedication sign](https://www.anaheim.net/1013/Nohl-Canyon-Storage-Tank)
  4. ![People standing next to dedication sign](https://www.anaheim.net/1013/Nohl-Canyon-Storage-Tank)
  5. ![People standing next to dedication sign](https://www.anaheim.net/1013/Nohl-Canyon-Storage-Tank)
  6. ![People standing next to dedication sign](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Path?filePath=%2fdocuments%5cIntranet%5c69%5c286%2fNohl+Canyon+Water+Storage+Tank+Sign+4_201502091022565930.jpg)


### Quick Facts
  
  
For more than three decades, this site housed a 60-million gallon open reservoir at adjacent Olive Hills.  
  
It has since been replaced with a 10-million gallon, partially-buried, drinking water storage tank which improves water quality and service reliability to our community.  
  
A water storage tank at this elevation helps maintain adequate water pressure to deliver water to central Anaheim by gravity. It provides critical short-term redundancy that ensures continued service when our imported water from MWD’s supply source is interrupted.  
  
This facility includes a leak detection system and can withstand a major earthquake.  
  
Dedicated in 2011.

  7. Walnut Canyon Reservoir


#  Walnut Canyon Reservoir 
![Aerial of Lenain and WCR](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37693)
## Walnut Canyon Reservoir is a 920-million-gallon reservoir that supplies water to Anaheim homes and businesses.
The reservoir receives deliveries of imported, untreated Colorado River water from the Metropolitan Water District (MWD) of Southern California. Water from the reservoir is then treated at the August F. Lenain Treatment Plant prior to delivery to Anaheim Public Utilities' customers. The reservoir is capable of storing 920 million gallons of imported water, enough to supply the area for 30-45 days in the event of an interruption to imported supplies from MWD.
Walnut Canyon Reservoir provides operational flexibility to minimize imported water peaking charges during summer periods, which results in fluctuations of water levels. Additionally, when groundwater wells that serve the majority of Anaheim are offline for upgrades, the reservoir is relied upon to serve customers that may cause lower water levels.
## Quick Facts
  * Constructed in 1968, Walnut Canyon Reservoir has served the community for over to 50 years.
  * A working water storage reservoir, it serves as a primary source of water to the Anaheim hill-and-canyon areas; which allows the City to manage short-term, emergency, and seasonal water supply needs.
  * This reservoir provides water for helicopter-based firefighting. Water levels are constantly monitored and coordinated with Anaheim Fire and Rescue Department to ensure sufficient availability of water.
  * The Walnut Canyon Reservoir is also historically significant. While the construction crew was restoring the concrete “A” monument, contractors found a handwritten letter inside, which was signed by the original City crew. This letter serves as a testament to the pride that City staff have long had in their work.


![Wide view of Walnut Canyon Reservoir with houses in the distance](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=2463)

  5. About the Utilities


#  About the Utilities 
## Overview
  * Largest population in Orange County
  * 10th largest population in California
  * Area: 50 square miles
  * Average annual rainfall: 13.4 inches
  * Average annual temperature: 67 degrees
  * Annual visitors: more than 25 million
  * Population: 360,000


Anaheim residents are more than utilities customers, they are owners of their utilities. They provide input to the decision process both directly and through an appointed resident advisory Public Utilities Board. With final authority vested in Anaheim's elected City Council, decisions are made in the best interest of our residents, quality of life, and local economy. As a municipal, not-for-profit utilities, our rates are based on our costs of providing water and electricity. 
## Local Ownership, Local Control
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is a city-owned, not-for-profit electric and water utility that offers quality electric and water services to residents and businesses in Anaheim at rates among the lowest in Orange County.
[![Electric Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31367)](https://www.anaheim.net/2104/)
[![Water Services](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31366)](https://www.anaheim.net/1694/)
[![Who governs the Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31219)](https://www.anaheim.net/1930/)
## Anaheim has been hard at work serving its residents ever since its foundation in 1857
  
For more than 150 years, the city has cultivated the ability to rise to any challenge with innovative solutions to better the community. In 1879, Anaheim’s leaders took responsibility for operating the water system for their fledgling city. Then, in 1894, they seized the opportunity to build the first municipal electric utility in Southern California, changing the face of the region for years to come. 
Such reliable, affordable water and power attracted businesses and families, evolving Anaheim into a leading industrial and manufacturing center. In the 1950s, the city’s many unique qualities attracted the attention of the famous Walt Disney, who was looking for the perfect place to build his dream park. Anaheim boldly embraced the opportunity, and was soon catapulted onto the world stage - first as a dynamic tourist destination and later as a bustling convention, sports and entertainment hub. 
As a result, the Anaheim of today looks much different from the Anaheim of the past — and its demands for water and electricity are far higher than our ancestors could ever have imagined. But the dedication of the city to serving its community and meeting challenges head-on has remained unchanged. Our residential electric rates are the lowest in Orange County, and we still work hard to deliver quality water at a competitive price. We also offer a value-packed array of [rebates and incentives](https://www.anaheim.net/404/Rebates-Incentives) — helping our customers make more efficient and economical use of the critical resources we provide.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  6. Turf Replacement Program


#  Turf Replacement Program 
![Ditch the Turf banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=65290)
Anaheim and its regional partners are encouraging residents and businesses to help the environment by reducing potable water use for the irrigation of lawns.
One option for customers is to ditch their grass turf by planting native, drought-tolerant plants that**promote biodiversity** , significantly **reduce water use** by using drip irrigation (by as much as 70%), and**reduce heat islanding** when low water-use trees and plants are installed rather than pavers or synthetic turf.
### Turf Replacement Program Rebate
Through the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California’s (Metropolitan) SoCal Water$mart Program, Anaheim Public Utilities customers are eligible to receive $3 per square foot of turf removed, up to 5,000 sq. ft. for residents.
**Combine with our TreePower program** for [4 free trees](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/TreePower-Program-58) to support your project! Choose from more than 40 available species! Select “Trees for Turf Removal” when you apply.
**Contact us for a free consultation** to discuss turf replacement options and rebate requirements*.  
Call 714-765-4218.
* * *
_*Rebates are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Synthetic turf is not eligible._
  

### Turf Replacement Program Rebate Requirements
  
To be eligible for a rebate, you **must apply and receive project start approval before removing any turf.**
**Synthetic turf is not eligible.  
**
Please visit [socalwatersmart.com](https://www.socalwatersmart.com/) to view program requirements and begin the application process.  
  
New front yard and/or parkway projects must follow [City of Anaheim Landscaping Requirements](https://anaheim.net/1064/City-of-Anaheim-Landscaping-Requirements). Prior to installation, the **Anaheim Planning Department must review and approve the proposed landscape design.** Please call Planning Services at 714-765-5139 for assistance.
###

#  Youth Programs 
Anaheim’s youth programs provide a safe environment for the youth in our community. Using a variety of activities (such as recreational games, arts, crafts, and special events) we help develop social skills and encourage positive self-image. Our programs will help you keep the kids entertained, physically and emotionally fit, as well as meet new people and enable them to lead constructive lives. To learn more about the services we offer, please click on one of the programs below.  

To find out more about the services we offer, please access the individual programs:  
  

  * [Anaheim STARS (Study Time, Arts, Recreation & Sports)](https://www.anaheim.net/2385/art-in-the-park-and-kids-in-motion-locations)
  * [CampVenture](http://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/2285/CampVenture)
  * [Fun on Wheels After School Programs ](https://anaheim.net/2240/Fun-on-Wheels)
  * [O.A.K.S. Day Camp](http://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/1167/OAKS-Day-Camp)
  * [Project SAY (Support Anaheim's Youth)](http://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/1483/Project-SAY-Support-Anaheims-Youth)
  * [Tiny Tots Academy](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  * [Ramp N' Roll](https://www.anaheim.net/4576/Ramp-N-Roll)

#  Fire Department Volunteer Opportunities 
Community Emergency Response Team (CERT)  
Anaheim CERT promotes the ongoing training and participation of our volunteer force by providing many educational opportunities to better prepare for and respond to disasters. For more information please [visit the CERT resource](https://www.anaheim.net/887/Community-Emergency-Response-Team-CERT).  
  
Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Service (RACES)  
RACES members register their skills, qualifications and radio equipment capability and donate their time for training and operational readiness in the event they are needed during a disaster. All radio operators are licensed by the Federal Communications Commission. Each RACES member agrees to attend regular meetings and participate in training sessions, donating their time and skills to provide supplemental or emergency communications in the event of a local or widespread disaster. For more information on volunteering, please [access the RACES resource](https://www.anaheim.net/1209/RACES) within the Fire Department.

#  Volunteer & Civic Engagement 
The City of Anaheim has a variety of volunteer opportunities to suit your needs, from volunteering with the Fire Department, the Police Department, or with the Community Services Department.
To learn more about volunteer opportunities throughout Anaheim, we encourage you to visit the following resources:
  * City of Anaheim Volunteer Opportunities
  * Community Groups
  * Fire Department Volunteer Opportunities
  * Police Department Volunteer Opportunities
  * Volunteer Center of Orange County
  * Volunteer Match


* * *
![Anaheim Volunteer Recognition](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42674)
Join us for our annual celebration recognizing the City of Anaheim’s Volunteers, Business Sponsors, Service Organizations, and Individual Donors.

#  About Anaheim Public Library 
  

#   

## ![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=26168)
The City Librarian, Audrey A. Lujan welcomes you to the Anaheim Public Library! We are the City's leading information hub with a network of seven library branches serving the city of Anaheim and surrounding communities, plus a [Mobile Library](http://www.anaheim.net/928/Bookmobile) (Bookmobile), a STEAM Van, a book vending machine at [ARTIC](http://www.anaheim.net/3329/ARTIC) transportation center and the [Anaheim Heritage Center](http://www.anaheim.net/2473/Anaheim-Heritage-Center) which houses our local history collection. 
The Anaheim Public Library is the cornerstone of your neighborhood, providing resources and opportunities for all ages. Learn about the engaging programs and unique services offered to you and your family. Your Anaheim Public Library Card gives you access to our digital collection of both Audiobooks and eBooks through the [cloudLibrary](https://ebook.yourcloudlibrary.com/library/AnaheimPublicLibrary/Featured?media=all&src=lib) app. In addition, we offer a variety of [book recommendations](http://anaheim.net/3226/Reading-Recommendations), [digital learning tools](http://anaheim.net/1105/Digital-Resources), and a portal to the [Library Catalog](https://ana.ent.sirsi.net/client/en_US/virtual) where you can access books, historical photos, and track your reading list. 
##   

  

  1. [Mission Statement](https://www.anaheim.net/1222/About-Us#tab07ace6e2-b0f2-45a5-87ea-d640e148f035_0)
  2. [Equity Statement](https://www.anaheim.net/1222/About-Us#tab07ace6e2-b0f2-45a5-87ea-d640e148f035_1)


  1. [Mission Statement](https://www.anaheim.net/1222/About-Us#tab07ace6e2-b0f2-45a5-87ea-d640e148f035_0)


# Mission Statement  

  
Anaheim Public Library promotes literacy, lifelong learning, and a love of reading through traditional print materials as well as virtual services.   
  
Our mission is to promote information through programming and engage our community while providing a safe space for the diverse members of Anaheim to share, thrive, explore, create, enjoy, honor, celebrate, and engage with one another.  
  
_Approved by the Anaheim Public Library Board on April 8, 2019._  

  1. [Equity Statement](https://www.anaheim.net/1222/About-Us#tab07ace6e2-b0f2-45a5-87ea-d640e148f035_1)


# Equity Statement
Anaheim Public Library is committed to achieving equity by making meaningful contributions and helping our community members realize their potential. 
Our library system is working to achieve equity in the neighborhoods we serve by:
  * Continuing and building upon an environment of respect, diversity, and inclusion.
  * Regularly evaluating services, programs, policies, and practices in order to eliminate barriers or structural inequity in favor of improved library access.
  * Collaborating with community leaders in order to identify current challenges and address how to best meet the needs of our community.
  * Encouraging open communication about the issues and values important to our community.
#   



#    
  

#

book talks, storytimes,   
and special events.

  5. RACES


#  RACES 
Thank you for your interest in the Anaheim Radio Amateur Civil Emergency Services (RACES) program, sponsored by Anaheim Fire & Rescue’s Emergency Management and Preparedness Section. RACES is an emergency management volunteer program that utilizes state of the art ham radio technology to provide vital communications support during emergency incidents and routine events and activities.   
  

### RACES volunteers are able to:
  
•  
•  
•  
•  
•  
•  
  
Anaheim RACES holds monthly meetings at the Downtown Anaheim Community Center. Contact us for details on when and where exactly meetings are held.  
  

### Weekly Net 
  
We encourage you to check in during our weekly net, which is held every Monday at 7:30 p.m. Weekly net frequency is:   
146.265 MHz PL 136.5   
  

### Annual Activities
  
•  
RACES participates in the City of Anaheim’s annual disaster exercises when appropriate. The team provides emergency communications in the field and in the Emergency Operations Center (EOC).  
•  
RACES members bring our Emergency Communications trailer and generator to participate in the annual Ham Radio Field Day, which is a 24 hour amateur radio exercise held simultaneously throughout the nation. We hold this event on the last full weekend in June each year.  

  

###  Requirements
  
Membership Requirements for Prospective members:  

  * Complete and submit an Anaheim RACES Volunteer Application
  * Complete the Disaster Service Worker Form
  * Pass livescan conducted by the Anaheim Police Department
  * Complete the following online FEMA course IS-100 and IS-700
  * Provide a copy of your Amateur Radio License

Suite 300
Phone: 714-520-2200  
**Emergency: 911**

#  Programs & Services 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [APL Accessibility ](https://www.anaheim.net/6597/APL-Accessibility)
Our goal at Anaheim Public Library is to provide all patrons a welcoming experience where they feel valued and have equal access to resources and materials.
  2. ####  [Programs & Events](https://www.anaheim.net/4745/Programs-Events)
Find out about all the upcoming special programs and events at your library.
  3. ####  [Heritage Services](https://www.anaheim.net/2474/Heritage-Services)
The Heritage Center has an inventory of almost a million items organized and cataloged for use by scholars, students and researchers in the Disney Resort Reading Room. The center is located at Carnegie Plaza in the Anaheim Muzeo complex. Heritage Services also oversees the historic Mother Colony House and Woelke-Stoffel Victorian on the Founders' Park site. 
  4. ####  [For Kids & Families](https://www.anaheim.net/1095/For-Kids-Families)
Discover all the Library has to offer for children of all ages, from eBooks and homework help resources, to storytime videos and reading lists.
  5. ####  [STEAM Services](https://www.anaheim.net/5508/STEAM-Services)
Learn about the STEAM programs and services available at your neighborhood Library.
  6. ####  [Link Labs](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs)
  7. ####  [For Teens](https://www.anaheim.net/1154/For-Teens)
  8. ####  [For Adults](https://www.anaheim.net/2497/For-Adults)
Anaheim Libraries offer many services for adults of all ages, from computer classes to author events.

  6. Become an Instructor


#  Become a Contract Recreation Class Instructor 
The City of Anaheim has a goal of providing a variety of classes to its residents through the teachings of independent contractors. You can be one of those instructors! Share your knowledge, experience, and talent with the residents of Anaheim to enrich their minds, lead healthy lifestyles and learn a new activity or hobby.
##    
Instructor Forms
  

Complete the Contract Class Instructor Application and class proposal form to be considered for Fall 2022. 
  * [Become a Contract Recreation Class Instructor](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/Recreation-Class-Instructor-Application-245)
  * [Submit a class proposal for Winter 2023](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/Contract-Recreation-Class-Proposal-Form--272)


Once we receive your application or class proposal form, you will be contacted by Community Services Program Staff.
