  3. [City Clerk](https://www.anaheim.net/218/City-Clerk)
  4. Passport Services


#  Passport Services 
Anaheim offers passport services **by** **appointment**. Appointments are available **Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 3 p.m**.
Plan for your visit by taking a moment to read the information and instructions here before scheduling your appointment below.
To**make an appointment,** select an available date and time for passport services. Each appointment requested is for one applicant, so make one appointment for each person applying if coming with multiple people.
For more help, please call (714) 765-4540.  
  
[![Schedule an Appointment Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=37581)](https://www.anaheim.net/5924/Schedule-an-Appointment)
**_○_**[ _**Current Passport Processing Times**_](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/how-apply/processing-times.html) (U.S. Department of State) |  **_○_**[ _**Track your Passport Application Status**_](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/need-passport/status.html)(U.S. Department of State)  
---|---  
**IMPORTANT: Please review all requirements and the fees below prior to your scheduled appointment. If you are missing any of the required items/documents or required payments at the time of your appointment, you may be asked to reschedule your appointment at a later date.** **Please call (714) 765-4540 with any questions or with any extraordinary situations you may have.** **Plan to arrive**** _15 minutes_****prior to your scheduled appointment time to account for parking and the security screening when entering City Hall.**  
---  
* * *
**Location and Hours :** **Office of the City Clerk**  
200 S. Anaheim Blvd., 2nd Floor  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
(714) 765-4540 **Passport Appointment Hours:** Monday through Friday  
9:00 AM - 3:00 PM  
**By****Appointment Only**  
---  
  1. [Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_0)
  2. [Fees](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_1)
  3. [Additional Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_2)


  1. [Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_0)


##  ****Requirements
  1. **Completed Application**[unsigned application (Form DS-11) completed single-sided in black ink]:
     * Social Security Number is required on the application
     * Forms can be found online at <https://eforms.state.gov/>
  2. **Evidence of U.S. Citizenship**(one of the following):
     * Certified Birth Certificate
       * Minors under age 16 must submit Certified Birth Certificate to show parental relationship
       * Abstract Birth Certificates nor Hospital Birth Certificates are acceptable
     * Consular Report of Birth Abroad
     * Certificate of Naturalization
     * Previously issued undamaged U.S. Passport
       * Applicable for adult applicants not eligible to renew their passport by mail
  3. **Photo Identification**(one of the following):
     * Valid Driver's License
       * Note: Out of State Identifications require a secondary form of identification
     * Current Valid Passport
     * Valid Military Identification
  4. **All Applicants must be present** :
     * All applicants, including children, must be present during application process. For children under 16, both parents (with identification) must be present at the time of applying. If both parents are not able to appear in person, call before your appointment for additional requirements.
  5. **Passport Photo** :
     * One 2" X 2" Passport Photo
     * Photo Services available onsite
     * Visit [travel.state.gov/photos](https://travel.state.gov/photos) for passport photo requirements
  6. **Payments**(two separate payments, **Cash not accepted**) see Fees:
     * Application Fee (U.S. Department of State) paid by Check or Money Order only
     * Execution Fee (City of Anaheim) paid by Check, Money Order, or Visa or Mastercard


  1. [Fees](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_1)


Fees
**Two Separate payments are required - (1) U.S. Department of State and (2) the City of Anaheim (*Cash not accepted)**
Application Fees  
---  
## **Paid to (1) U.S. Department of State​**
| 
## **_*Check or Money Order only_**  
**Adult Passport Book** (age 16 and over) | **$ 130**  
**Adult Passport Card**(age 16 and over) | **$ 30**  
**Minor Passport Book**(age 15 and under) | **$ 100**  
**Minor Passport Card** (age 15 and under) | **$ 15**  
Optional Fees  
**Expedite Fee** (available to all applicants) | **$60**  
---|---  
**1 - 3 Day Return Delivery**  
(not available for Passport Cards) | **$22.05**  
  

Additional Fees  
---  
## **Paid to (2) City of Anaheim**
| _*****Check, Money Order or Visa/MasterCard****_  
**Execution Fee** (per application) | **$ 35**  
**Passport Photo** (per person) | **$ 10**  
**Photocopies** | **$0.15 (per page)**  
  

  
[Fee Calculator](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/how-apply/fees/fee-calculator.html) (U.S. Department of State) _*does not include additional fees such as photocopies and passport photos_
  1. [Additional Resources](https://www.anaheim.net/1875/Passport-Services#tab334f2b3e-6d21-424e-af6a-cbc71e13a637_2)


Additional Resources
  * [Current Passport Processing Times](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/how-apply/processing-times.html) (U.S. Department of State)
  * [Track your Passport Application Status](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/need-passport/status.html) (U.S. Department of State)
  * Renewing a U.S. Passport
    * [By Mail](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/have-passport/renew.html) (U.S. Department of State)
    * [Online](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/have-passport/renew-online.html) (U.S. Department of State)
  * [Applying for a passport due to a Life-or-Death Emergency](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/passports/get-fast/emergencies.html) (U.S. Department of State)
  * [Información en Español](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/es/pasaportes.html) (U.S. Department of State)

#  Online Payment 
9
1
1
1
  1. ####  General Services Payments 
If you’ve received a General Services Invoice, you can make your payment here. Please have your invoice on hand.
[Link to page](https://anaheimepay.net/GSOP)
  2. ####  Pay Library Fines 
Pay your library fines here.
[Link to page](http://www.anaheim.net/902/Library)
  3. ####  Pay or Contest Parking Citation 
A webpage for paying or contesting your parking ticket.
[Link to page](http://www.pticket.com/anaheim)
  4. ####  Pay Utilities Bill 
Make a payment on a utility bill.
[Link to page](https://apu51.ez-pay.io/validate.aspx?BillerId=VmicfoDAU6)
  5. ####  Permits 
Pay for miscellaneous fees on an issued permit.
[Link to page](https://www.anaheim.net/3532/)
  6. ####  Public Works E-Plan Check Submittals 
This is the City of Anaheim Electronic Plan Portal where you can submit your electronic plans for review by the City of Anaheim.
[Link to page](http://www.anaheim.net/5761/Public-Works---Electronic-Plan-Review)
  7. ####  Public Works Permitting Payments 
This site is for making trust account payments associated with a Public Works electronic plan check submittal or replenishment.
[Link to page](https://aca-prod.accela.com/anaheim/default.aspx)
  8. ####  Public Works E-Plan Payment 
Public Works E-Plan Payment
[Link to page](https://aca-prod.accela.com/anaheim/default.aspx)
  9. ####  Public Works Permit & Plan Check Status 
Information about searching for online permit requests and payments.
[Link to page](https://www.anaheim.net/1929/)

  5. Who Governs the Utilities


#  Who Governs the Utilities? 
**The[City Council ](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)represents residents and businesses in Anaheim and is made up of Anaheim's mayor, who is elected at large, and six City Council members elected by voters in their districts. **
**The Anaheim City Council****appoints****a 7 member** [**Public Utilities Board**](https://www.anaheim.net/1585/Public-Utilities-Board)**to****makes recommendations to the City Council concerning the operation of electric, water, and other public utilities under the management of the Public Utilities Department.**
## Public Utilities Board Responsibilities
  * Make recommendations concerning the establishment of rates, rules, and regulations for the operation of the electrical and water utilities of the City.
  * Make recommendations concerning the acquisition, construction, improvement, extension, enlargement, diminution, or curtailment of all or any part of the electric, water and any other public utilities under the operation and/or management of the Public Utilities Department.
  * Recommend financing, including the issuance of bonds for the electric and water utilities.
  * Review annual budget of the department and make recommendations accordingly.
  * Establish a Public Utilities Hearing Board composed of 3 current members of the Public Utilities Board.
  * Perform other related duties as outlined in the Municipal Code and such additional duties and functions as may be required from time to time by specific action and direction of the City Council.


## Compensation
There is no compensation for being a member of the Public Utilities Board.
## Term
Members serve for 2 consecutive 4-year terms and may not concurrently serve on 2 or more City boards and commissions.
## Conflict Disclosure
Members are required to complete and file, with the City Clerk, an annual Statement of Economic Interest, Form 700.
## Application/Selection Process
Scheduled and unscheduled vacancies are advertised by the City Clerk. Applicants must complete and submit their application online at [City of Anaheim: Board Application](https://anaheim.granicus.com/boards/forms/283/apply/4616798?code=f76c0456-7461-47b9-9f11-ccc6b9518933). 
Applicants will be forwarded to Council for their consideration. At an agenized Council meeting, nominations are made from the Council floor and voted on by Council.
## References
  * Anaheim City Charter §900
  * Municipal Code §1.04.700-730
  * Ordinance Nos. 3557(§1); 5397(§1)
  * 5774(§24). [5851 and 6066]


![awt.jpg](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=15676)

#  Featured Homes 
![1406 W. Ball Road](https://local.anaheim.net/com_dev/ART/FeatureHome01-2012.jpg) This remarkably preserved craftsman style home located on Ball Road between Walnut Street and Euclid Street was built by Sidney and Emma Holman in 1905 on a twenty acre citrus ranch known as the “Holman Place”. At that time, the Ranch was located “out in the country” surrounded by thousands of orange and lemon trees. Now the Holman House sits on a densely landscaped ¼ acre lot surrounded by the Wonderland Homes tract built in the mid-50’s. Peter and Linda Stewart have owned the home for over 35 years and have lovingly maintained and restored the home so that it retains the same look as it would have had over 100 years ago. The Holman House was designated as a Historically Significant Structure by the Anaheim City Council in September 2011 and received a Mills Act designation in December 2011. It is one of a very small number of houses to have received this distinction outside the City’s four downtown historic districts. This clapboard and shingle home has a deep sweeping front porch covered by a large gable that extends along the entire front of the house. The base, steps and built-in porch planters are all made of poured concrete. The unique low railing around the perimeter of the porch consists of cast stone/molded concrete blocks stacked in checkerboard fashion. The multi-paned leaded glass windows, original door and period screen door reflect attention to detail of craftsmanship prominent in Anaheim’s surviving orange grove homes.
![](https://local.anaheim.net/com_dev/ART/FeatureHome.jpg) Our featured home is nestled in one of Downtown Anaheim's latest created neighborhoods in the Anaheim Colony Historic District. This Queen Anne Victorian home, built in 1897 by John Rea (father of Kate and Ella Rea, for whom Katella Avenue was named) was originally located on the corner of Broadway and Philadelphia, and then moved to 125 W. Elm Street in 1922. This beautiful home was once again moved in 2007, and has been fully restored at 129 W. Stueckle Avenue. This rare architectural style and survivor of an early Anaheim era is a true gem to the community. This house was also home to former Anaheim Mayor, Louis Miller in 1928-1931.  
  
  
  

![](https://local.anaheim.net/com_dev/ART/685NHelenaEdit.jpg) This unique red brick Tudor Revival style residence, located at 685 N. Helena Street, was built in 1952 by former Mayor Charles Pearson and his wife Sarah. Mr. Pearson was a notable member of the Anaheim City Council for 25 years, during which time he was mayor from 1940 to 1959. The Pearson residence features a multi-gabled shingle roof, a prominent stylized brick chimney and a meandering brick pathway leading to an arched entryway. This residence is one of 43 historic homes built between 1941 and 1955 within the Hoskins Historic District. The District, adopted by the City Council in 2008, is a prestigious neighborhood of rambling Ranch style and stately Colonial and Tudor Revival homes highlighted by beautifully landscaped expansive front yards. This collection of mid-twentieth century homes illustrates Anaheim life among the middle and upper middle class of that era.
![](https://local.anaheim.net/com_dev/ART/FeatureHome09-2011.jpg) This single-story Craftsman style bungalow, built in 1912, sits prominently on a corner, directly across from Pearson Park. The home sits along a short block of wonderful residences that are best seen by a sidewalk stroll. Known as the Hilend House, this home, with its cross-gabled roof, brick columns and clapboard style redwood siding is accentuated by interesting Oriental influenced elements. Those features include the curving rafter tails and a unique set of four flared supports holding one long trim board with curved ends centered over the wide front porch. The rich colors selected for this home highlight the many trim features on the house, while allowing the siding to blend with the landscaping that complements the home. The lot that this house sits on was once owned by Clementina Zimmerman Schmidt who has been credited by historians with suggesting the name “Anaheim” which was eventually shortened and selected by a vote of the Los Angeles Vineyard Society members. James S. Hilend, a general contractor, eventually bought this lot and built the house. Over the course of the first 84 years of the life of this house, ownership had remained within two families, although several families had called it home over those years.

#  Rates, Rules & Regulations 
## Electric Utility
The electric tariff schedules have been approved by resolution by the City Council of the City of Anaheim and are the effective rates and rules of the Electric Utility. The City Council may amend or cancel these rates and rules by resolution. No official or employee of the City of Anaheim or the Electric Utility has any authority to waive, alter, or amend these tariff schedules in any respect, except in the manner provided by the tariff schedules.
  * [Title Page](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1963/)
  * [Introduction](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1960/)
  * [Table of Contents](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1962/)
  * [Electric Utility Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1961/)
  * [Rate Schedules](https://www.anaheim.net/653/)
  * [Rules](https://www.anaheim.net/883/)


## Water Utility
The water tariff schedules have been approved by resolution by the City Council of the City of Anaheim and are the effective rates and rules of the Water Utility. The City Council may amend or cancel these rates and rules by resolution. No official or employee of the City of Anaheim or the Water Utility has any authority to waive, alter, or amend these tariff schedules in any respect, except in the manner provided by the tariff schedules.
  * [Title Page](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1967/)
  * [Introduction](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1964/)
  * [Table of Contents](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1966/)
  * [Water Utility Policy](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/1965/)
  * [Rates Schedules](https://www.anaheim.net/891/)
  * [Rules](https://www.anaheim.net/894/)

#  City Clerk Bookshelf & Records 
Welcome to the City Clerk Bookshelf and Records – City resources at your fingertips. The Office of the City Clerk administers the records management program for the City of Anaheim. If you need assistance, please contact our office at (714) 765-5166 or cityclerk@anaheim.net.
* * *
## Records
  
[**Records Public Portal**](http://records.anaheim.net/cityclerk/Welcome.aspx) : City records readily available to the public online through the Public Portal include City Council Minutes, Resolutions, and Ordinances; Boards and Commissions minutes; Investment Portfolio Reports; Building Permits; Planning Commission records; Public Works Grading Plans; and Public Works Improvement Plans.
If you are unable to find the records you are looking for, please submit a [**Public Records Request**](http://www.anaheim.net/2153/Request-a-Public-Record).
  * [Public Records Request Archive](http://www.anaheim.net/2924/Public-Records-Request-Archive)
  * [Records Retention Schedule](http://www.anaheim.net/2905/Records-Retention-Schedules)


## Bookshelf

  * [City Charter](http://www.anaheim.net/2895/City-Charter)
  * [Ordinances, Resolutions, and Minutes](http://records.anaheim.net/cityclerk/Browse.aspx?startid=104223&dbid=0&)
  * [Council Policies](http://www.anaheim.net/2892/Council-Policies)
  * [Boards & Commissions Handbook](http://www.anaheim.net/2894/Boards-Commissions-Manual)
  * [Administrative Regulations](http://www.anaheim.net/2896/Administrative-Regulations)
  * [Enterprise System Catalog](http://www.anaheim.net/3310/Enterprise-System-Catalog)


## Filings
  * [Campaign Finance Disclosure](https://public.netfile.com/pub2/Default.aspx?aid=ANA)
  * [Ethics Certificates - Assembly Bill 1234](https://records.anaheim.net/CityClerk/Browse.aspx?startid=4211306)
  * [Event Ticket Distribution Disclosures (Form 802)](https://www.anaheim.net/6487/Event-Ticket-Distribution-Disclosures-Fo)
  * [Public Official Appointments Agency Report (Form 806)](https://public.netfile.com/pub/agr806.aspx?aid=ana)
  * [Statement of Economic Interests (Form 700)](https://public.netfile.com/pub/?aid=ANA)

  6. Anaheim 311


#  Anaheim 311 
311 is the City of Anaheim non-emergency phone number that connects callers to City Hall though one easy to remember phone number. Community members can call 311 within city limits or dial 714-765-4311 outside of city limits to reach a customer service representative.  

  * 311 customer service representatives can connect you with city staff or assist you with any question you may have.
  * Representatives are available in both English and Spanish, translation services are available for other languages.
  * 311 Call Center hours are Monday through Friday, 7:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. After hours self-service options are available or callers may also leave a message.

Community members may also use [our online system](https://www.anaheim.net/anytime) to ask a question or report a concern.  

### Contact Us
  1. ### **Anaheim 311**
  
Phone: 311  
Phone: 714-765-4311  
Phone: 714-765-5125 (TDD/TTY)
### **Hours**

#  Streetlight Services 
### To report a streetlight issue, please [submit a request ](https://www.anaheim.net/2671/Anaheim-Anytime-Request?id=2969)or contact us at 714-765-3300.
  
**For streetlight related problems that require immediate attention, such as broken wires, contact us immediately at 714-765-3300.**
The majority of streetlight issues are typically repaired within 3 to 5 working days. However, there are instances where repairs may take longer due to issues resulting from faulty cables, vandalism, and vehicle accidents. 
When submitting a streetlight service request, **please include the street address and streetlight pole number to assist** in identifying the correct streetlight and help expedite the repair process. 
Thank you for your commitment to improving streetlight reliability in Anaheim.
### Streetlight Replacement Program
  
The city of Anaheim has an ongoing program to improve roadway visibility and safety within various neighborhoods. New streetlight requests are coordinated by the Neighborhood Services section within the Community Services Department, and prioritized based on community feedback, assessed needs by other departments such as Police or Code Enforcement, and available funding. Roadways with minimal existing lighting and/or experiencing reports of illicit activities are generally assigned a higher priority. Once a list of priority locations has been established, a project will be formed and engineered to install the new streetlights.   

![street lineman 016](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51103)
### Report a streetlight issue:  
  

  * [Anaheim Anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/2671/Anaheim-Anytime-Request?id=2969)
  * Call 714-765-3300 


* * *
### Questions about this program? 
  
Please contact:
**Emily Perez**  
**Neighborhood Services**  
714-765-4397  
eperez1@anaheim.net
  
**Vinh Tran**  
**Anaheim Public Utilities**  
714-765-4273   
vtran@anaheim.net

  5. About Electric Services


#  About Electric Services 
### Anaheim Public Utilities operates the only municipal electric system in Orange County. That means that the ratepayers of this community own their utility, and therefore, the key decisions concerning its operation can be made right here in Anaheim.
## Did You Know
  * Anaheim residents enjoy the lowest electric rates in Orange County.
  * Anaheim is one of the few Orange County cities that do not collect a utility users tax.
  * Anaheim Public Utilities owns one of the largest municipal solar photovoltaic (PV) system on top of a convention center in the North America. At 2.4 megawatts and consisting of 7,908 panels, the system produces 3.5 million kWh's annually, which is enough to power 600 homes in Anaheim.
  * Qualifying Anaheim senior, long-term disabled, and military veteran [customers can save 12%](https://anaheim.net/1520/Utility-Discount-Program) off their electric and water portions of their Anaheim Public Utilities bill.
  * The first 300 kWh's a month for typical Anaheim residents are billed at a low lifeline rate – more than 40% lower.
  * Added [lifeline allowances](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/369/Medical-Lifeline-Allowance-Application-PDF?bidId=) are available to customers with medical needs.
  * Qualifying businesses are able to switch to Anaheim's voluntary TOU rate, to save money by operating outside of hours when electric demand is highest.


### [Utitities Audited Financials and Continuing Disclosure](https://www.anaheim.net/901/Investor-Relations)
**Electric supply  
**
Anaheim Public Utilities' power supply comes from resources located in Anaheim and across the Western United States.
# Power generating capacity   
---  
[Anaheim Solar Energy Plant   
](https://www.anaheim.net/5090) | Anaheim, CA | Solar - _renewable_ | 2.1 MW  
[Brea Power II](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21467/Brea-Power-II) | Brea, CA | Landfill Gas - _renewable_ | 27.8 MW  
[Bowerman Power Facility](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21466/Bowerman-Power-Facility) | Irvine, CA | Landfill Gas - _renewable_ | 19.6 MW  
[Canyon Power Project](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21469/Canyon-Power-Project) | Anaheim, CA | Natural Gas | 200 MW  
[Desert Harvest Facility](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21472/Desert-Harvest-or-Maverick-Facility) | Desert Center, CA | Solar -  _renewable_ | 36 MW  
[High Winds Energy Center](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21449/High-Winds-Energy-Center) | Birdslanding , CA | Wind - _renewable_ | 6 MW   
[Hoover Dam](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21450/Hoover-Dam) | NV/AZ border | Large Hydro | 40 MW  
[Intermountain Power Plant](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21451/Intermountain-Power-Plant) | near Delta, UT | Coal (Gas 2025) | 236 MW  
[Magnolia Power Project ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21453/Magnolia-Power-Project) | Burbank, CA | Natural Gas | 118 MW  
[MWD Hydroelectric Project](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21455/MWD-Hydroelectric-Project) | LA Basin Area, CA | Small Hydro - _renewable_ | 9.7 MW   
[Pleasant Valley Energy Center](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21456/Pleasant-Valley-Energy-Center) | Evanston, WY | Wind - _renewable_ | 30 MW  
[Thermo No.1](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21460/Thermo-No-1) | Beaver County, UT | Geothermal - _renewable_ | 11 MW  
[San Gorgonio Wind Farm](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21458/San-Gorgonio-Wind-Farm) | Whitewater, CA | Wind - _renewable_ | 31 MW  
[Westside Solar WSP PV1](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/21461/Westside-Solar-WSP-PV1) | Lemoore , CA | Solar - _renewable_ | 2 MW  
  

Total Generated & Purchased Power (FY 2023): |  2,721,438 MWh  
---|---  
FY 2023 Distribution system | Circuit miles  
---|---  
**Transmission, 69kV, circuit miles** | 89.0  
**Distribution, 12kV and lower, circuit miles** |   
- Overhead | 383  
- Underground | 786  
**Substations** | **14**  
FY 2023 Transformer capacity | kVA  
---|---  
220kV to 69kV | 1,808,000  
69kV to 12kV | 1,325,800  
12kV to customer | 1,832,239  
FY 2023 Number of meters | #  
---|---  
Residential | 105,422  
Commercial | 17,500  
Industrial | 290  
Other | 110  
Other utilities | 11  
**Total meters** | **123,33**  
FY 2023 Megawatt-hour sales | MWh  
---|---  
Residential | 637,348  
Commercial | 731,065  
Industrial | 855,536  
Other | 493  
Other utilities | 470,473  
**Total sales** | **2,694,915**  
FY 2023 Revenue from sales | $  
---|---  
Residential | $ 106,124,000  
Commercial | 122,100,000  
Industrial | 120,336,000  
Other | 3,094,000  
Other utilities | 35,320,000  
**Total sales** | **$387,004,000**  
**Definitions**  
**  
**
  * **1 kilowatt (kW)** = 1,000 Watts.
  * **1 megawatt (MW)** = 1,000 kW - Enough electricity to serve about 750 Anaheim residences.
  * **kW and MW** - Refer to the instantaneous amount of electricity being used or generated.
  * **Peak demand** -
  * **1 kilowatt-hour**(kWh) - Lights ten 100-watt light bulbs or 66 LED equivalent bulbs for one hour.
  * **1 megawatt-hour**(MWh) - Lights ten thousand 100-watt light bulbs or six-thousand LED equivalent bulbs for one hour.
  * **1 MWh** = 1,000 kWh.
  * **kWh and MWh** - Refer to the amount of electricity used during one hour.

  1. ####  [Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/665/Administration)
Fire Administration provides administrative support to the Fire Chief as well as carries out a variety of other duties outlined in this section. 
  2. ####  [Financial Services](https://www.anaheim.net/732/Administrative-Services)
The Finance Section of the Anaheim Fire Department provides financial services that support the operating functions of the department. 
  3. ####  [Fire Prevention / Community Risk Reduction](https://www.anaheim.net/699/Fire-Prevention-Community-Risk-Reduction)
The Fire Prevention and Community Risk Reduction Division operates under the direction of the Fire Marshal, and consists of 4 principle sections outlined in this resource. 
  4. ####  [Operations](https://www.anaheim.net/678/Operations)
The Operations Division is the largest division in the Anaheim Fire Department. 
  5. ####  [Support Services](https://www.anaheim.net/735/Support-Services)
The Support Services Division provides non-emergency support to the Operations Division.

  1. [Home](https://www.anaheim.net/)
  2. [Departments](https://www.anaheim.net/148/Departments)
  3. [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
  4. Anaheim Anytime 


![Anaheim Anytime logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35594)
**Anaheim Anytime** provides our residents, businesses and visitors the ability to **share problems, issues or other concerns they see in their neighborhood or around town** with the city 24 hours a day, seven days a week.
Anaheim Anytime is our **p****artnership with our community to address issue and maintain neighborhoods**. Use it to report **graffiti, abandoned items, streetlight outages** and other issues. It's the most effective way to bring issues to our attention, with every Anaheim Anytime inquiry **assigned and followed up on**. 
**Please note:** Requests are monitored during business hours. If you have an **emergency, please call 911**. If you have a **non-emergency police issue** , question or request, please call (714) 765-1900.

  5. Request for Public Records


#  Request for Public Records 
STEP 1: |  COMPLETE and submit the online form below by entering all fields on this form. This form is to facilitate and expedite the processing of your public records request. Describe identifiable records in the possession of the City; your request must be sufficiently focused and specific to allow the City to locate the requested record(s).  
  
---|---  
STEP 2: |  WAIT to receive a response from the City. The City shall determine within 10-days from receipt of a public records request, whether the request, in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records in possession of the City [GC 7927.535(a)].  
  
Please note that if you are requesting the opportunity to inspect records, you will be requested to make an appointment to return at a later date/time to view the documents.  
  
Charges for the direct cost of duplication will apply. **Documents will****not** **be copied until payment has been received.** If payment is not received within 20 days after invoice is sent, you may be required to submit a new request.  
(see [instructions](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5106/INSTRUCTIONS-FOR-REQUESTING-PUBLIC-RECORDS-?bidId=) for additional information)  
ALTERNATIVELY, a completed [request form](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/5105/Public-Records-Request-Form) can be submitted by mail, fax, email or in person to Custodian of Records, Office of the City Clerk, 200 S. Anaheim Blvd. recordsrequest@anaheim.net; fax (714) 765-4105. 
  1. [Records Public Portal](http://records.anaheim.net/cityclerk/Welcome.aspx?)
  2. [Request a Public Record](http://www.anaheim.net/2153/Request-a-Public-Record)

#  Fun on Wheels 
  1. ![Fun on Wheels \(11\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=59015)
  2. ![Fun on Wheels February \(8\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=59016)
  3. ![Fun on Wheels February \(6\)](https://www.anaheim.net/2240/Fun-on-Wheels)
  4. ![Fun on Wheels \(5\)](https://www.anaheim.net/2240/Fun-on-Wheels)
  5. ![Fun on Wheels \(10\)](https://www.anaheim.net/2240/Fun-on-Wheels)
  6. ![Rose-Bush-Vine leprechaun craft 3](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=59020)


Fun on Wheels Mobile Recreation Program is a free drop-in program designed to bring recreation and other resources into the neighborhoods for children, ages 5-16. The program offers homework assistance, fitness fun, art enrichment, and resource materials. 
Children are encouraged to remain at the program location, but are free to come and go at any time. **The staff are not responsible for children who choose to leave the program location.**
We look forward to seeing you soon! 
  * Anna Drive: 600 N. Block of Anna Drive
  * Stoddard Park: 1901 9th St, Anaheim, CA 92802
  * Kodiak: 300 N. Block of Kodiak St
  * Danbrook Elementary School: 320 S Danbrook Dr, Anaheim, CA 92804
  * Balsam/Curtis: 2105 N. Block of Curtis Ct. 
  * N. Pauline/N.Topeka: 515 N. Block of Pauline St. 
  * Balsam/East: 900 E. Block of Balsam Ave
  * Benmore/Canfield: 1500 E. Block of Canfield Lane
  * Rose/Bush/Vine: 400 N. Block of Vine St
  * Rio Vista Park: 201 N Park Vista St, Anaheim, CA 92806 * Returns Aug. 30th


For more information, please call (714) 765-5191.All programs are closed on days of inclement weather and school breaks. During extreme heat or rain, please call our Inclement Weather Hotline at (714) 765-4090 to receive up to date information of site cancellations.

#  Planning Commission 
![Council Chambers](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=7052)
The Planning Commission is comprised of seven City residents, appointed by the City Council to review both developmental proposals and policies related to land use within the City. The Commission meets alternating Mondays. The meetings are open to the public and public comment on city planning matters is encouraged.
[![PC View Live Stream Banner Gradiant Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53542)](https://anaheim.granicus.com/player/event/1784?view_id=2&redirect=true)

#  Contact 
To learn more about the Short-Term Rental program go to [](http://anaheim.net/574/Short-Term-Rental-Program) [](http://www.anaheim.net/STRor) www.anaheim.net/STR, visit us at City Hall or call Business License staff at 714-765-5194.
You may submit your questions regarding short-term rentals to STRComments@anaheim.net.
Anaheim City Hall  
200 S. Anaheim Blvd. #136, First Floor  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
714-765-5194  
Hours: 8 AM - 5 PM Monday - Friday
[](http://user.govoutreach.com/anaheim/faq.php)

#  Ponderosa Teen Center 
The Project S.A.Y. (Support Anaheim's Youth) Ponderosa Teen Program is open to Junior High and High School youth, ages 13-17. The Teen Center is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 3:00 p.m. - 7:00 p.m. Teen activities include: Leadership activities, drop-in sports, homework help, computer lab, fitness programs, development workshops, tutoring, and youth excursions,
[![community services interior logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42964)](https://www.anaheim.net/6094)

#  Anaheim S.T.A.R.S. 
  1. ![Miraloma STARS](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58983)
  2. ![IMG_4576](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58984)
  3. ![STARS Pearson-Bear Craft](https://www.anaheim.net/2385/Anaheim-STARS)
  4. ![STARS Ponderosa-Spash Pads](https://www.anaheim.net/2385/Anaheim-STARS)
  5. ![STARS TWILA](https://www.anaheim.net/2385/Anaheim-STARS)
  6. ![STARS Barton-Elephant](https://www.anaheim.net/2385/Anaheim-STARS)
  7. ![STARS Brookhurst-Bird House3](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58990)


S.T.A.R.S (Study-Time, Arts, Recreation, and Sports) is offered throughout Anaheim. This free, drop-in program will offer children, ages 5-16, academic support, art enrichment, sports, and recreation opportunities. Children are encouraged to remain at the program location but are free to come and go at any time. **The staff are not responsible for children who choose to leave the program location.**  
  
This is a year around program offered Monday through Friday, from the time students are released from school until 6:00 p.m. but times may vary based on location and individual school schedules. Hours adjust during school breaks and will be closed on holidays. Please see below for locations:
  * Brookhurst Community Center: 2271 West Crescent Avenue, Anaheim, CA 92801
  * Miraloma Family Resource Center: 2600 East Miraloma Way, Anaheim, CA 92806
  * Pearson Park: 400 North Harbor Blvd., Anaheim, CA 92805 
  * Twila Reid Park: 3100 West Orange Avenue, Anaheim, CA 92804


We are pleased to announce that the City of Anaheim's S.T.A.R.S program has partnered with the Anaheim Elementary School District's ELOP program. You will now be able to find us in 5 Elementary schools through the district, which will run afterschool Monday - Friday. We will also be following the districts bell and calendar schedules throughout the school year. 
  * Clara Barton Elementary:1926 W Clearbrook Lane, Anaheim, CA 92804
  * Thomas Edison Elementary: 1526 E Romneya Dr., Anaheim, CA 92805
  * John Marshall Elementary: 2066 W Falmouth Ave, Anaheim, CA 92801
  * Benito Juarez Elementary: 841 S Sunkist Street, Anaheim, CA 92806
  * Ponderosa Elementary: 2135 S Mountain Ave, Anaheim, CA 92802
  * Roosevelt Elementary: 1600 E Vermont Ave, Anaheim, CA 92805


For more information, please call (714) 765-5191. During extreme heat or rain, please call our Inclement Weather Hotline at (714) 765-4090 to receive up to date information of site cancellations. Come out and see us!

Anaheim, CA 92805

#  Request 
We offer several way in which you can interact with us. To request our participation at your [community event](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Fire-Rescue-7/Community-Events-Request-Form-351), schedule a [school program](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Fire-Rescue-7/School-Program-Request-Form-350) by a fire company or [tour a fire station](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Fire-Rescue-7/Station-Tour-Request-Form-349) please click on the appropriate links.

#  DAYC Teen Center 
The Project S.A.Y. (Support Anaheim's Youth) DAYC Teen Program is open to Junior High and High School youth, ages 13-17. The Teen Center is open Tuesday, Thursday, Friday from 3:30 p.m. - 7:30 p.m. Teen activities include: Leadership activities, drop-in sports, homework help, computer lab, fitness programs, development workshops, tutoring, and youth excursions,

#  Tiny Tots Academy 
  1. ![BCC staff writing](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53345)
  2. ![EACC Group](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53346)
  3. ![EACC Halloween](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  4. ![EACC jungle](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  5. ![BCC Birthday Party](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  6. ![BCC staff ](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  7. ![EACC class safari](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  8. ![Steam Van PRC](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  9. ![EACC Jungle 2](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  10. ![EACC safari 2](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  11. ![EACC safari 3](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  12. ![Jungle BCC](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  13. ![PRC letters](https://www.anaheim.net/2287/Tiny-Tots-Academy)
  14. ![Steam Van BCC](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53358)


**Our Program Philosophy**
The Tiny Tots Academy is a Recreational Program for children ages 3-5 that recognizes the unique and individual needs of each child. We prioritize parent involvement as we carry out programming to ensure that we are supporting our Tiny Tots inside and outside of the program. We strive to be a resource for the community as we work _with_ parents to strengthen our Tiny Tots’ social-emotional development through socialization, self-esteem building, and self-confidence.
**Mission Statement**
At the Tiny Tots Academy, we help our kids get F.I.I.T. for kindergarten by building their **F** oundational skills, encouraging them to **I** nteract with kids their age, and having a safe space to use their **I** magination, all while implementing classroom **T** raditions.
**The 5 Key Skills**
Programming is designed to strengthen each child’s school readiness by focusing on the 5 KEY SKILLS:
![5 Key Skills](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=50906)
**Programming**
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50904) to see a sample schedule of daily activities at the Tiny Tots Academy.
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50903) to see a sample of our programming curriculum.
Classes are held year-round and offered Monday through Friday from 9:00 a.m. - 12:00 p.m. There are 3 different enrollment options available:
  * Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
  * Tuesday and Thursday
  * Monday through Friday


NOTE: All participants must be fully potty trained. Pull-ups or diapers are not permitted.
The Tiny Tots Academy is now accepting toddlers ages 3 and up! For children under 3 years old **and** fully potty trained, we encourage families to schedule a tour to determine their child’s program readiness.
  
For more information or to schedule a tour, call (714) 765-4980. Registration can be completed [online](https://secure.rec1.com/CA/city-of-anaheim/catalog/index/27d2d8c29b3ae3bd16a3e94d53463cb2?filter=c2VhcmNoPXRpbnkrdG90cythY2FkZW15).  
  

## Locations
  

We have three convenient locations throughout the City of Anaheim.
  

**[**Brookhurst Community Center**](https://www.google.com/maps/place/Brookhurst+Community+Center+and+Park/@33.8420208,-117.9626091,15z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x0:0xf08989cd46365147?sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwizzvfF8prVAhXEw1QKHbw6B90Q_BIIhgEwDg)** | 2271 W. Crescent Avenue, Anaheim 92801   
---|---  
**[**East Anaheim Community Center**](https://www.google.com/maps/place/East+Anaheim+Community+Center/@33.8687745,-117.7463798,15z/data=!4m2!3m1!1s0x0:0x591899bbc5171291?sa=X&ved=0ahUKEwjrpZjV8prVAhUlr1QKHQrTDBgQ_BIIejAK)** | 8201 E. Santa Ana Canyon Road, Anaheim 92808   
**[**Peralta Recreation Center**](https://www.google.com/maps/place/111+N+Pinney+Dr,+Anaheim,+CA+92807/@33.8513324,-117.8042541,3860m/data=!3m1!1e3!4m13!1m7!3m6!1s0x80dcd11e99bfba09:0x3ab83e1584d0faf1!2s111+N+Pinney+Dr,+Anaheim,+CA+92807!3b1!8m2!3d33.8517146!4d-117.8024664!3m4!1s0x80dcd11e99bfba09:0x3ab83e1584d0faf1!8m2!3d33.8517146!4d-117.8024664)** | 111 N. Pinney Drive, Anaheim 92807

#  Miraloma Teen Center 
The Project S.A.Y. (Support Anaheim's Youth) Miraloma Teen Program is open to Junior High and High School youth, ages 13-17. The Teen Center is open Monday, Wednesday, Friday from 3:00 p.m. - 6:00 p.m. Teen activities include: Leadership activities, drop-in sports, homework help, computer lab, fitness programs, development workshops, tutoring, and youth excursions,
[![community services interior logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42964)](https://www.anaheim.net/6094)

#  Public Works Records 
The following records are available for customers to research and download on our public Laserfiche [database](http://records.anaheim.net/cityclerk/Welcome.aspx?cr=1):
  * Grading Plans
  * WQMP Reports
  * Improvement Plans (Street, Sewer, Storm Drain, Signal, Landscape, etc.)


Cookies must be enabled. If you are unfamiliar with Laserfiche, you may find these [instructions](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/67340) useful.
If you are in need of assistance or would like to submit a Public Records Request, please contact Public Works Records at PWRecords@anaheim.net or 714-765-5179.
**County Records**
To obtain copies of County records such as Tract Maps, Parcel Maps, Lot Line Adjustments, Centerline Ties (Corner Records), Certificates of Compliance, and more, please visit [Orange County Public Works Land Records](https://www.ocgis.com/ocpw/landrecords/). To contact Orange County Public Works records, please call 714-967-0852.

#  Crime Alerts 
Stay up to date on your neighborhood by signing up for Crime Alerts. It’s quick and easy to subscribe to this service. You can customize your alerts by how often you want them, the area you are interested in and the type of crime that interests you.  
  
Your email address and personal information will not be shared with any third parties. You can unsubscribe at any time.  

  

[![Crime Alert Signup Screenshot](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6162)](http://www.crimemapping.com/subscriptions/subscribe.aspx "CrimeMapping.com")
[Go To CrimeMapping.com »](http://www.crimemapping.com/subscriptions/subscribe.aspx)

#  Heritage Services 
The Heritage Center has an inventory of almost a million items organized and cataloged for use by scholars, students and researchers in the Center's Reading Room. We are located at Carnegie Plaza in the Anaheim Muzeo complex. Heritage Services also oversees the historic Mother Colony House and Woelke-Stoffel Victorian on the site of the Anaheim Founders' Park. 
### Heritage Center
[![Heritage Center Disney Resort Reading Room](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6286)](http://www.anaheim.net/2473/Anaheim-Heritage-Center)

## 2019 Transportation Fair 
Thank you to everyone that participated in the Annual Transportation Fair & Chili Cook-Off! This was the most successful event to date. Proceeds will be donated to  
  
The Transportation Fair is hosted by the Public Works Department. The intent of this fair is to educate the community and promote alternative modes of transportation such as walk, bike, carpool, bus, vanpool, train, and electric vehicles. We invite transportation related vendors to showcase their services to the public and employees.  
  
Contact the Anaheim Transportation Center for more questions about the event (714) 765-KARS (5277).  
  
  

  
  

  1. [Anaheim Transportation Center](https://www.anaheim.net/5412/Anaheim-Transportation-Center)

#  For Adults

  1. [Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/2497/For-Adults#tab5129df4a-18ea-4d94-8556-579ac8d6cb39_0)


# Programs for Adults
Whether you are exploring a creative hobby or want to learn something new, your neighborhood branch offers various engaging programs. Find various engaging programs and workshops ranging from gardening to health care, local history, and writing to much more. 
  
[![Find Adult Programs Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42294)](https://anaheim.net/calendar.aspx?CID=38&showPastEvents=false)
  1. [Resources & Services](https://www.anaheim.net/2497/For-Adults#tab5129df4a-18ea-4d94-8556-579ac8d6cb39_1)


# Resources & Services for Adults
**Online Resources**   
You can access many of our online resources for free with your Library card number. Take free online classes to master new skills, access thousands of current magazines, and more.
**Career Resources**  
The Library is a great place to start your job search, with resources for discovering a new career path, gaining new job skills, creating a cover letter and resume, and finding job opportunities.
**Citizenship Corner**  
New citizens can find helpful resources needed to succeed in the United States naturalization test. The Citizenship Corner at Central Library offers assistance with community partners, virtual citizenship classes, study materials, and more. You can also find books focusing on naturalization, finances, employment, and more.
  

[](https://anaheim.net/5430/Citizenship-Corner)[![Explore Online Resources Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42293)](https://www.anaheim.net/1105/Online-Resources) [![Career Resources Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42363)](https://anaheim.net/1125/Career-Resources)[![Citizenship Corner Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42308)](https://anaheim.net/5430/Citizenship-Corner)
  

  

  

  

  

  

  

  1. [Books & More](https://www.anaheim.net/2497/For-Adults#tab5129df4a-18ea-4d94-8556-579ac8d6cb39_2)


# Books & More  
  

  1. Browse the Library Catalog to find books, book club kits, DVDs, and more.
  2. Download free eBooks and audiobooks with cloudLibrary.
  3. Find that next great book with our Book News newsletters and recommendations from our Library Book Clubs.[![Find Books and More Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42263)](https://ana.ent.sirsi.net/client/en_US/virtual)[![eBooks and audiobooks Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42361)](https://ebook.yourcloudlibrary.com/library/AnaheimPublicLibrary/Browse)[![Join a Book Club Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=42295)](https://anaheim.net/992/Book-Clubs-for-Adults)

  5. [Community Engagement](https://www.anaheim.net/248/Community-Engagement)
  6. Customer Service


#  Customer Service 
The Anaheim Police Department takes pride in offering World Class Customer Service. We would appreciate if you could take a moment to give us your feedback, in order to help us identify ways to improve our service level and attain our goal of providing excellent customer service to our residents and visitors. Thank you in advance for your time and feedback.  

Message from the Chief of Police
The Anaheim Police Department is committed to providing professional, quality service to our community. Our highly trained and dedicated employees strive to meet the demands and expectations of a diverse society, often under challenging and complex circumstances. To assist us in consistently delivering on these commitments, we have a system in place to receive and respond to complaints from members of our community including residents, visitors and those who work in Anaheim. Once received, your complaint will be thoroughly and objectively investigated to gather all of the facts to determine what occurred. If we determine the actions or performance of our employee(s) were not consistent with our policies or fell short of our expectations in terms of performance, appropriate action will be taken with the objective to maintain and improve our service to our community. 
How do I file a compliment?  
  
If you want to share your experience and provide us your feedback to help us recognize our department personnel for delivering exceptional service, please click here for the online form in [English](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Customer-Service-Personnel-Compliment-En-93) or [Spanish](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Servicio-al-Cliente-Elogio-de-un-Emplead-96).
How do I file a complaint?
  

If you wish to file a formal complaint, please complete a Personnel Complaint Form. You may obtain this form at any of these locations:
  

  * Any police department station
  * The City Clerk's Office
  * Any Anaheim Public Library
  * The Community Services Office
  * [Download the form online](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50892/Personnel-Complaint-Form---Merged-Aug2023)


All forms should be submitted to:
  

> _Anaheim Police Department  
>  425 S. Harbor Boulevard  
> Anaheim, CA 92805_
  

Or you can complete the form online, click here for the online form in [English](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Customer-Service-Personnel-Complaint-Eng-84) or [Spanish](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Police-Department-13/Customer-Service-Quejas-Contra-El-Person-92). 
What will happen to the officer / employee?  
  
It will depend on the situation and what the officer's or employee's actions were. If the employee's actions were criminal, he/she will be dealt with in the same manner as any other citizen. If the officer's actions were improper but not criminal, he/she may be disciplined. 
Will I be told of the results of the investigation?  
  
Yes. You will receive a letter from the Office of the Chief of Police advising you of the disposition of your complaint.

  5. Jail


#  Jail 
The Anaheim Detention Facility is located in the Anaheim Police Department main station at 425 South Harbor Boulevard in Anaheim. The public entrance to the Detention Facility is located on the south side of the building on Santa Ana Street, west of Harbor Boulevard.   
  
The Detention Facility is a Type 1 jail facility and can house approximately 120 pre-trial arrestees for both felony and misdemeanor bookings. 
### Visitations
  

A person who has been arrested and booked in the Anaheim Detention Facility may be allowed visitors between the hours of 10:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. daily, but are not allowed visitors on the day they are booked.
  

Work Furlough inmates are allowed visitors only on weekends and holidays between the hours of 2:00 p.m and 5:00 p.m.
  

### Prison Rape Elimination Act (PREA)
  

  

**Zero-Tolerance for Sexual Abuse in Detention**
  

Sexual abuse in detention is a vastly underreported crime. As in the community, many victims of sexual abuse in custody do not report abuse due to shame or fear that they will not be believed. It is also common for victims to face retaliation for speaking out. In recognition of these barriers, the Anaheim Police Department Detention Facility applies the Prison Rape Elimination Act (PREA) standards to allow for reporting safer and easier for victims of sexual abuse and sexual harassment. An example of one of these reporting provisions is to accept third-party reports of sexual abuse and sexual harassment.
  

A third-party reporter is someone who reports sexual abuse and sexual harassment but is neither the victim nor the abuser. The person may have been told by the victim about the abuse or harassment, or witnessed it first-hand. “Third-party” includes other inmates, staff members, family members, legal representatives, contract employees, service providers, community or religious volunteers, among others.
  

The Anaheim Police Department investigates all in-custody allegations of sexual abuse. What can I do if I suspect that my loved one is being subjected to harassment, pressured to have sexual relations, or is a victim of abuse or assault?
  

  * Immediately contact the on-duty Supervisor at the Anaheim Police Department Detention Facility at (714) 765-1826 or the Anaheim Police Department at (714) 765-1900
  * Encourage your loved one to report the incident and/or find support


The Anaheim Police Department has a Zero-Tolerance for all forms of sexual misconduct and is dedicated to providing a safe and secure environment for detained individuals and personnel.
  

> **PREA Facility Coordinator**  
>  Lt. Matt Adrian   
> Detention Facility Commander  
> madrian@anaheim.net  
> 425 South Harbor Boulevard  
> Anaheim Ca. 92805

  1. ####  [Who's In Jail](https://www.anaheim.net/2177/Whos-In-Jail)
Who's In Jail will allow you to search for a person currently housed in the Anaheim Detention Facility, or you can call (714) 765-1988. For inmate information for the Orange County Jail, call (714) 647-4666. 
  2. ####  [Arrest Logs](https://local.anaheim.net/images/articles/4119/Arrest_Log.xml)
Logs of the persons who have been recently arrested.
  3. ####  [Work Release](https://www.anaheim.net/255/Work-Release)
The Anaheim Detention Facility offers a work release program for individuals eligible to serve their court commitment via an alternative sentencing program. Program participants are housed in individual sleeping quarters and have minimum contact with all other non-sentenced inmates.

#  How-To Guides 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [How to Get Started – California Friendly Garden ](http://www.bewaterwise.com/conservation_Tips.shtml)
Wondering how to get started to make your garden more California Friendly® ? You don’t have to do a major renovation. Here are some ideas that will save water, improve plant health, and reduce run-off – all California Friendly® .
  2. ####  [How to Select California Friendly Plants Video ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eYPqqj-tc7s)
Dr. Darren Haver explains how to select beautiful California-friendly, drought-tolerant plants for your landscape.
  3. ####  [How to Adjust Your Sprinklers at Home Video ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TbKB0yOLGf4)
Learn how to save water and eliminate runoff by adjusting your sprinklers at home.
  4. ####  [How to Eliminate Runoff from Your Sprinklers Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DKiI8M3OGkc)
Learn how to save water and eliminate runoff by adjusting your sprinklers at home.
  5. ####  [How to Choose Weather Based Irrigation Controller](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Smart_Controller_Brochure_2014.pdf)
A smart sprinkler controller automatically adjusts irrigation schedules in response to changing weather conditions. It monitors information such as rain, wind, temperature, humidity, soil moisture, the amount of time it takes for water to evaporate and plant type to calculate the amount of water needed to keep plants healthy.
  6. ####  [How to Choose Water-Efficient Sprinkler Nozzles](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Rotating_Nozzles_Brochure_2014.pdf)
The latest innovation in sprinkler technology are nozzles that turn a standard pop-up spray head into a precision device that can save water in your landscape. In addition, water jetting from these nozzles is more resistant to wind, less likely to mist, and significantly reduces run-off onto streets and sidewalks. 
  7. ####  [How to Choose a Water-Efficient Clothes Washer](http://www.bewaterwise.com/pdfs/Choosing_Washer_Brochure_2014.pdf)
A simple rule of thumb is to look for a front-loading washer instead of a typical top-loading one. frontloading washers use about half of the water to wash a full load of laundry, compared to traditional top loaders. 
  8. ####  [How to Save Water Outdoors and Indoors ](http://www.bewaterwise.com/tips.shtml)
Up to 70% of your annual water use is outside your home … watering your lawn and garden, and keeping your property clean. Landscaping with native and drought-tolerant plants, and following these simple tips will help you use water more efficiently and save money on your water bill.

#  ESS - Employee Self Service 
ESS allows City of Anaheim Employees to view or manage their personal, benefits and payroll information.
These are some of the common activities performed in ESS:
  * Entering Timesheets
  * Viewing Benefits Coverage
  * Viewing Paystub and W2 information
  * Requesting Address and Emergency Contact Updates


  * [ Login  
](https://cityofanaheim-erp.hostams.com/PRDESS1X1/Advantage4)


ESS Availability: **Available**
  1. [First Time Logging Into ESS?](https://www.anaheim.net/2578/ESS---Employee-Self-Service#tab929bbd0d-5b6e-44bb-b3ef-a69f878041ae_0)


## First Time User ID - User Name and Password
Your user ID is your Human Resources Employee ID located at the top of your paystub.
Your password includes the characters 'Ana-' followed by your birthday (MMDDYY) and the last four digits of your Social Security Number (SSN).
User Name and Password are case sensitive.
**Example  
**  
---  
Employee Information  
Employee ID = 999999  
Birthday = February 3, 1967  
SSN = 555-55-5555 | ESS User ID and Password  
ESS User ID = 999999  
ESS Password = Ana-0203675555  
  

_For security reasons, the system will prompt you to change your password the first time you log in._ _After successfully logging in, be sure to set up your _**Password Hints**_ , which will allow you to reset your password yourself. Please see the **Change your Password** guide at myinfo.anaheim.net for password change instructions. _
For assistance with your User ID or Password, please contact the Help Desk at 714-765-5104.
**Help Desk will be available from Monday to Friday between 7am to 6pm.**
  1. [Password Management](https://www.anaheim.net/2578/ESS---Employee-Self-Service#tab929bbd0d-5b6e-44bb-b3ef-a69f878041ae_1)


The **Password Management** tab allows you to change your Advantage password.  
Enter the current password in the _Current Password_ field and then enter the new password in the _New Password_ and _Confirm Password_ fields. Select **Save** to save the new password.
Password must be at least 8 characters long with at least 1 of the following: number, capital letter and a special symbol (i.e. $, %, ‐. @)
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=50863)
  

  1. [How To?](https://www.anaheim.net/2578/ESS---Employee-Self-Service#tab929bbd0d-5b6e-44bb-b3ef-a69f878041ae_2)

  * [Adv 4 ERP Basic Navigation and Employee Business Role](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50862/Adv-4-ERP-Basic-Navigation-and-Employee-Business-Role)


For technical issues, contact the Help Desk at 714-765-5104. For all other issues please send an email to the ESS Administrators at **Benefits@anaheim.net**.

  5. Records


#  Records 
The Records Bureau provides a vital service to the community. Utilizing a sophisticated records management system, we are responsible for collecting, maintaining, and distributing all police reports and related records. The Records Bureau can be reached 7 days a week between 6:00 a.m. and 6:30 p.m. at [(714) 765-1990](tel:%207147651990).  

9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [Alarm Permits](https://www.anaheim.net/260/Alarm-Permits)
The Anaheim Police Department has a Burglary Alarm Permit, which is required for any alarm installation in Anaheim, that provides valuable information to Police Dispatchers that can be relayed to responding Officers.
  2. ####  [Bicycle Registration](https://www.anaheim.net/3501/Bicycle-Registration)
Bicycle Registration is provided by the Anaheim Police Department for City of Anaheim residents only. Your bicycle information is stored in our local database, and then accessible in the event your bicycle is lost or stolen. 
  3. ####  [Calls for Service](https://www.anaheim.net/2668/Calls-for-Service)
The Anaheim Police Department maintains a log of Calls for Service at the front counter of the police department for seven (7) days. The department also participates in a county wide program through the Orange County Sheriff’s Department to display daily calls for service in Anaheim.
  4. ####  [Clearance Letter](https://www.anaheim.net/5918/Clearance-Letter)
California Penal Code Section 13300(c)(8) enables a person to request a record clearance or “Letter of Good Conduct” for VISA, foreign travel, immigration and foreign adoption purposes. 
  5. ####  [File a Police Report](https://pd.anaheim.net/260/File-a-Police-Report)
The Anaheim Police Department Reporting System allows you to submit a report immediately for a variety incidents and print a copy of the police report for free.
  6. ####  [Local Criminal History](https://www.anaheim.net/5917/Local-Criminal-History)
Access to criminal history summary records, maintained by the Department of Justice, is restricted by law to legitimate law enforcement purposes and authorized applicant agencies. However, individuals have the right to request a copy of their own criminal history record from the Department to review for accuracy and completeness. 
  7. ####  [Public Records Request - Calls for Service](https://www.anaheim.net/2680/Public-Records-Request---Calls-for-Servi)
Requests may be submitted for a call for service at a specific location, a location over a time frame or types of calls/incidents.
  8. ####  [Request a Copy of a Police Report](https://www.anaheim.net/2499/Request-a-Copy-of-a-Police-Report)
Police report requests submitted to the Anaheim Police Department will be processed according to the California Public Records Act (CPRA) and many may be exempt from disclosure. Please be advised that not all requests for reports will be granted.
  9. ####  [Traffic Accident Reports](https://www.anaheim.net/259/Traffic-Accident-Reports)
The Anaheim Police Department Accident Report Database allows you to search for any non-injury accident report. All reports take a minimum of 10 working days from the date of the accident to become available online.


  1. # Contact Us

425 S. Harbor Boulevard  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
Phone: [(714) 765-1990](tel:7147651990)
### **Hours**
Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM-5:00 PM  
Closed on all City Holidays, Saturday, and Sunday

#  Traffic Accident Reports 
The Anaheim Police Department Accident Report Database allows you to search for certain traffic related reports, such as non-injury accidents/collisions and hit-and-runs. For non-injury traffic accidents or hit-and-runs that were filed using the [online reporting system](https://www.anaheim.net/1643/File-a-Police-Report), cases may take additional time to review before becoming available online. If there are additional criminal offenses associated with a traffic report, these cases may not be immediately available. 
To locate a report you must select “California” for State, “Anaheim Police Department” for Jurisdiction, and enter a report number only.  

[Search for traffic accident report ](https://policereports.lexisnexis.com/)

  6. Home & Business Security Inspections


#  Home & Business Security Inspections 
The Anaheim Police Department offers FREE Security Inspections to Residents and Businesses in the City of Anaheim. The inspection will include a review of your home or business existing locks, lighting, landscaping and identify any problematic areas where your home or business is unprotected. The inspection typically takes approximately 20-30 minutes. The goal of the Police Department is to enhance your personal protection and safety.

#  Convention, Sports & Entertainment 
## Anaheim Convention Center
  
  
The largest convention center on the West Coast, Anaheim Convention Center opened in 1967 and is owned and operated by the City of Anaheim. Located in the center of The Anaheim Resort, the Anaheim Convention Center will soon offer more than 1 million square feet of event, mixed use, ballroom and outdoor event space.   
  
The spectacular glass walls soar 190 feet and welcome more than 1 million visitors every year. The Convention Center hosts approximately 300 events each year, consisting of trade shows, conventions, corporate events, consumer shows, meetings, social functions and banquets. 
[![Anaheim Convention Center ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=5628) ](https://www.anaheim.net/1117/Convention-Sports-Entertainment)
800 W Katella Ave.   
Anaheim, CA 92802   
[Click Here](https://www.anaheim.net/1117/Convention-Sports-Entertainment) for Details.
Angel Stadium of Anaheim  
**  
** In 1996, the City of Anaheim entered into a long-term lease with Angels Baseball to manage the 45,000 seat Angel Stadium of Anaheim. This world class venue is home to the 2002 MLB World Champion Angels and 2014 American League West Division Champions.   
  
Angel Stadium of Anaheim hosts other events such as: Supercross, Monster Trucks, concerts, CIF football and Home Shows.   

[![Angel Stadium of Anaheim Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6880)](http://www.angelsbaseball.com%20)
2000 Gene Autry Way  
Anaheim, CA 92806   
[www.angelsbaseball.com](http://www.angelsbaseball.com%20) **  
**
ARTIC   
  
ARTIC is an iconic transportation facility serving the three million Orange County residents as well as more than 40 million visitors annually. Transportation services offered at ARTIC include: Amtrak Metrolink; Greyhound, Megabus and OCTA bus services; Anaheim Resort Transportation; taxis; bike lockers and racks; and auto and carpool/vanpool parking. 
[![ARTIC Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6876)](https://www.ocvibe.com/artic)
2626 E. Katella Ave.  
Anaheim, CA 92806  
[www.articanaheim.com ](https://www.ocvibe.com/artic/about-artic/)  
  

City National Grove of Anaheim   
  
In 2002, the City of Anaheim purchased the then Grove of Anaheim and entered into a multi-year agreement with Nederlander-Grove, LLC to manage the venue on behalf of the City of Anaheim.   
  
City National Grove of Anaheim provides ultimate live music and entertainment with a limited seating capacity, which ensures that every seat is desirable. City National Grove is also the only live music venue that can offer high quality sit-down dining experience. In addition to concerts, the Grove welcomes corporate and private meetings, weddings, formals, private meetings and large banquets. 
[![Grove of Anaheim Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6882)](http://www.citynationalgroveofanaheim.com)
2200 E. Katella Ave.   
Anaheim, CA 92806   
[www.citynationalgroveofanaheim.com](http://www.citynationalgroveofanaheim.com)
Honda Center  
  
Honda Center opened in 1993 and is home to the National Hockey League's Anaheim Ducks. In 2003, the City of Anaheim entered into a long-term lease with Anaheim Arena Management for the operation of the Honda Center. In 2005, Honda Center was ranked third in the world for concert bookings by Billboard magazine. Honda Center continues to be one of the premier entertainment venues in the United States and has hosted many top acts such as U2, the Eagles, Paul McCartney, Elton John, Gwen Stefani and Jimmy Buffet. 
[![honda center Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=6881)](http://www.hondacenter.com)
2695 E. Katella Ave.   
Anaheim, CA 92806   
[www.hondacenter.com](http://www.hondacenter.com)

  6. Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting


#  Dusk-to-Dawn Lighting - Commercial 
Commercial customers can receive free high-efficiency LED fixtures:  
  

  * Wall Fixtures 
  * Pole Mountable Parking Lot Lights
  * Purchase your own lights and receive a rebate under the [Lighting Incentives Program](http://www.anaheim.net/961/Lighting-Incentives-Program "Lighting Incentives Program")


These lights with photo sensors will automatically turn lights on at dusk and off at dawn. LED light fixtures have a longer life span and higher efficiency than previous bulb types.   
  
For large facilities, the long life span of the LEDs can significantly reduce maintenance costs. The high-efficiency bulbs also use over 70 percent less energy than standard fixtures, while providing a similar level of light output.
![comm d2d light](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23038)
![Dusk to Dawn Installation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66249)
![Outdoor dusk to dawn](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27555)
![Dusk-to-dawn light](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=27556)
![Dusk to Dawn Commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66252)
[](https://www.anaheim.net/2628/Dusk-to-Dawn-Lighting) [](https://www.anaheim.net/2628/Dusk-to-Dawn-Lighting)
###  **How the Program Works**  
  

  1. Contact us at 714-765-4258 to schedule an appointment.
  2. We will perform a customized lighting survey to determine the types, quantity, and placement of fixtures needed at your site. Lighting needs will determine how many fixtures will be given. (Up to 10 fixtures per customer.)
  3. When fixtures are received, you will be asked to sign an agreement noting the terms of the program. (See program criteria.)
  4. You will have 30 days to install your lights. We recommend using a licensed electrician.
  5. Approximately 30 days after receiving your lights, call 714-765-4258 to confirm installation.
  6. Installations are subject to an inspection.


  

###  **Program Criteria**  
  

  * Participants must be Anaheim Public Utilities electric customers with current and non-delinquent accounts.
  * Lights must be new installations for multi-family, commercial, industrial, or institutional customers.
  * Lights must be installed within the 30-day period.
  * Lights must be on all hours of darkness as regulated by the photocell, and must be maintained and used for five years from installation.
  * Participant must be the owner or legal representative of the property receiving the light fixture.
  * Customers must permit inspection of the installed fixture(s) by an Anaheim Public Utilities representative.


  

###  **Providing Your Own Lights?**  
  

Visit our [Lighting Incentives](http://www.anaheim.net/961/Lighting-Incentives-Program "Lighting Incentives") page to see if you qualify for a rebate.
For more information, please call 714-765-4258.  
  
Program is subject to available funding. Up to 10 fixtures per customer.

#  Dog Walker Watch 
The Anaheim Police Department invites dog walkers to attend a crime awareness program at a local park that encourages dog walkers throughout our community to assist as “extra eyes and ears.” Some of the information discussed at these meetings is personal safety information/precautions to be aware of while walking your pets in and around your neighborhood, observing and reporting suspicious activity and the importance of having a partnership between the community and Police Department. 
If you would like more information on how to get involved and start a Dog Walker Watch, please contact the Crime Prevention Specialist for your area.

  6. Coffee with a Cop


#  Coffee with a Cop 
This is a great opportunity for residents to talk and get to know Anaheim Police Officers in a casual setting where all questions, concerns and comments are welcome. Meetings are typically held at various locations throughout the City and give residents an opportunity to build a relationship with officers that work, and have knowledge of what goes on, in the area. Your participation is very important as we work together to make our neighborhoods safe and crime free. 
If you would like more information on how to get involved or host a Coffee with a Cop, please contact the Crime Prevention Specialist for your area.

  6. Neighborhood Watch


#  Neighborhood Watch 
Neighborhood Watch is a great way for neighbors to get to know each other and work together, get trained to recognize and report suspicious activities in your neighborhood, implement crime prevention techniques such as home security and security through environmental design and enlist the active participation of citizens in cooperation with law enforcement to reduce crime in your community. 
If you would like more information on how to get involved and start a Neighborhood Watch, please contact the Crime Prevention Specialist for your area. 
# South & West District
  

### Carmela Mendoza
  
**Crime Prevention Specialist**  
Phone: [(714) 765-7962](tel:7147657962)  
Email: CMendoza@anaheim.net  
  

# Central & East District
  

###  Rocio Burress 
  
**Crime Prevention Specialist**  
Phone: [(714) 765-1918](tel:7147651918)  
Email: RBurress@anaheim.net

#  Public Records Request - Calls for Service 
Requests may be submitted for a call for service at a specific location, a location over a time frame or types of calls/incidents.   
  

  * Request for incident history will be reviewed for the type of incident and release of information.
  * Calls for service will be prepared for a location / address and time frame.
  * The requester will be contacted with the amount due for copy fees which are .75 cents for the first page and .20 cents for each additional page.
  * All requests are processed in 7 to 10 days.


[Click here to submit a request](https://cityofanaheimcapd.nextrequest.com/)
![QR Code for PD Records Request](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=52717)
  

  1. # Contact Us
### **Records Bureau**
425 S. Harbor Boulevard  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
Phone: [(714) 765-1990](tel:7147651990)
### **Hours**
Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM-5:00 PM  
Closed on all City Holidays, Saturday, and Sunday

##    
  
Downtown Anaheim  
Sunday, Dec. 7, 2025  
  
Nutcracker Holiday Village  
noon  
  
Tree Lighting  
6 p.m.
#   

  

_Corner of Clementine Street and Center Street Promenade. Ring in the holidays with a very special tree lighting celebration. Join the Nutcracker Holiday Committee, the City of Anaheim, the Downtown Anaheim Association, and the Anaheim Community Foundation for holiday entertainment food, and fun! Guests will enjoy a variety of holiday happenings, including dog costume contest, holiday crafts, unique shopping, pictures with Santa, and the tree lighting at the steps of City Hall._
[![community services interior logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42964)](https://www.anaheim.net/6094)

  5. [Retired Senior Volunteer Patrol](https://www.anaheim.net/265/Retired-Senior-Volunteer-Patrol)
  6. Vacation House Check


#  Vacation House Check 
As an Anaheim resident, you have the advantage of a Retired Senior Volunteer Patrol program (RSVP) in your city. One of the many services RSVPs offer Anaheim residents is vacation checks. If you are going on vacation, notify the Anaheim Police RSVP office to sign up for a vacation check. Trained volunteers will perform a thorough inspection of your property while you are away. Vacation checks include a perimeter check, physical and/or visual check of all doors and windows, verification of vehicles left on the property and verification of any people found on the property. Exterior areas of your home that are not accessible will not be checked. If something appears suspicious, the RSVPs will call for the assistance of a police officer. 
To take advantage of this free service, complete a [Vacation House Check Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/Home/View/6157) and return it to:   
  

> _Anaheim Police Department  
>  Attention: RSVP  
>  425 S. Harbor Boulevard  
>  Anaheim, CA 92805_
  
Or contact the RSVP Helpdesk at [(714) 765-1987](tel:7147651987) or RSVP@anaheim.net.

  5. Form 700 Statement of Economic Interests


#  Statement of Economic Interests - Form 700 
The Political Reform Act ("PRA"), also known as the Conflict of Interest Code, requires designated public officials to file a Statement of Economic Interests ("SEI"), Form 700 (aka Conflict of Interest Statement). The purpose is to disclose assets and income which may be materially affected by their official actions.
  

The City Clerk reviews its Conflict of Interest Code every two years, as required by the PRA. There are generally three types of statements filed on Form 700: 1) Assuming Office, which is filed when a new employee fills a designated position; 2) Leaving Office, which is filed when an employee leaves a designated position; and 3) Annual statement, which is filed by continuing designated employees and must be filed no later that April 1st of each year.
  

[E-file your Form 700](https://www.netfile.com/Filer/Authentication/LogIn "Netfile")(Statement of Economic Interest)
### Forms
  * [](http://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/4253)[Form 700 (2025/26 Printable Version)](http://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/14247)

For Live Assistance contact the FPPC at 866-ASK-FPPC or the Office of the City Clerk at 714-765-5166.
