#  Voter Information 
  * [**Vote Center & Ballot Box Locations throughout Anaheim**](https://www.anaheim.net/6734/Vote-Center-and-Ballot-Box-Locations-thr)


* * *
  1. [Eligibility](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_0)
  2. [Registration Options](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_1)
  3. [Vote Centers](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_2)


  1. [Eligibility](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_0)


Eligibility Requirements to Register and Vote   
A person must be:
  * A United States citizen and a resident of California
  * At least 18 years of age (or will be by the date of the upcoming election)
  * Not currently in state or federal prison or on parole for the conviction of a felony
  * Not currently found mentally incompetent to vote by a court


  1. [Registration Options](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_1)


Voter Registration Options
  * Online: Visit the [California Online Voter Registration](http://registertovote.ca.gov/) website and complete the online application. To register online you will need:  
- Your California driver license or California identification card number  
- The last four (4) digits of your Social Security number  
- Your date of birth  
  

  * By mail: Obtain a registration form from the City Clerk’s office, post office, libraries, Orange County Registrar of Voters, or DMV. It is important that your voter registration application be filled out completely and be postmarked or hand-delivered to the Orange County Registrar of Voters at least 15 days before the election. Complete, sign, and mail the original form to the following address:  
Orange County Registrar of Voters   
P.O. Box 11298  
Santa Ana, CA 92711  
  

  * In Person: Visit the Orange County Registrar of Voters Office:  
1300 South Grand Avenue, Building C  
Santa Ana, CA 92705

  

  1. [Vote Centers](https://www.anaheim.net/2922/Voter-Information#tabf2b301ab-35e1-4add-92b7-4f296445897d_2)


**Vote Centers** mean more voting options for voters - vote centers will be open for voting and registration for up to 4 days prior to election day and voters will have the opportunity to cast their ballot at any vote center throughout Orange County. Also, with this new voting model, all voters will receive a vote-by-mail ballot in the mail prior to the election. [Learn more](http://www.anaheim.net/5414/Orange-County-Vote-Centers)
[![Register to Vote Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34398)](https://ocvote.gov/registration/register-to-vote)
[![Interactive District Map Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34260)](https://www.anaheim.net/6243/Interactive-District-Map)
[![Ballot Return Options Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34400)](https://ocvote.gov/voting/voting-at-home)
[![In Person Voting Options Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34399)](https://ocvote.gov/voting/your-in-person-voting-options)
For additional information regarding registration and voting visit the [Orange County Registrar of Voters](https://ocvote.gov/) and the [California Secretary of State](https://www.sos.ca.gov/elections/) websites.

#  Districts 
In order to best serve our community, the Anaheim Police Department divides the city into four police districts: West, Central, South, and East. Based on where you live or work in the city, you have representatives assigned to assist you. As an example, if you are experiencing long-term problems in your neighborhood, your Crime Prevention Specialist can assist in working with you to remedy the issue.  
  
Please use the map below to determine what District you live or work in and who to contact:  
  

  1. Search for your address in the search box.
  2. As the maps re-positions to show a black dot on your address then click in the colored area around the dot to see the Crime Prevention Specialist responsible for your area.


# South & West District

#  Outreach Events 
Anaheim Public Utilities has a presence at over 40 community events a year, ranging from resource fairs to community festivals. We value the opportunity to visit our customers, provide information on our many programs, and answer questions. For more details on events near you, call 714-765-4250.
Don’t forget to follow our Anaheim Public Utilities [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/anaheimutilities) and [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/anaheimutilities/) pages to stay up to date on upcoming community events and special promotions.
![](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53943)
> Anaheim Public Utilities promotional items, contests, and opportunity drawings are open to Anaheim residents only (proof of residency may be required).
> City of Anaheim employees and their immediate family are not eligible to participate.
  

[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)
  1. [Public Power Week](https://www.anaheim.net/6249/Public-Power-Week)
  2. [Water Awareness Month](https://www.anaheim.net/6557/Water-Awareness-Month)

#  Public Records Request Archive 
Public records requests are governed by state law and are available to the citizenry to access their government. In an effort to increase the transparency of City government, below is a table of those certain Public Records Act requests received and processed by the City Clerk's Office. This list is not intended to be inclusive of all requests received by the city under the Public Records Act. _*columns are sortable_

#  Candidate Resources 
The next General Municipal Election will be held on **Tuesday, November 3, 2026**. The election will be for the Mayor (At-large) and three (3) Council Member District Seats (Districts 2, 3, and 6), each for a term of four (4) years.
Individuals interested in running for City Council are encouraged to contact the City Clerk at (714) 765-5166 or via email at tbass@anaheim.net.
* * *
  

  1. [Eligibility](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_0)
  2. [Campaign Contribution Limit](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_1)
  3. [Candidate Handbook](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_2)


  1. [Eligibility](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_0)


## Eligibility to Run for Anaheim City Council
  
  

  * A citizen of the United States
  * At least 18 years of age
  * Mayor: A resident and qualified elector of the City of Anaheim, at least 30 days immediately preceding the filing of nomination papers
  * Council Member: A resident and qualified elector of the respective district, at least 30 days immediately preceding the filing of nomination papers


  1. [Campaign Contribution Limit](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_1)


## Campaign Contribution Limit
## **  
**
**  
**Candidates or candidate controlled committees shall not accept any contributions which will cause the total amount received by such person to exceed the contribution limit. Effective January 1, 2025, the campaign contribution limit is $2,700. ([AMC 1.09.050](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-53266))
[learn more](https://www.anaheim.net/4569/Campaign-Contribution-Limits)
  1. [Candidate Handbook](https://www.anaheim.net/2915/Candidate-Resources#tab27373036-dceb-4aef-b80e-ded9652673c0_2)


## Candidate Handbook
  
  
The [Candidate Handbook](http://www.anaheim.net/3396/Candidate-Handbook) (Handbook) is prepared for Anaheim residents wishing to become a candidate for the office of Council Member and can be used as a general guide to assist in facilitating the election process. The purpose of the Handbook is to provide general information, and is not intended to provide legal advice and should not be used as a substitute for legal counsel; it does not necessarily include all provisions which may affect candidates and does not have the force and effect of law, regulation, or rule.  
[learn more](http://www.anaheim.net/3396/Candidate-Handbook)

#  Election Calendar 
## General Municipal Election Calendar, November 3, 2026
The next General Municipal Election will be held on **Tuesday, November 3, 2026**. The election will be for the Mayor (At-large) and three (3) Council Member District Seats (Districts 2, 3, and 6), each for a term of four (4) years.
## FPPC Filing Deadlines
**DATE** | **EVENT/REQUIREMENT**  
---|---  
July 31, 2026 |  **SEMI-ANNUAL STATEMENT DUE (Form 460)**  
(Period: January 1 through June 30, 2026)  
Within 24 Hours |  **CONTRIBUTION REPORTS (Form 496/Form 497)**  
(contribution/expenditure of $1,000 or more, aggregate)  
(Period: August 5 through November 3, 2026)  
September 24, 2026 |  **1ST PRE-ELECTION STATEMENT DUE (Form 460)**  
(Period: July 1 through September 19, 2026)  
October 22, 2026 |  **2ND PRE-ELECTION STATEMENT DUE (Form 460)**  
(Period: September 20 through October 17, 2026)  
February 1, 2026 |  **SEMI-ANNUAL STATEMENT DUE (Form 460)**  
(Period: October 18 through December 31, 2026)  
## General Municipal Election Calendar, November 3, 2026
**DATE** | **EVENT/REQUIREMENT**  
---|---  
July 13, 2026 - August 7, 2026 |  **NOMINATION PERIOD**  
Between these dates, candidates may obtain nomination materials and file documents with the City Clerk (Monday – Friday, 8 a.m. – 5 p.m., **by appointment**).  
August 10, 2026 - August 12, 2026* |  **EXTENDED CANDIDATE FILING PERIOD**  
*Filing is extended if an incumbent eligible for re-election **does not** file for re-election prior to 5:00 p.m. on August 7, 2026. Incumbents are not eligible to file during the extended period.  
August 13, 2026 |  **RANDOMIZED ALPHABET DRAWING**  
Secretary of State to conduct a random alpha draw to determine the order of candidate's name on the ballot.  
August 8, 2026 - August 17, 2026 |  **PUBLIC EXAMINATION PERIOD**  
Public review period for the candidate statements and ballot designations.  
*(Examination period extended to August 24, 2026 only for a candidate seat if it has a nomination filing period extended through August 12, 2026.)  
September 7, 2026 - October 20, 2026 | **FILING PERIOD FOR WRITE-IN CANDIDATES**  
October 19, 2026* |  **15-DAY VOTER REGISTRATION DEADLINE FOR THE NOVEMBER 2026 ELECTION**  
*Conditional voter registration extends the 15-day registration deadline, allowing voters to register and vote provisionally 14 days prior to an election and on Election Day.  
November 3, 2026 | **ELECTION DAY**  
December 2026 |  **CERTIFICATION OF RESULTS**  
Certification of election results and installation of newly elected officials  
(Council meeting date to be determined).

#  Energy & Water Saving House 
The interactive Energy & Water Saving House shows you how you can lower your utility bills by installing efficiency measures, and provides links to many electric and water rebates available.
![Cam3_sm_11-03](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=11166)
Exterior
[![Image of rain barrel](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_01sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_01.jpg "Every time it rains, be prepared with a rain barrel, and capture the water to reuse later for your plants.  Rain barrels are eligible for a rebate.  \(Minimum size: 50 gallons\) <a href='https://anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of drip system](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_02sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_02.jpg "Drip irrigation is a good system to water a small yard, flower garden, shrubs, and hard-to-water areas. Using a drip system will minimize water loss through evaporation or runoff. Using a drip system in mulched areas is beneficial because it can directly wet the soil and prevent mulch from washing away. Drip systems use 20 to 50 percent less water than traditional pop-up sprinklers, and depending on the area, can save up to 30,000 gallons of water per year.") [![Image of rotating nozzles](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_03sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_03.jpg "Rotating nozzles apply water more slowly to help reduce runoff and encourage healthy plant growth.  Rotating nozzles can reduce water use by up to 30% than that of traditional spray heads.  Rotating nozzles are eligible for a rebate \(minimum of 30 nozzles\). <a href='/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of mulch around trees](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_04sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_04.jpg "Mulch is a protective ground cover that saves water, reduces evaporation, prevents erosion, and controls weeds.  Placing two to three inches of mulch around trees and shrubs can save up to 100 gallons per day.") [![Image of turf](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_05sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_05.jpg "Turf \(grass\) is one of the most water-intensive plants in your landscape.  Replacing grass with California Friendly plants or artificial turf reduces daily watering and can save 44 gallons per square foot of turf removed.  For a limited time, Anaheim Public Utilities is offering a rebate of $1 per square foot, subject to available funding. <a href='https://anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of garden hose](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_06sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_06.jpg "Using an unrestricted garden hose can waste water at over 12 gallons per minute.  Attaching a shut-off hose nozzle will allow you to water more efficiently and eliminate water waste.  Most shut-off nozzles have adjustable spray settings that can range from full force to mist.  Contact Anaheim Public Utilities to sign up for a Home Utility Check-Up and get one for free. <a href='/475/Home-Utility-Check-up '>For more information</a>") [![Image of soil moisture sensors](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_07sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_07.jpg "Soil moisture sensors are placed below ground in the root zones of lawns and landscapes to determine if --and how long-- to water.  Sensor-based products are available as stand-alone controllers or add-on devices to existing controllers. They can reduce outdoor water use by up to 70% without sacrificing the quality or health of your landscape.  Soil moisture sensor systems are eligible for a rebate. <a href='https://anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient air conditioning](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_08sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_08.jpg "Switching to an energy-efficient central air conditioning and heating system can save you between 20-50 percent, depending on your upgrade.  <a href='https://anaheim.net/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient pool pump](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_09sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_09.jpg "There are a variety of ways to save money on your pool: By upgrading to an energy-efficient pool pump, turning your heat down a few degrees or when not in season or in use, and using pool covers to save energy. <a href='t/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of solar panels](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_10sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_EXT_10.png "Adding solar panels is an excellent way to reduce energy use and costs inside of your home. Use solar panels to gather and store energy from the sun to be converted into electricity for the rest of your home.") ![Exterior](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/Exterior.png)   

Interior
[![Image of high-efficiency washer](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_01sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_01.jpg "High-efficiency clothes washers use 55 percent less water than standard clothes washers.  Less water means less energy needed for water heating and lowering your energy bill, too.  The potential water savings is 15 gallons per day or more than 75,000 gallons over the washer’s lifetime.  High-efficiency clothes washers are eligible for a rebate. <a href='https://anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of high-efficiency toilets](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_02sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_02.jpg "High-efficiency toilets use 20 percent less water than standard toilets. In just one year, you can save more than 8,000 gallons of water.  For a limited time, high-efficiency toilets are eligible for a rebate. <a href='https://anaheim.net/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of toilet tank bag](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_03sm.png) ](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_03.jpg "The toilet tank bag saves 0.8 gallons per flush. The bag is easy to fill and has a patented air lock and fill valve. You fill the bag with water to the top, snap to close, and hang in the toilet tank. The tank bag has an anti-evaporative airlock so the bag will never need to be refilled and odors can be prevented. <a href='t/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of low-flow showerhead](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_05sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_05.jpg "Low-flow showerheads use no more than two gallons per minute. They are inexpensive, easy to install, save water, and reduce energy needed for water heating.  The average family could save up to 2,900 gallons per year by installing a low-flow showerhead. Contact Anaheim Public Utilities to sign up for a Home Utility Check-Up and have it installed for free. ") [![Image of shower timer](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_06sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_06.jpg "Having a shower timer saves water and energy by providing a way to monitor and reduce showering time.  Most timers are set for five minutes.  Cutting back showering time to five minutes can save up to 18 gallons per day with a low-flow showerhead.") [![Image of aerator](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07.jpg "Aerators limit the flow of water through the faucet.  By installing aerators on your bathroom and kitchen faucets, you can save 4.7 and 10 gallons per day, respectively.  Installing an aerator reduces water use and requires less energy to heat water when needed. Contact Anaheim Public Utilities to sign up for a Home Utility Check-Up and have them installed for free.") [![Image of aerator](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07asm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07.jpg "Aerators limit the flow of water through the faucet.  By installing aerators on your bathroom and kitchen faucets, you can save 4.7 and 10 gallons per day, respectively.  Installing an aerator reduces water use and requires less energy to heat water when needed. Contact Anaheim Public Utilities to sign up for a Home Utility Check-Up and get them installed for free.") [![Image of aerator](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07bsm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_07.jpg "Aerators limit the flow of water through the faucet.  By installing aerators on your bathroom and kitchen faucets, you can save 4.7 and 10 gallons per day, respectively.  Installing an aerator reduces water use and requires less energy to heat water when needed. Contact Anaheim Public Utilities to sign up for a Home Utility Check-Up and get them installed for free.") [![Image of irrigation controller](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_08sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_08.jpg "Weather-based irrigation controllers automatically adjust the watering based on current weather conditions and can reduce unnecessary watering and save approximately 13,000 gallons per year.  Weather-based irrigation controllers are eligible for a rebate. <a href='/945/Water-Rebates-Incentives'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient refrigerator](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_09sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_09.jpg "Replace your old refrigerator with a new, energy-efficient one.  Learn about rebates and recycling programs. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient dishwasher](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_10sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_10.jpg "Get an ENERGY STAR© rated dishwasher and save water with every wash.  Also, wait until you have a full load of dishes and run your dishwasher during off-peak hours to save money. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient air conditioning window unit](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_11sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_11.jpg "Replace your air conditioning window unit with an energy-efficient model. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of energy-efficient window](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_12sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_12.jpg "Upgrading to energy-efficient windows and doors can put technology to work for you.  Find out whether small improvements can make a difference or if replacing windows is your best option:  Anaheim.net/SaveEnergy. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of piping](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_13sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_13.jpg) [![Image of ceiling fan](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_14sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_14.jpg "Ceiling fans can cool you by about five degrees, allowing you to give your air conditioner a bit of a break.  Anaheim Public Utilities offers rebates. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of attic fan](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_15sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_15.jpg "Attic fans are an important part of home cooling -- learn about available rebates and incentives!. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of whole house fan](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_16sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_16.jpg "Whole house fans help cool the house at lower rates than air conditioning. <a href='/1467/Home-Incentives-Program-Rebates'>For more information</a>") [![Image of furnace](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_17sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_17new.jpg "Most homes use furnaces for their heat source.  Upgrading or retrofitting your furnace can be a good way to save money during the winter.") [![Image of refrigerator](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_19sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_19.png "Often times it costs more money for you to operate an old appliance than it does for you to invest in a new ENERGY STAR© appliance. Recycle your refrigerator/freezer and receive a $50 rebate. <a href='/937/'>For more information</a>") [![Image of Electric Vehicle chargining station](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_20sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_20.png "A home electric vehicle \(EV\) charging station doesn’t require as much electricity as you might have thought. Charging at off-peak hours helps support the efficiency of the electricity grid and you may be eligible for a $500 rebate. <a href='/593/'>For more information</a>") [![Image of LED lightbulb](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_21sm.png)](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/CloseUp_21.png "Replace traditional lightbulbs in high-use fixtures with LED lights that use 70-90% less energy and last 10 to 25 times longer.") ![Interior](https://local.anaheim.net/Utilities/EnergySavings/Interior.png)

#  Experiencing an Outage? 
## Please call 911 to report a fire or medical emergency. DO NOT approach [downed power lines](https://www.anaheim.net/4754/Power-Outage-Safety).
**_View outage maps below for status on current outages._**
[![report an outage Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34455)](https://www.anaheim.net/2671/Anaheim-Anytime-Request?id=5804)
[![electric outage map](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34458)](https://www.anaheim.net/2829)
[![water outage map](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34457)](https://www.anaheim.net/3145)
[![sign up Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34456)](http://www.anaheimalert.net/)
## Why is my power off?
Power outages may occur due to a number of different causes, from regional, large-scale blackouts, to local problems on power lines serving your home or business. Some examples of power outage causes include:  

  * Non-payment
  * Cars hitting power poles
  * Mylar balloons tangled in power lines
  * Equipment issues
  * Extreme weather
  * Trees falling on overhead power lines
  * Transmission lines damaged from wildfires


## Why is my water off?
Water service may be disrupted by a variety of causes, including:  

  * Non-payment
  * Water main break
  * Service line leak
  * Equipment failure 
  * Planned shutdown for maintenance
  * Construction activities 
  * Water facilities damaged from natural disasters (earthquake, wildland fire, etc.) 
  * Water quality concerns


## When Power Goes Off
  
If one of these problems should occur, Anaheim’s local grid is designed to detect most outages and quickly restore power to as many customers as possible. Automated equipment and computer monitoring systems will inform utility operators of large outages, who will dispatch field staff to investigate the cause.  
  
Anaheim’s electric operations center is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, to dispatch a troubleshooter to respond quickly to power outages. However, if significant repairs are required after normal business hours, additional staffing for crews will require employees to report in, assemble materials, and make the necessary repairs.  
  
In the meantime, [here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/10532/Outage-Prep?bidId=) is some helpful information to ensure that you and your family are prepared.   
  
Anaheim’s staff is trained to work on high voltage electric systems to restore power in a safe manner to all customers. Please know that we are making every effort to restore your power as soon as possible.   
  
If you were less than satisfied with our response to a power outage, or if you have questions or suggestions that you would like considered by executive management, please [complete this form](http://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Share-with-the-General-Manager-48) to send a message to the Anaheim Public Utilities General Manager.  

[![Outage Alert Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37500)](https://member.everbridge.net/892807736725456/login)
### Contact Us
  1. 714-765-3300 or Email
## Call Center:
Mon - Thurs: 7 am - 6 pm  
Fri: 7 am - 5:30 pm
## In Person:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm

  6. Special Event Support


#  Special Event Support 
The City values and encourages events, programs, and services that benefit Anaheim residents. Historically, Anaheim has supported various community events either with in-kind support or financial support based on citywide community need and budgetary allowances. In order to ensure equal opportunity for organizations to submit their proposals for approval and obtain support, organizations will need to complete an application and follow certain guidelines.
  1. [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/3117/Special-Event-Support#tab4398f65c-da67-44e0-bbfd-efdd9c93cc49_1)
  2. [Additional Information](https://www.anaheim.net/3117/Special-Event-Support#tab4398f65c-da67-44e0-bbfd-efdd9c93cc49_2)


  1. [Application](https://www.anaheim.net/3117/Special-Event-Support#tab4398f65c-da67-44e0-bbfd-efdd9c93cc49_1)


**APPLICATION DUE DATES:  
  
Formal Sponsorship - 5PM on March 31, 2026 (postmarks will not be accepted)  
Informal Sponsorship - 60 Days prior to Date of Event   
**
  * [Online Application](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/Special-Event-Financial-Support-Applicat-104 "Special Event Support Online Form")
  * [Download Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/19159)  
Complete and return to: Anaheim Community Services Department  
Special Event Financial Support Request  
200 S. Anaheim Blvd., Suite 433  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
  



  1. [Additional Information](https://www.anaheim.net/3117/Special-Event-Support#tab4398f65c-da67-44e0-bbfd-efdd9c93cc49_2)


[Anaheim City Council Special Event Financial Support Policy PDF](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/10513)
[Special Event Support FAQ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/19234/Special-Event-Financial-Support-FAQ?bidId=)
  

**Who is eligible to apply for Special Event Support?**
  * Incorporated nonprofit organizations with a 501(c)(3) designation
  * California certified tax-exempt nonprofit organizations
  * Public agencies, including public schools or school foundations in school districts
  * Committees formally established by City Council
  * Established clubs
  * Associations or organized groups that provide services and programs directly to Anaheim for the benefit of its residents


**What kind of event is eligible for support?**  
  
Events that meet the following criteria are eligible:
  * Enhances current City programs or core services by providing additional programming, financial and/or in-kind resources, community outreach, staffing, volunteers or other tangible support to the City
  * Is open to the public and does not require any entrance fees paid for admittance
  * Provides significant citywide value, presented for the anticipated enjoyment and participation by a broad spectrum of the Anaheim community; the event shall not intentionally nor by design be presented for or intended to draw participation from a specific neighborhood area or region within the City and shall not exclude the participation of any segment of the community


**How do I apply for support?**
Applications must be submitted no later than **5pm on March 31, 2026** to apply for support for the following fiscal year (fiscal year runs July – June respectively).  
  
Informal City Sponsorship applications are due **60 days** **prior to date of event.**
  * Complete the application by using the [Online Form](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/Special-Event-Financial-Support-Applicat-104 "Special Event Support Online Form") or
  * [Download](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/19159), complete, and return the application to:


Anaheim Community Services Department  
Special Event Financial Support Request  
200 S. Anaheim Blvd., Suite 433  
Anaheim, CA 92805
  * Postmarks will not be accepted


**What kind of support can I receive if my application is approved?**  
  
The City may provide **Formal City Sponsorship** via monetary funding and/or **Informal Co-Sponsorship** of in-kind services to support nonprofit entity events, programs or services that benefit Anaheim residents.
Informal Co-Sponsorship which does not provide monetary support, but may include:  
  
**What is the maximum amount of support I can receive for my event or program?**
The amount of funding allocated for approved events may range from a minimum of $1,000 to a maximum of $10,000.

#  Contract Administration 
Contract Administration is responsible for the management, monitoring and administration of all capital improvement construction contracts and projects as well as all design and construction of single and multi-level public buildings and miscellaneous facilities through out the City  
  
This section works closely with our inspection section and other city departments/divisions to ensure compliance with budgets, plans and specifications. 
![Hard hat and plans](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=176)

#  La Palma Water Complex 
![La Palma 2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23304)
![La Palma 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=23305)
The La Palma Complex serves the residences and businesses of Central and West Anaheim providing water supply and peak pumping capacity to fight fires and meet maximum day water demands. The facilities include the followings:
  * (1) 4 million gallon (4 MG) water storage reservoir that stores water
  * (1) well that pumps groundwater to fill reservoir
  * (1) pump Station that pumps water from reservoir to water distribution system


The dedication for the La Palma Water Complex was held on July 31, 2018.

#  Trade Shows & Special Event Information 
_Having a Special Event or Trade Show in our City? Download important information, permits and instructions here.  
_
[Floor Plan Submittal Info](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50631/Floor-Plan-Submittal-Info) for trade shows, general sessions, banquets & breakout sessions.
We have provided requirements that are site specific for the following venues: [Anaheim Convention Center](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12514), [Anaheim Marriott Hotel](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12519), [Disneyland Resort Hotels](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12517), [Anaheim Hilton Hotel](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/28379/Hilton-Hotel-Requirements-2019), [Anaheim Sheraton Park Hotel](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/67529/Sheraton-Park-Hotel-Requirements), & [Glover Stadium](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/46571/Glover-Stadium-Requirements).
If you are an exhibitor in a trade show or planning an indoor event, please review our [Exhibitor & Event Info](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50634/Exhibitor-and-Event-Info) for general information and items that require a permit. For exhibitors in the NAMM or NPEW Shows, please review the [NAMM Supplemental](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/67531/NAMM-Exhibitor-Supplemental) or [NPEW Supplemental](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/68219/NPEW-Exhibitor-Supplemental) that highlights specific requirements for this show. For companies that are licensed through the California State Fire Marshal's Office for flame treatment of drapes, curtains or decorative material, we have provided a [Flame Retardant Applicators List](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/52025/Flame-Retardant-Applicators). 
## Special Events
For a list of all events or activities that require a permit, please refer to our [Event Permit Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50633/Event-Permit-Application). We have provided permit instruction for [Tents](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12521), [Carnivals/Outdoor Festivals](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12516), [Vendor Festival Requirements](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/64360/Festival-Vendor-Requirements), [Live Christmas Trees in Public Buildings](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/46618/Live-Christmas-Trees-in-Public-Buildings), [Christmas Tree & Pumpkin Lots](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/46619/Christmas-Tree-and-Pumpkin-Patch-Lots), and the use of [Candles](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12515) for banquets, weddings and religious ceremonies.
The use of fireworks, sparklers, bottle sparklers, special effects, or cold spark devices requires a permit from Anaheim Fire & Rescue. All items listed including cold spark devices are considered a pyrotechnic device by the CA State Fire Marshal’s Office and requires you to hire a fireworks company to submit all required state licensing and documentation. It also requires fire standby by the fireworks company and Anaheim Fire & Rescue. Some facilities and hotels do not allow pyrotechnics inside their facility.
Please contact tradeshowinspector@anaheim.net for more information.
[![Anaheim Fire Patch Logo 275px](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44704)](https://www.anaheim.net/6096)

  7. Street Lighting Projects


#  Street Lighting Projects 
The City of Anaheim has an LED Street Light Replacement Program that focuses on street light reliability improvements and new lighting requests to enhance pedestrian and roadway visibility, and address public safety and security needs within various neighborhoods.  

In 2017, Utilities began accelerating the upgrade of its streetlights with _light emitting_ diodes (LED), which have become an industry standard due to the technology’s efficiency, longevity, and gradual reduction in price. LED streetlights are expected to last twice as long as the older, high-pressure sodium (HPS) streetlights, reducing future maintenance requirements.
The program consists of:
  * Upgrading approximately 24,000+ streetlights from high-pressure sodium (HPS) to Light Emitting Diodes (LED). Approximately 92% of the streetlights have been upgraded to date.
  * New street light requests are tracked by Neighborhood Services in the Community Services Department and prioritized based on community feedback, assessed needs by other departments, and available funding.

**La Palma Parkway Neighborhood  
****Project Completed: October 2024**
This project installed 17 new LED streetlights to supplement additional lighting on various streets and improved roadway visibility adjacent to La Palma Park.
**Alberta Street Neighborhood**  
**Project Completed: August 2024**
This project installed 25 new LED streetlights on Alberta Street from Anaheim Boulevard to Olive Street.
**Rose/Vine/Bush Neighborhood  
Project Completed: January 2025**
This project installed 30 new LED streetlights in Rose, Vine, and Bush neighborhoods.
**Santa Ana Canyon Road  
Project Completed: June 2025**
This project installed 36 new LED streetlights on Santa Canyon Road from Imperial Highway to Fairmont Boulevard and improved roadway visibility.

[![Report fireworks online banner  Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=36698)](https://survey123.arcgis.com/share/e07b840530c44759aff42083537713f3)
# The 2026 fireworks fundraiser season is here!
Each year, eight Anaheim-based nonprofits and eight high schools sell Safe and Sane fireworks —the only fireworks allowed in Anaheim and only on July 4 —to raise money they use all year for their community programs.
A lottery is held yearly to select the nonprofits that will operate stands. Operation includes submitting a permit, staffing the stand and providing onsite security 24 hours a day, seven days a week, from June 28 through July 4.  
  
Lottery applications are due by mail to the Anaheim City Clerk's Office at 200 S. Anaheim Blvd., Anaheim, CA 92805. The application must be postmarked no later than April 23.
[![Lottery Graphic Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=70141)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/70140/2026-Fireworks-Lottery-Application)
##  **Important dates:**  
  

  * **Thursday, March 19: Applications posted**
  * **Thursday, April 23: Applications due**
  * **Monday, April 27: Lottery at 4 p.m. via Zoom**
  * **Friday, May 1: Permit application posted**
  * **Monday, June 1: Permit applications due**
  * **Sunday, June 28: Sales begin**


Fireworks may be discharged between 10 a.m. and 10 p.m. only on July 4.
![Informational bilingual flyer about fireworks 2025](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64906)

#  Department Orders 
Department Orders establish and document the administrative guidelines and specific procedures of the Public Utilities Department consistent with City of Anaheim policies and applicable regulations. Department Orders collectively facilitate staff efficiency, effectiveness, and accountability by establishing and communicating standards and processes by individual and group responsibilities.   

![Anaheim Public Utilities ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=29950)

#  Solar Contractors 
## Anaheim Public Utilities does not endorse or pre-approve any solar contractors
or their qualifications to install privately-owned solar panels. Any such representation by a solar contractor is false. It is important for every customer considering a solar contractor to thoroughly investigate that contractor's credentials.  
[](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/12212 "Fraud Tips")  

![Solar Energy Rebates](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=7001)
If you receive unsolicited phone calls from contractors or telemarketers for solar or other energy-efficiency technology companies, you can:
  * Contact the [Better Business Bureau](https://www.bbb.org/consumer-complaints/file-a-complaint/get-started)
  * Contact the [Contractors State License Board](http://www.cslb.ca.gov/Consumers/Filing_A_Complaint/)
  * Register with the "[Do Not Call” list (DNC)](https://www.donotcall.gov/) 1-888-382-1222 
  * File a complaint for DNC violations with the [Federal Communications Commission](https://consumercomplaints.fcc.gov/hc/en-us/requests/new?ticket_form_id=39744)
  * Use the function on your phone to block unwanted calls

#  Innovative Conservation Program 
## Overview
  
The Innovative Conservation Program (ICP) provides funding for research to help document water savings and reliability of innovative water-saving devices, technologies, and strategies. Any public, private, or non-profit organizations are eligible to apply for funding. **  
  
** The next round of ICP is expected to launch in 2020 and is subject to funding availability.
For more information, please call (714) 765-5029 or [Click here](http://www.bewaterwise.com/ICP.shtml).

  1. [Home](https://www.anaheim.net/)

  3. [City Council](https://www.anaheim.net/173/City-Council)
  4. Public Participation at a City Council Meeting


#  Public Participation at a City Council Meeting 
## PUBLIC PARTICIPATION:
In addition to attending City Council meetings in person, members of the public can access the meeting live on-line at www.anaheim.net/councilvideos and on Cable Channel 3.The following options are available to the public to correspond with the City Council, and all other related agencies, on any agenda items or matters within the jurisdiction of such governing bodies:   
  

## 1) IN-PERSON COMMENTS
  
**Members of the public can provide in-person comments at the podium in the Council Chamber.** Those wanting to address the City Council can do so during the Public Comment period. The speaker must submit a speaker card to the City Clerk. The name and contact information on the card is optional; unidentified speakers will be called to speak by the speaker card number. The time limit established for public comments is three minutes per speaker unless a different time limit is announced.   
  
There are two Public Comment periods with speakers having one opportunity to address the Council. The first Public Comment period is for speakers wishing to speak on any item on the agenda, except for scheduled public hearings. After all agenda item speakers have been heard, and if the first Public Comment period has not exceeded 90 minutes, any time remaining will be provided to Non-Agenda item speakers until the 90 minute time limitation has been exhausted. The second Public Comment period, following the Report on Closed Session Items, will be opened to Non-Agenda item speakers not heard during the first Public Comment period.  
  
For public hearings, the Mayor or Chairperson will separately call for testimony at the time of each public hearing. Testimony during public hearings is subject to the following time limits: Project applicant or issue initiator: twenty minutes for initial presentation and ten minutes for rebuttal; Residents within the noticed area of the subject property: ten minutes; All other members of the public: three minutes. Public hearings regarding legislative matters: five minutes. The Mayor or Chairperson may limit the length of comments during public hearings due to the number of persons wishing to speak or if comments become repetitious or irrelevant.   
  
Speakers shall refrain from profane language and other disruptive remarks or behavior which disrupts or disturbs the meeting, or risk being removed (AMC 1.12.017)  
  

## 2) PUBLIC COMMENTS VIA E-MAIL
  
Members of the public can submit comments electronically for City Council consideration by sending them to publiccomment@anaheim.net. To ensure distribution to the City Council prior to consideration of the agenda, we encourage the public to submit comments at least 2 hours prior to the scheduled start time of the meeting (time is noted on the Council Agenda). Those comments, as well as any comments received after that time will be distributed to the City Council, posted on the City’s website, and will be made part of the official public record of the meeting.  
  

* * *

#  Power Content Label (PCL) 
AB 162 (Statute of 2009) and Senate Bill 1305 (Statute of 1997) require retail electricity suppliers to disclose information to California consumers about the energy resources used to generate the electricity they sell. The power content label provides information about the energy resources used to generate electricity put into the power grid. Just as a nutrition label provides information about the food you eat, the power content label provides information about your electricity sources.  
  
In December 2019, the California Energy Commission (CEC) modified how all utilities calculate their Power Content Label (PCL). The new calculation indicates Anaheim Public Utilities’ coal percentage increased beginning on the 2019 label. The utility’s overall portfolio has not increased its usage of coal; it is a result of the newly implemented calculation.  
  
Anaheim Public Utilities is actively working to increase energy deliveries from renewable energy resources and decrease greenhouse gas emissions associated with its energy portfolio. Investments in the generation of community-produced solar power and encouraging conservation through programs and rebates have contributed to these efforts.   
  
Over the past five years, the utility has reduced its emissions by ~40%, well ahead of the statewide goal established with the passage of SB 32 (Statute of 2016) to reduce emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030. We also forecast to be completely out of coal by July 2025 when our final coal contract with Intermountain Power Plant (IPP) transitions to natural gas. We project a further reduction in emissions to ~70% below 1990 levels in July 2027 when the IPP contract expires.   

  

![2024 Power Content Label](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45686)
![GHG Graph](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=34799)
**For more information, Anaheim Public Utilities’[Greenhouse Gas Reduction Plan ](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/7987/Greenhouse-Gas-Reduction-Plan?bidId=)outlines how we are working with partners and the community to be 100% carbon free by the year 2045.**
**[Past Power Content Labels](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/13303)  
  
Power Content Label Information**
  

[California Energy Commission – Power Content Label](https://www.energy.ca.gov/programs-and-topics/programs/power-source-disclosure/power-content-label)

## Council Member Kristen Maahs
  
Kristen Maahs was elected to the Anaheim City Council in November 2024, representing District 5 in central-east Anaheim. 
A fourth-generation Anaheim resident, Maahs is focused funding and equipping Anaheim’s police and firefighters, addressing homelessness with compassion and impact and promoting a strong economy through support of startups, existing Anaheim businesses and those new to our city.
  

Maahs also looks to build on Anaheim’s extensive reforms for transparency and accountability while ensuring public money is spent efficiently with a focus on public services.
Her district includes neighborhoods on either side of the Orange (57) Freeway, including the Platinum Triangle, the fastest growing community in Anaheim.
  

District 5 also includes well-known venues Angel Stadium of Anaheim, Honda Center and OCVibe, ARTIC as well as other business in and around the Platinum Triangle.
Since 2020, Maahs has served on the Anaheim Cultural and Heritage Commission, including as chair. The commission advises the City Council on arts, culture and historic preservation.
She also has served as president of the Anaheim Historical Society, which helps preserve the city’s history and historic places.
Maahs works as associate director of community outreach and advocacy at Alzheimer’s Orange County, an Irvine-based nonprofit that provides programs, support and information for caregivers and those impacted by Alzheimer’s and related dementias. 
She is also co-chair of the senior food security committee of the Orange County Aging Services Collaborative, which brings together agencies and nonprofits to support our county’s seniors.
Maahs also is a member of the active older adult committee of the Anaheim Human Services Network, which connects the city, nonprofits, faith-based groups and others to support Anaheim seniors. 
She’s volunteered for the Anaheim Fall Festival & Halloween Parade and the K&A Community Holiday Brunch.
Born and raised in Anaheim, Maahs’ family history in the city goes back more than 100 years when her grandfather’s family moved to the young city.
A historic family house in downtown Anaheim is known as the “The Maahs House.”
Maahs herself grew up in District 5, attending Anaheim schools and working for a time at Ganahl Lumber on Ball Road.
She holds a bachelor’s in health science with an emphasis on community health education from California State University, Fullerton. 
Maahs lives in District 5 with her husband and young son.
![D5](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=24005)

![Photo of Anaheim District 1 Council Member Ryan Balius at ARTIC tranist center](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57980)
##  Council Member Ryan Balius  
  

Ryan Balius was elected to the Anaheim City Council in November 2024, representing District 1 in west Anaheim. 
A 25-year resident of west Anaheim, Balius is focused on advancing the ongoing rebuilding of Beach Boulevard with continued police enforcement, more potential motel closures and redevelopment with homes, businesses and public spaces.
District 1, which goes from north Brookhurst Street to Anaheim’s western city lines with Buena Park, Cypress and Stanton, is home to neighborhoods, businesses, parks, schools, a library and Anaheim’s 1.5-mile stretch of Beach Boulevard.
Balius is also focused on staffing and equipping Anaheim Police and firefighters, affordable housing, addressing the impacts of homelessness on businesses and neighborhoods, getting people out of homelessness with effective services and improving intersections for better safety and traffic flow.
Prior to joining the City Council, Balius served on the Anaheim Parks and Recreation Commission, starting in 2016 and most recently as chair. The commission advises the City Council and the city’s community services director on parks, recreation and programs.
He has also served as a member of the Anaheim Services Needs Assessment Youth and Family Advisory Committee in 2024. 
Earlier, Balius took part in the Beach Boulevard Specific Plan, a 2019 city plan with community input to bring homes, shopping, restaurants, other businesses and gathering places to Beach.
He was active for more than 15 years with West Anaheim Neighborhood Development Council, a resident group that staged community events and worked to better west Anaheim.
Now retired, Balius worked for 34 years at the nearby city of Cerritos, first in parks and recreation and later in information technology.
Balius, who grew up in Cerritos, is the proud son of working parents. His brother is a sailor on active duty with the Naval Construction Battalions, better known as the Seabees responsible for engineering and building bases, airfields, roads and other military needs.
He holds a bachelor’s from California State University, Fullerton, with a minor in management information systems.
Balius lives with his wife Angie, a principal in the Garden Grove Unified School District, in the same home they bought when they moved to west Anaheim 25 years ago.
He’s an Eagle Scout, the highest rank within Boy Scouts of America, and a three-time Ironman Triathlon finisher.
![D1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=24007)

#  Resources 
## Architectural Style Guides
The City of Anaheim’s Architectural Style Guides are reference tools for owners and managers of historic buildings. Use the documents below to identify the features and materials that define your building’s historic character. In cases where you may wish to make changes to your building, the guides will help you determine compatible features and materials. 
Owners should consult with City Historic Preservation staff before making any changes to their historic properties.
### Illustrated Glossary of Historic Architecture - Across Multiple Styles
[![illustrated glossary Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=30359)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/37923/Anaheim-Illustrated-Glossary-of-Historic-Architecture)

#  Engineering Services 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [Inspection Requests](https://aca-prod.accela.com/anaheim/default.aspx)
In order to successfully process your request, a valid permit number and email address will be required.
  2. ####  [Subdivisions / Development Services](https://www.anaheim.net/506/Subdivisions-Development-Services)
We provide development-related services for private sector development and development by other public agencies or public utilities.
  3. ####  [Traffic & Transportation](https://www.anaheim.net/361/Traffic-Transportation)
This division provides for mobility and safety of people and goods.
  4. ####  [Planning & Design Services](https://www.anaheim.net/522/Planning-Design-Services)
The Design Services Section is responsible for the overall planning and development of the City's arterial highways and the master planning and design of all arterial street, storm drain, and sewer improvements plans.
  5. ####  [Engineering Requests for Proposals](https://www.anaheim.net/2928/Engineering-Requests-for-Proposals)

# Anaheim State of the City Address
Each year, Anaheim's mayor gives an address on the state of the city. The event is normally held at City National Grove of Anaheim.
See the menu to the left for past addresses.
