  1. [CustomLinks_139](https://www.anaheim.net/4993/CustomLinks-139)
  2. Community Care Response Team 


![CCRT logo final](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35555)
[![report online Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35603)](https://www.anaheim.net/anytime)
![HOTLINE NUMBER](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=35556)
Anaheim's newly-formed **Community Care Response Team** , or CCRT, is the next phase of our ongoing efforts to address homelessness, by **expanding and enhancing outreach efforts** in our city.
We began a soft rollout of the CCRT on Jan. 1, 2021 and continue to ramp up outreach. We launched Anaheim's dedicated homeless hotline Jan. 20.
If you have a complaint regarding homelessness or need help, please call (714) 820-9090 or report it online via [Anaheim Anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/anytime).
**About CCRT**
The CCRT is a **partnership** between the **city of Anaheim** and our nonprofit homeless outreach partner **City Net** , approved by the City Council in late 2020.
The team works**7 a.m. to 9 p.m., seven days a week** and is made up of outreach workers, mental health clinicians, nurse practitioners and plain-clothes, private safety officers.
The team also manages a **dedicated, live call center** to answer calls from residents and businesses related to homeless concerns, as well as calls redirected from the police dispatch line.
The goal of the CCRT is to provide more effective outreach and better use city resources by diverting homeless calls from the Anaheim Police Department. However, the **Community Policing Teams** , along with Code Enforcement and Public Works, stand ready to assist on any homeless calls where additional support is needed.
Police will now focus on homeless calls that require **public safety response or involve criminal activity**.
The CCRT will address all non-emergency homeless calls in an effort to **increase outreach focused on providing services,****addressing mental health and helping more people out of homelessness.**
If you have a complaint or concern regarding homelessness, please submit your complaint online via **Anaheim Anytime** at [Anaheim.net/anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/anytime) or call (714) 820-9090.
[Click here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58823/CCRT-Dashboard---December-2024) to see CCRT's recent progress.
[![CCRT videocover Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38433)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ov6iNlj7k4o)
[![resource guide Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=36114)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36115/CCRT-Info-Card-FINAL)

#  Air Purifiers - For School 
**
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers K-12 public and private schools rebates for room air cleaners, also known as air purifier.  

  
**
Air purifiers are portable appliances that remove fine particles, such as pollen, dust, and unpleasant odors from the air. Using ENERGY STAR® portable air purifiers is a great way to improve indoor air quality in a single room or area while using energy efficiently.
  * ENERGY STAR® certified air purifier: $75 per unit (maximum incentive up to $25,000)
  * For complete rebate [program guidelines, W-9 and application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/36232/Schools-Air-Purifier).


For more information about ENERGY STAR® certified room air purifiers and a list of certified models, visit [energystar.gov/productfinder](https://www.energystar.gov/productfinder/product/certified-room-air-cleaners/results).
  

## Helpful Resources:
  

[ENERGY STAR®](https://www.energystar.gov/products/appliances/air_purifiers_cleaners)
[Environmental Protection Agency](https://www.epa.gov/coronavirus/air-cleaners-hvac-filters-and-coronavirus-covid-19)

  7. Getting Started with Your EV


#  Getting Started with Your EV 
## Before Purchasing an Electric Vehicle (EV)
  

If you are thinking about purchasing or leasing an electric vehicle, it is important to know that there are a number of decisions you need to make and actions you may need to take before you drive one home, such as:
  * Does your home have sufficient electrical capacity to charge the battery?
  * How will an EV impact your electricity bill?
  * Will you be using a [Residential ](https://www.anaheim.net/6332/)[Level I or Level II charger](https://anaheim.net/6332/Residential-EV-Charging)?


As you’re considering your purchase or lease, please [contact Anaheim Public Utilities](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?DID=86). Contacting us early in the process will help determine how to better serve your new electrical needs, and it will allow us to keep you informed of important electric vehicle information, charging options, and getting your home plug-in ready.
  

For additional information, visit the virtual showroom at [](https://ev.anaheim.net/)<https://ev.anaheim.net/> to select a make and model and to explore available options.
### Contact Us
  1. ## Electric Vehicle Concierge 
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
[Contact Form](http://ca-anaheim.civicplus.com/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Electric-Vehicle-Concierge-55)
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.   
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)[](https://www.anaheim.net/1069/)  
  
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


## Before Bringing the EV Home
  

Once you decide on a make and model, you’ll need to consider how you plan to charge the vehicle. Most homeowners will simply plug in their electric vehicles at home. Depending on the EV you choose, you may be able to plug into a standard grounded, 3-prong 120-volt household outlet. Or, to charge your EV in half the time it takes to charge with a 120V, you might consider installing a 240-volt circuit, similar to what an electric stove or central air conditioning system use. The 240V requires that you purchase the Electrical Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE) recommended for the vehicle.
  

The below steps are intended to walk residential customers through the process of installing a Level 2 EV charging station at home. While these steps apply to the majority of residential customers, there may be additional steps involved, such as when your panel is over 200 amps or when city-owned facilities require relocation or undergrounding. If you or your electrician believe any of these situations apply to you, please call Electrical Engineering at 714-765-5156 for additional information on how to proceed with your service request.

  6. Local Criminal History


#  Local Criminal History 
Access to criminal history summary records, maintained by the Department of Justice, is restricted by law to legitimate law enforcement purposes and authorized applicant agencies. However, individuals have the right to request a copy of their own criminal history record from the Department to review for accuracy and completeness. Requests from third parties are not authorized and will not be processed.
_**Note:** Criminal history records requested for an individual’s own review cannot be used for Visa/Immigration or any foreign nation transactions. If you are seeking a background check for Visa/Immigration or any foreign nation transactions, see _[ _Clearance Letters_](https://www.anaheim.net/5918/Clearance-Letter) _._
To request your criminal history, please visit the Attorney General’s website <https://oag.ca.gov/fingerprints/record-review> to review the fingerprint and background checks process. 
  1. # Contact Us

425 S. Harbor Boulevard  
Anaheim, CA 92805  
Phone: [(714) 765-1990](tel:7147651990)
### **Hours**
Monday-Friday, 8:00 AM-5:00 PM  
Closed on all City Holidays, Saturday, and Sunday

#  myPower Savings Program 
![myPower](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=19589)
Anaheim Public Utilities is inviting you to participate in the myPower Savings Program. To reduce the need to build new power plants, Anaheim is paying eligible residential customers to reduce electricity usage when needed.   
  
You can earn an instant enrollment reward (up to a $30 bill credit) and additional Program event participation rewards ($1.00 for every kilowatt-hour of energy you reduce during event hours; up to $100 in bill credits per fiscal year). Enroll now to receive Program event notifications and plan to reduce or shift your electricity usage.   

### myPower Savings Event Details
  * Event participation is voluntary.
  * Events are limited to non-holiday weekdays, up to 3 hours per event, and 1 event per day. Total number of events will not exceed 10 events per year.
  * Events can be called at any time between 6 AM and 10 PM, but are typically called in the late afternoon to early evening.
  * Bill credits are calculated based on the difference between your electricity usage during and just prior to an event. The baseline energy usage is taken one hour prior to the event, then compared to the usage during each hour of the event.
  * **[myPower FAQ](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/16359/Residential-Demand-Response-Program-FAQ?bidId=)**.


### Contact Us
  1. ## Residential Rebates & Incentives
  
714-765-4250 or Email  
  
TTY/TDD: Dial 711 and ask the TRS operator to connect you to 714-765-3300.
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.  
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


### It's Simple to Participate
  1. Enroll in the Program by filling out the application.
  2. Receive an instant enrollment reward on your electric bill based on your power saving appliances.
  3. Receive event notifications by email or text.
  4. Opt-in to an event by responding to the event notification and reducing electricity usage during the specified event hours.
  5. Receive electric bill credits based on your actual electricity usage reductions.


Rewards for Enrollment If You Have…  | Program Enrollment Reward  
---|---  
Central air conditioner and pool pump | $30  
Central air conditioner | $20  
Wall air conditioner or EV charger | $10  
Rewards for Reducing Power Usage During Program Events…  | Reward  
---|---  
For each kilowatt-hour (kWh) you reduce  | $1.00/kWh   
### Simple Ways to Reduce Power Usage during Events 
  * Raise air conditioner’s setting to 78 degrees or turn it off.
  * Turn off pool pump or EV charger during program event hours.
  * Run major appliances before or after program events.
  * Turn off unnecessary lights and appliances.


Note: Your customer information will not be shared, sold, or used other than to communicate with you about other Anaheim Public Utilities program incentives. 
For notification preferences or more questions, please contact us by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008. 
## Go to the [**APPLICATION**](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/myPower-Savings-Program-233)
Program Requirements: In order to participate in the myPower Savings Program, customers must have an interval recording smart meter installed at their homes, enroll in the Program, and sign-up to receive Program event notification. Customers may select to receive event notification via email or text message. Anaheim Public Utilities will send advance notifications of pending Program events through a customer’s selected delivery method. If text message delivery is selected, the customer’s carrier's text messaging rates will apply. Anaheim Public Utilities does not warrant or guarantee receipt of event notifications. As an enrollee of the myPower Savings Program, it is the customer’s responsibility to ensure that Anaheim Public Utilities has his or her preferred and most current contact information. Enrollment rewards are subject to verification by Anaheim Public Utilities. Program requirements are subject to change at any time. Other restrictions may apply. Enrolled customers can update notification methods or terminate participation in the Program at any time by emailing mypowersavings@anaheim.net or calling 714-765-5008.

#  Information Services 
  * Manages the acquisition, maintenance and security of all of the city’s Information Services assets including all network infrastructure, system servers, enterprise data, personal computers, and telecommunications equipment.
  * Provides Information Services support for all departments in the city.
  * Responsible for maintaining continuous operations of the city’s network infrastructure as well as availability of all core systems.
  * Maintains the City’s IT Service Desk, providing user support for hardware and software requests/issues.

  5. Be Ready


![Be ready web banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=41185)
Be Ready Anaheim is the power of preparedness. 
Every day, Anaheim's emergency response teams ― police, fire, public utilities and public works ― are ready to serve our city in times of need.
But community preparedness is more than that.
It is people, families, volunteers, neighborhoods, workplaces, community groups and organizations prepared for emergencies and resiliency.
While not fun to think about, the threat of earthquakes, wildfires, severe storms and other natural and human-made disasters is real and always with us.
Be Ready Anaheim is our partnership with the community to be ready for, to ride out and to recover from disasters and other challenges that confront our city.
[![New be rain ready graphic Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=40932)](http://www.anaheim.net/2985/Preparing-for-Winter-Rain)

  5. Current Building Codes and Design Criteria


#  Current Building Codes and Design Criteria 
The 2025 California Building Codes and City of Anaheim Amendments are enforceable for all residential and non-residential construction projects submitted on or after January 1, 2026. Projects submitted prior to January 1, 2026 shall comply with the 2022 Codes. The state codes have been amended by the City of Anaheim to address local topographic, climatic, and/or geologic conditions, and to include provisions pertaining to the administration of these codes. The amendments can be found under Title 15 of the Anaheim Municipal Code.
  * [**City of Anaheim Local Amendments**](https://codelibrary.amlegal.com/codes/anaheim/latest/anaheim_ca/0-0-0-62428)


# **2025 California Building Codes**
  * 2025 California Building Code (Volume 1 & Volume 2) [![California Building Code Picture 2025 Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=69658)](https://www.dgs.ca.gov/BSC/Codes)
  * 2025 California Residential Code
  * 2025 California Electrical Code
  * 2025 California Mechanical Code
  * 2025 California Plumbing Code
  * 2025 California Energy Code
  * 2025 California Historical Building Code
  * 2025 California Fire Code
  * 2025 California Existing Building Code
  * 2025 California Green Building Standards Code


The California Building Standards Codes are available to view at the link below.
  * [](https://www.anaheim.net/6018/Current-Building-Codes-and-Design-Criter) [](https://www.anaheim.net/6018/Current-Building-Codes-and-Design-Criter)**[California Building Standards Code Online](https://www.dgs.ca.gov/BSC/Codes)**


# Local Design Criteria
  * Seismic Design Category D, E or F as determined by USGS Maps and CBC Chapter 16
  * Wind: 
    * Risk Category I: 90 mph
    * Risk Category II: 95 mph
    * Risk Category III: 100 mph
    * Risk Category IV: 105 mph
  * Climate Zone 8
  * Earthquake Zones of Required Investigation : [CGS Maps](https://maps.conservation.ca.gov/cgs/EQZApp/app/)
  * Fire Hazard Severity Zones: [CAL Fire Maps](https://egis.fire.ca.gov/FHSZ/)

  2. [Departments](https://www.anaheim.net/148/Departments)
  3. [Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/490/Planning-Building)
  4. [Building Division](https://www.anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division)
  5. Permit Processing Info


#  Permit Processing Info 
Building Division staff reviews construction plans to verify compliance with State Building Codes and local regulations. We are currently under the provisions of the 2025 Edition of the California Building Standards Codes Cycle.
If you are wondering if you need a Building Permit for your proposed project, please see the following PDF for an detailed explanation of when a permit is required:  [When Is A Permit Required?](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/41215)
To submit a project for plan review, please "Plans Required for Submittal" handout below to determine the number of plan sets required based on your scope of work.
[Plans Required for Submittal](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/41216)
We will also need a completed Plan Check Application form. All forms can be found at the following link: 
[Building Permit Forms, Applications & Fees](https://www.anaheim.net/6013/Forms-Applications-and-Fees)
When submitting for a second cycle of plan review, prior to issuance, this is referred to as a resubmittal (Not a revision). Each subsequent submittal is called a resubmittal. DO NOT cloud the changes made to the plans. Please provide a narrative identifying the changes. Re-submittals of plans due to plan check comments will require:
  1. Old Plan Check Sets
  2. New Corrected Construction Sets
  3. Plan Check Comment Response Letter
  4. DO NOT CLOUD CORRECTIONS


If the plans have been approved, the permit issued, and a change to the plan is required. This is called a revision. All changes to the plans MUST be clouded. If your project needs a revision, please see the following document for steps and procedures.
[Plan Revision Procedure](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39718)
* * *
# Construction Plan Requirements
Detailed construction plans are required for most types of projects. Construction plans must clearly indicate the location, nature, and extent of the project, and show in detail how it will comply with the Building Codes.
For a permit application to be deemed complete for submittal, the submittal documents must contain all information as required per California Building Code Section 107.
**This includes, but not be limited to:**
## **Architectural Plans***
  * Site Plan showing Property Line Boundaries, All Structures on Property and Project Location
  * Detailed and Dimensioned Floor and Roof Plans
  * Detailed and Dimensioned Building Elevations and Cross-Sections
  * Means of Egress Analysis
  * Green Building Code Requirements
  * Title 24 Energy Calculations


## **Structural Plans***
  * Foundation Plan
  * Framing Plans
  * Roof Framing Plans
  * Structural Details
  * Structural Calculations


## Mechanical, Electrical, Plumbing and Landscaping Plans (as necessary)
*Please be advised that not all items may be necessary based on the proposed scope of work. Please speak with a Plan Check Engineer with the Building Division to clarify if an item is necessary for submittal.
* * *
# **Getting Construction Plans Prepared for Your Project**
Construction plans must be prepared by a qualified person. Building & Safety staff does not prepare plans. For most project types, the plans must be prepared by a California Licensed Architect or Engineer.
For help hiring an Architect, please download the [Consumer's Guide to Hiring an Architect](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39682).
**Submitting incomplete, inaccurate, or non-compliant plans could delay the permit process for your project and incur additional plan review fees.**
* * *
# **Over-the-Counter Plan Review**
Smaller projects which require no more than 30 minutes for plan review can typically be reviewed over-the-counter. Depending on the complexity and scope of your project, or the existing conditions which may be present, Building & Safety may require that you submit your plans for a more detailed review. The following types of projects are normally reviewed over-the-counter:
  * Residential Patio Covers
  * Swimming Pools
  * Wall Signs
  * Re-roofing

#  Free Water Efficiency Seminars 
![Be Water Smart](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42376)
##  **Online Landscaping Workshop Series**  
  

Anaheim Public Utilities is pleased to announce a NEW series of online virtual Landscaping Workshops, hosted in partnership with the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California and presented by the [Green Gardens Group](https://greengardensgroup.com/), a leader in sustainable landscaping. Online Workshops will provide an overview of California Friendly and native landscape training, along with elements of turf removal and garden transformation. Please visit the Green Gardens Group web page using the below link to view workshop dates and times. Registration is required to attend.
##  **[](https://greengardensgroup.com/turf-transformation/)**[**Register Here**](https://greengardensgroup.com/turf-transformation/)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  6. Fiber Lease Agreement


#  Fiber Lease Agreement 
  
Anaheim Public Utilities operates a fiber optics network to control and monitor its water and electric systems and provide connectivity for municipal services. The fiber network has spare capacity that broadband service providers can lease to provide high-speed internet access to their customer in Anaheim. Here’s the link to view the fiber optics lease agreement: [Sample fiber optics lease agreement](http://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/23291/Sample-Fiber-Lease-Agreement) and [fiber lease rates](http://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/23293/Anaheims-Fiber-Optics-Leasing-Guidelines). 
For more information on wireless improvements by various cell companies providers, please refer to the below links:  
  

  * [Verizon](https://www.verizon.com/about/our-company/5g/what-5g)
  * [AT&T](https://www.att.com/5g/consumer/)
  * [T-Mobile](https://www.t-mobile.com/5g)
  * [Sprint](https://www.sprint.com/en/landings/5g.html)
  * [Crown Castle](https://www.crowncastle.com/communities/5g-information)
  * [Mobilitie](http://www.mobilitie.com/connectedcity/index.html)


If you have any other questions, please contact Jean Pawlicki at 714-765-4535 or [JPawlicki@anaheim.net](https://www.anaheim.net/5996/JPawlicki@anaheim.net).

  5. Certificates of Occupancy & TCOs


#  Certificates of Occupancy & TCOs 
# Certificates of Occupancy
A Certificate of Occupancy (CofO) is a document issued by the Building Division which certifies that a commercial space or newly constructed residential building has been inspected for compliance with the California Building Standards Code and local ordinances which govern construction and occupancy.
The City of Anaheim issues the Certificate of Occupancy to the space or building, and not to the specific business. Should a business vacate a space, and another business of the same use/occupancy takes possession of the space without making any modifications requiring a building permit, the existing CofO remains in effect.
When a commercial space is modified and a building permit is required, the city will conduct a record search to determine if a new Certificate of Occupancy must be issued. A new CofO is triggered when the use or area of the space is changed. If so, the new CofO will be issued when all of the work has passed a final building inspection. If no new CofO is required, the existing CofO on file will remain in effect.
Once a permit is issued, city Building Inspectors will perform the necessary inspections to confirm the space complies with the building codes for the proposed use and that no hazardous or substandard conditions exist.
# Temporary Certificates of Occupancy
Building & Safety may issue a Temporary Certificate of Occupancy (TCO) on a case-by-case basis. TCOs must be coordinated with the Building Inspector and approved by the Building Official. An additional fee is required for a TCO and is valid for a period of 30 calendar days. Approvals from other City Departments and Divsions may also be required prior to the issuance of a TCO including Public Works Department, Fire Department and the Planning Division. The following items will be considered before a TCO is issued:
  * The scope of the remaining work and its impact on the use of the space
  * How the space will be maintained safe and accessible while the remaining work is completed
  * The timeframe needed to complete the remaining work


[Application for Temporary Use and Occupancy (TCO)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39747)
[Application for Temporary Utility Services](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/39748)

#  Supplier Registration 
>   
> 
## How to Register
Thank you for your interest in doing business with the city. We welcome new suppliers and are committed to providing opportunities for all qualified businesses to submit bids. We use an electronic bid system to register our suppliers, send them automated email notices of bid opportunities, and to receive, tabulate, and post our bid results.
If you have already registered your company, you may update your registration information at any time. To register for the first time please visit the [City of Anaheim OpenGov Vendor Portal](https://anaheim.net/6619/Bids-Requests-for-Proposals-RFPs) and follow the instructions provided.
Required Documentation  
You will need to have the following information available to complete your registration:  

  * Alternate email address. This should be one that is checked daily, regardless of any employee absences or personnel changes. Bid notices are sent simultaneously to both the primary and alternate email addresses to help ensure your company receives the notice in a timely manner.
  * Any applicable license numbers such as State Contractor’s License, City of Anaheim Business License, etc.
  * Category Codes. We use NIGP codes to identify our supplier’s type of business and direct our bid notifications to the appropriate companies. If you do not know your company’s applicable NIGP code you will be able to look it up within the category code section of our system.
  * Name and email address of the primary contact person who should receive our bid notifications
  * Your Company’s Tax ID number or your Social Security Number

Additional information and tips for registering are located within the vendor registration portal. Once you have registered, please be sure to keep your contact information current in our system. If the email addresses you provide become invalid, you will not receive our bid notifications or correspondence.  
  
If you have any questions, please contact our staff for assistance.

#  Renewables Portfolio Standard 
![2020 Resource Mix \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38177)
![2020 Renewables \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38176)
![2030 Resource Mix \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38179)
![2045 Resource Mix \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38181)
![2030 Renewables \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38178)
![2045 Renewables \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=38180)
### Renewable Resource Portfolio – Today and the Future
Since 2003, Anaheim Public Utilities Department has been committed to providing customers with increasing amounts of energy from renewable resources, while reducing the consumption of non-renewable energy sources. California’s ambitious energy policy goals are aimed at reducing reliance on fossil fuels and significantly lowering greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions. A key measure for achieving these goals is the State’s Renewables Portfolio Standard (RPS), which requires all utilities in the state to incrementally increase the amount of renewable energy delivered to its customers.
Senate Bill (SB) 100, enacted on September 10, 2018, increased the renewable procurement target from 50% to 60% by 2030, and set a state policy goal to have 100% of retail load be served by carbon-free resources by 2045. Anaheim Public Utilities plans to increase renewable power from resources such as wind, solar, geothermal, and biogas while reducing coal power to zero as contracts expire. A balanced and diversified energy portfolio, including renewables, can reduce the risk of price spikes due to fuel supply disruptions and resulting price fluctuations. Doing this over the next several years will keep the impact to electric rates to a minimum. 
### Contact Us
  1. ## Renewable Portfolio Standard
  
714-765-4405 or Email
### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm  
  
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.   
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
  
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


[![RPS Percentages Graphic](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=32245)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/32246/RPS-Percentage-Target)
### Power Content Label
Anaheim’s RPS is in full compliance with state laws, which encourage the transition from fossil fuel resources to renewable and sustainable power resources. State law requires utilities to disclose information to California consumers about the energy resources used to generate the electricity they sell. You can think of the Power Content Label (PCL) as a "nutrition label" for electricity. The PCL provides information about the energy resources used to generate electricity that is put into the power grid. Just as a nutrition label provides information about the food you eat, the PCL provides information about electricity consumed in your home.
Anaheim Public Utilities continues to transition from carbon intensive coal energy to clean renewable energy. From 2004 to 2021, Anaheim Public Utilities has increased renewable energy from 1% to 36% while significantly reducing coal power through the full divesture of San Juan Generating Station at the end of 2017, and the planned exit of Intermountain Power Plant contract in 2027. The percentage of renewable energy generated in any given year can vary due to the nature of renewable technology. For example, more hours of sunshine and wind will increase energy output from solar and wind resources. For this reason, there may be a difference between annual energy production from renewables, and the renewable energy targets mentioned in the section above.
The PCL is updated in the 4th quarter of every year with the previous year’s power mix, and can be found [here](http://www.anaheim.net/3452/Power-Content-Label).

#  Is Your Business Ready to Go Solar? 
![solar commercial](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42709)
Installing solar panels is a big decision for any business. Anaheim Public Utilities is here to help you go solar at your property. 
If you are planning to install solar panels at your business, please review the checklist below to ensure your system is properly permitted by the City’s Planning and Building Department and meets interconnection requirements from Anaheim Public Utilities.
**Important Note:** Early NEM 1.0 programs in California were intended to encourage private investment in solar and to increase the amount of renewable energy used in California. While early NEM 1.0 programs were successful in meeting these goals, they also had a tendency to shift the cost of maintaining the local grid to non-solar customers. Anaheim Public Utilities is a not-for-profit municipal utility and as such, is required to recover our cost of maintaining the local grid and purchasing power on behalf of all customers. NEM 1.0 customers who are grandfathered will not be placed on NEM 2.0 unless they seek to expand their solar panels, at which time, customers will be placed solely on NEM 2.0 for their entire system.
## Here are nine steps to installing solar for your Anaheim business:
  1. **Right-size your PV system by ensuring your building is as energy efficient as possible.** You can schedule your free [Comprehensive Energy Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/940) that will help you understand your energy usage by calling 714-765-4250.
  2. **Know how much solar energy is needed.** Obtain your annual energy usage by logging on to your account [online](http://anaheim.net/2567/Manage-My-Account-Online) to check past consumption. If you don’t have online billing, then always feel free to call our customer service staff at 714-765-3300.
  3. **Understand how**[**net energy metering (NEM) 2.0**](http://www.anaheim.net/5587/NEM-20)**works.**
  4. **Decide on payment options: complete purchase, power purchase agreement, or lease.** Research how your choice may affect a future sale of your property. And don’t forget about a warranty for your entire system – not just the solar panels.
  5. **Obtain**[**contractor bids**](https://www.cslb.ca.gov/)**from reputable firms who are****properly**[**licensed**](http://anaheim.net/2659/Business-License)**and insured.** We recommend a minimum of three bids prior to signing a contract. The solar equipment being provided to you should be listed on the state’s [website](https://www.energy.ca.gov/programs-and-topics/programs/solar-equipment-lists).
  6. **Contractor submits plans to the**[**Anaheim Planning & Building**](https://www.anaheim.net/5252/Building-Division)**for a building permit.**
  7. **Construct your solar panel system.**
  8. **Obtain final building permit sign off in order to have a bi-directional and power production meter installed by Anaheim Public Utilities.**


## Important Things to Know
  1. [Battery Storage](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_0)
  2. [Energy Efficiency](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_1)
  3. [Payment Options for Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_2)
  4. [Contractor Selection](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_3)


  1. [Battery Storage](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_0)


Under NEM 2.0, excess energy produced during the day is best stored on your property instead of being sent back to the grid at the wholesale credit rates offered. With storage, the homeowner manages their production, storage, and how much energy they use from the grid.
  1. [Energy Efficiency](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_1)


[Reducing your energy use](http://anaheim.net/2532/For-Your-Home) is the easiest way to reduce your electricity bill and by doing so, you can install a smaller less costly solar system. You can schedule your free [Comprehensive Energy Assessment](https://www.anaheim.net/940) that will help you understand your energy usage by calling 714-765-4250.
  1. [Payment Options for Solar](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_2)


**Purchase System** – Business owner pays cash or with a loan.
**Power Purchase Agreement (PPA)** – Contractor will install and own the solar system and the business owner will purchase the power from the system at a negotiated rate. Be careful of the annual escalator percentage and be aware of any issues that may arise when (if) selling your property. 
**Lease** – Contractor will install and own the solar and the business owner will pay a flat monthly lease rate, just like leasing a car. Be careful of the annual escalator percentage and be aware of any issues that may arise when (if) selling the property. 
  1. [Contractor Selection](https://www.anaheim.net/6136/Solar-Energy-and-Net-Metering-For-Busine#tab96e4b441-d556-4aff-92b4-c9bc8879cd49_3)


Contractors will provide free estimates. You want to ensure the contractor is licensed and their license is currently [valid](https://www.cslb.ca.gov/). Ask your neighbors and friends for references and compare contractors and rates. It’s recommend that you obtain at least three quotes from licensed contractors prior to making a decision. Anaheim Public Utilities cannot recommend contractors as a municipal utility.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  6. Be Ready Anaheim: A Hazard Mitigation Plan


![Haz mit page header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42228)
[![Approved plan click here Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48821)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/48820/Anaheim-LHMP-Plan-2022)
[![Hazards map link Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48927)](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/experiencebuilder/experience/?id=18a98741d67f49ccb4021813182688be&page=home&views=view_13)
Disasters are a part of life. And while we don't like thinking about the next big earthquake, wildfire or pandemic, being prepared and having a plan in place will us ride them out and on our way to recovery sooner.
The city is undertaking the creation of a hazard mitigation plan that will help us anticipate the natural and human-caused hazards that could affect Anaheim, assess their potential damage and create a path to better prepare our community. 
# What are the hazards?
The hazards we're assessing are both natural and caused by humans:
  * Wildfires and urban fires
  * Earthquakes 
  * Severe weather (wind, heat, drought)
  * Landslides
  * Floods
  * Climate change
  * Vector and pest issues
  * Public health emergencies
  * Hazardous materials spills
  * Terrorism
  * Civil unrest


# Why do we need a plan?
A hazard mitigation plan will help Anaheim better plan for future emergencies. Usually after a disaster occurs, communities take steps to recover from the emergency and rebuild.   
  
This plan is a way for the city to better prepare for these disasters, so when they do happen, less damage occurs and recovery is easier. Our plan will help us reduce property damage, injury and loss of life from disasters.   
  
Aside from protecting public health and safety, this approach can save money. Studies estimate that every dollar spent on mitigation saves an average of four dollars on response and recovery costs. 
Additionally, having a hazard mitigation plan in place will make Anaheim eligible for state funding and Federal Emergency Management Agency grants that can be used to further improve our safety and preparedness. 
# What's inside the plan?
The city's hazard mitigation plan includes four main sections:
  * **HAZARDS:** a summary of the natural and human-caused hazards that pose a risk to our community, including descriptions of past disasters and the chances of these disasters occurring in the future
  * **THREAT ASSESSMENT:** a look at the threat level and potential impacts for each hazard facing Anaheim residents, including our most vulnerable; this will include threats to important buildings and infrastructure, such as police and fire stations, hospitals, roads and utility lines. 
  * **STRATEGY:** specific policy recommendations that will reduce the threat and impact from hazards to Anaheim; these will be carried over the next five years
  * **MAINTENANCE:** with a plan is in place, we'll continue to update it with new information or strategies


# Who prepared the plan?
City staff, community stakeholders and consultants
# How can I get involved?
  * **Email:** reach out to our project leader, Dr. Jannine Wilmoth, at jwilmoth@anaheim.net


# What can I do now to prepare for a disaster?
  * **Know the hazards** that may affect you at your home, work, or school. You can find more [here](http://myhazards.caloes.ca.gov/).
  * **Assemble an emergency kit** for your home. In a disaster, you may have to rely on supplies in your emergency kit for at least three days. Be sure to include supplies for any pets and anyone in your home with special needs. Learn more [here](https://www.ready.gov/kit). 
  * **Have a disaster plan** for your household, including how people should contact each other if a disaster occurs and where you should meet.
  * **Know your neighbors** and how you can help them in an emergency. In a disaster, emergency responders may not be able to reach your neighborhood for a while. Know if your neighbors have any special needs, and be sure to check on them as soon as you can.
  * Make sure your **homeowner’s or renter’s insurance****covers you** from disasters such as earthquakes and floods. If these disasters occur, having good insurance coverage will help you recover easier.
  * **Join Anaheim CERT** , a group of volunteers trained by the city to assist emergency responders during disasters. Training is free and offered at times throughout the year.
  * **Speak to your employer about creating a disaster recovery** , workforce communication, or business continuity plan. If they already have one or more of these plans in place, make sure you and your coworkers know it.


  1. [Know Your Way](https://www.anaheim.net/5204/Know-Your-Way) [](https://www.anaheim.net/6102/Be-Ready-Anaheim-A-Hazard-Mitigation-Pla "Expand Know Your Way Submenu")
    1. [Know Your Way Evacuation Zones](https://www.anaheim.net/6063/Know-Your-Way-Evacuation-Zones)

#  Services & Department Information 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [Get a Permit](https://www.anaheim.net/6757/Get-a-Permit)
Page to assist user where to help with Permits for Fire & Rescue
  2. ####  [Documents & Forms](https://www.anaheim.net/659/Documents-Forms)
Select various documents and forms to view from the Fire and Rescue Department.
  3. ####  [Metro Net Fire Authority](https://www.metronetfire.org/)
Metro Cities Fire Authority, also known as Metro Net Fire Dispatch, is an emergency communications center located in Orange County, California. Metro Net Fire Dispatch provides 9-1-1 Fire and EMS dispatch services to more than 1.2 million people in an area approximately 200 square miles.
  4. ####  [North Net Training Center](https://www.northnettraining.net/)
Web link to the North Net Training Authority webpage.
  5. ####  [Reporting a Hazardous Materials Incident](https://www.anaheim.net/826/Reporting-a-Hazardous-Materials-Incident)
Locate steps to take in the event that you've experienced or witnessed a Hazardous Materials Incident.
  6. ####  [Public Service Announcements](https://www.anaheim.net/2388/Public-Service-Announcements)
Anaheim Fire & Rescue's Public Service Announcement videos on Heart Attacks, Home Sprinklers, Carbon Monoxide, Smoke Alarms, Kitchen Safety, Pool Safety, Bike Safety, and Home Escape Plan.
  7. ####  [Request a Speaker or Presentation](https://www.anaheim.net/859/Request-a-Speaker-or-Presentation)
The Anaheim Fire and Rescue Department has staff and volunteer speakers available for your next community meeting or event. 
  8. ####  [Station Locations](https://www.anaheim.net/650/Station-Locations)
Access a map that displays the location of all of the Anaheim Fire Stations.
  9. ####  [Request](https://www.anaheim.net/2280/Request)
A page where you can find links to request Fire & Rescue's programs such as participating in your community event, school program, or you can even tour a fire station!
  10. ####  [Tradeshows & Special Event Information](https://www.anaheim.net/3394/Tradeshows-Special-Event-Information)
Having a Special Event of Tradeshow in our City? Download important information, permits and instructions here. 
  11. ####  [Pay Permit Fees](https://www.anaheim.net/6035/Pay-Permit-Fees)
With our new Accela program, we are offering new features to provide you with easier access, convenient payment options, and real-time status updates on your records.

#  Community Info 
9
1
1
2
  1. ####  [Anaheim Anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/2128/Anaheim-Anytime)
Anaheim Anytime is the place to get answers to your questions.
  2. ####  [Anaheim 311](https://www.anaheim.net/189/_311)
Anaheim 311 is a non-emergency hotline that allows residents, businesses and visitors to contact Anaheim City Hall through 1 easy to remember phone number, 311.
  3. ####  [Andy’s Updates Newsletter](https://www.anaheim.net/1725/Anaheim-Newsletter)
Anaheim's monthly email newsletter lets residents know what is happening in Anaheim.
  4. ####  [City Council meetings](https://www.anaheim.net/2527/Agendas)
This is the City Council meetings video page where you can watch a live City Council meeting or go back and watch an older meeting.
  5. ####  [City news](https://www.anaheim.net/1737/News-Media-Center)
See news events, updates and announcements from the city.
  6. ####  [City of Kindness](https://www.anaheim.net/6359/City-of-Kindness)

  8. ####  [Emergency Preparedness](https://www.anaheim.net/847/Emergency-Management-Preparedness)
Emergency preparedness is a process that protects communities from hazards, or threats to life or property. 
  9. ####  [Family and Youth Programs](https://www.anaheim.net/917/Programs-Assisting-Youth-Families-Senior)
Know the program offerings for family, seniors and youth in the community.
  10. ####  [Active Older Adults](https://www.anaheim.net/6576/)
Learn about the activities that encourage both physical and social engagement for active older adults aged 55 and over and of all abilities.
  11. ####  [Parks](https://www.anaheim.net/916/Parks-Facilities)
Links to intro page about parks
  12. ####  [Street Sweeping](https://www.anaheim.net/480/Street-Sweeping)
All streets will be swept on a weekly basis, weather permitting.
  13. ####  [Trash Collection](https://www.anaheim.net/2218/Find-Trash-Collection-and-Street-Sweepin)
Know your trash collection schedule
  14. ####  [Visit Anaheim](https://visitanaheim.org/)
Peruse options for lodging, food, and events during your visit to Anaheim and Orange County. 
  15. ####  [Welcoming Anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/4863/Welcoming-Anaheim)
Anaheim is a welcoming city that hosts people from around the world and is also proudly home to people from all over.

  6. Digging Safely


#  Digging Safely 
Before digging on your property or in the sidewalk / street area for your project such as a new pool in the yard or replacing your sewer pipe, you or your contractor must first call [**DigAlert**](https://newtinb.digalert.org/direct/) or call **811** or **800-422-4133.** DigAlert is a free service that would then contact the utility companies to mark your work area for their underground lines. This would avoid fines of up to $100,000, costly damage repairs to those lines, and significant injury. 
Please note that we will only mark our electric, water, and utility-owned fiber optics lines that we own and maintain (usually up to the meters), and will not mark any private lines, such as: lines going to fire pits, BBQs, pool heaters, spas, etc. Other utility companies including natural gas, telephone, or internet will be responsible for marking their lines.
We mark our lines using paint, chalk, flags or whiskers using a standard color code shown below. If the marks are near or through your work area, you are required by law to dig by hand, 24 inches on both sides of the actual line to know its exact location and depth before using any power equipment. 
![Digging Street](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45203)
![Marking Street](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45202)
### Standard Color Code
![Digging Color Key](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=45170)
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

  6. Bulky Item Collection


#  Bulky Item Collection 
[![Graphic to click for more information on bulky item pickups Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57658)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57657/Bulky-item-flyer-Oct-2024)
### Did You Miss Your Annual Neighborhood Clean-up?
No worries! Anaheim offers a year-round solution for disposing of large items like furniture, appliances, and more, through our FREE bulky item pickup service provided by Republic Services. 
Each household is entitled to three (3) bulky item pickups per calendar year at no cost, with collections limited to twenty (20) items per pick up. Simply prepare a list of the items you need hauled away, and on the scheduled pick-up date, move them to the curb for collection**.** Please note, that Republic Services does not enter homes to retrieve items. 
### How do I Use this Service? 
To use this service, schedule an appointment by contacting Republic Services Customer Service at (714) 238-2444 or visiting [www.anaheim.net/republicservices](https://www.anaheim.net/republicservices). Bulky item collections can be arranged consecutively, but please schedule pickups prior to your move-out date, as this service is not intended for emergency removals. Once your three annual pick-ups are used, they renew at the start of the following calendar year.
For abandoned bulky items on public property, such as alleys, streets or sidewalks, contact 3-1-1 or file an [Anaheim Anytime Request](https://www.anaheim.net/2671/Anaheim-Anytime-Request), and our Public Works Department will coordinate with Republic Services for pick-up. This service does not affect your household's designated annual pick-ups. Check out the other services your city provides for [solid waste and recycling.](https://www.anaheim.net/474/Solid-Waste-Recycling)
[![community services interior logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42964)](https://www.anaheim.net/6094)

#  Business Development 
The Business Development team provides high-level customer service. We can help current and new Anaheim businesses quickly and efficiently by coordinating efforts with departments across our city to guarantee rapid responses to business questions and issues.
Our team can help connect you with various city partners and their resources, including Planning & Building, Public Works, Anaheim Public Utilities, Housing and Community Development, Anaheim Fire & rescue and the Anaheim Police Department.
**We'll help:**
  * Understand your business needs
  * Utilize existing internal and external partners and their resources
  * And help align your business needs with those resources  
  



**We can help start and grow your business with:**
  * Site selection assistance
  * Business concierge service
  * Assistance with workforce development
  * Streamlined permit assistance
  * Utility incentive programs
  * Planning application assistance
  * Business license assistance


**Let us help you with your business needs!**

#  Electric Vehicles 
### Anaheim Public Utilities offers EV rebates and programs to support our customer's path towards electrification. Whether you are new to the process or need hands-on support with your site plans, we're here to help you every step of the way. 
###   

### For more information about Anaheim Public Utilities' future adoption of EV’s in our community, please review our [Integrated Resources Plan](https://www.anaheim.net/4864) and [Southern California Association of Governments study](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50587/APU-Southern-California-Association-of-Governments-study).
###   

### Contact us by email or call 714-765-4250.

  4. Sergio M. Ramirez, Director Economic Development


#  Sergio M. Ramirez, Director Economic Development 
![Sergio Ramirez_Headshot](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=66222)
**Mr. Sergio M. Ramirez** is director of Economic Development for the City of Anaheim, where he has served since August 2021. The newly formed Department was established by the City Council on April 13, 2021.
Mr. Ramirez plays a leading role in once-in-a-generation investment and development planned around the [](https://www.hondacenter.com/)[**Honda Center**](https://www.hondacenter.com/)**,** the [](https://www.theanaheimstadium.com/)[**Angel Stadium**](https://www.theanaheimstadium.com/)**,** the [](https://disneyland.disney.go.com/)[**Disneyland Resort**](https://disneyland.disney.go.com/)**,** and throughout the City.   
  
He is leading revitalization efforts on Beach Boulevard, Brookhurst Street, Euclid Street, State College Boulevard, and other major corridors across the city.  
  
In the Anaheim Canyon, Ramirez is enhancing the 2,600-acre business park with a diverse range of business clusters focused around industry and innovation. Recently, he has begun implementation of a new branding and signage campaign for the area, as an Innovation District. 
Mr. Ramirez previously served as the Deputy Director for property and economic development for the City of Long Beach, where he worked on $5 billion in housing, retail, and industrial projects. This included the 2nd and PCH retail center, redevelopment in Belmont Shore, the Shoreline Gateway Tower residential project, Shoreline Village redevelopment, and the redevelopment of Boeing's former Long Beach site with the attraction businesses satellite launch and other innovative uses.
Before joining Long Beach in January 2017, Mr. Ramirez served as senior project manager and project manager II for the City of Anaheim's Community and Economic Development Department from 2004 to 2016.
Mr. Ramirez holds a Masters Degree in Public Administration from the California State University Los Angeles, and a Bachelors of Science in Urban and Regional Planning from the California State Polytechnic University, Pomona. 
He is actively involved in professional groups including the Urban Land Institute (ULI), the International Council of Shopping Centers (ICSC), and the National Association of Industrial and office Properties (NAIOP).
Contact information:  
Phone number: (714) 765-4627  
Email: SRamirez@anaheim.net

  2. [Contact Economic Development Department](https://www.anaheim.net/6436/Contact-Economic-Development-Department)

#  Legislative Impacts on Utilities 
Each year, thousands of new bills are introduced as part of the state and federal legislative process, with many having the potential to affect Anaheim Public Utilities’ operations and rates.
Even though Anaheim Public Utilities operates on a not-for-profit basis, many legislative bills still result in increased costs to provide electric service in California. In fact, recent and pending legislative mandates are expected to continue to increase Electric service costs into the future.
However, more and more legislative and regulatory mandates, as well as their associated costs, remain beyond the control of our local policy body. Instead, they are controlled by state and federal policy makers, and even though many of our local Orange County legislators urge their peers to consider the costs when implementing new legislation, many bills are still passed through the legislature that will ultimately raise the cost of both electric and water services.
## Affordable Electric and Water Rates
Anaheim residents still enjoy affordable electric and water rates in comparison to other Orange County cities. Our focus is to maximize customer benefits and to minimize any negative impacts that proposed legislation may have on your rates. Our agency is municipally-owned and our budget and policies are reviewed by the Public Utilities Board and approved by Anaheim City Council.
![CA state capital](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31523)
![CA State Flag Capital](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=31522)

#  Stephen Stoewer, Senior Project Manager 
![Stephen Stoewer, Senior Project Manager SM](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=47004)
**Mr. Stephen Stoewer** , as a City of Anaheim employee for over 22 years, is dedicated and passionate towards helping residents improve their quality of life. Mr. Stoewer has many roles for the Economic Development department, his responsibilities inlcude; Successor Agency Property Management, Environmental Assessment and Remediation of Contaminated Properties, Capital Improvement Projects, Parking Management, and Property Management. Mr. Stoewer is passionate about cleaning up contaminated properties for future residential and commercial development that will ultimately improve our resident’s quality of life.   
  
Contact information:
Phone: (714) 765-4338   
Email: SStoewer@anaheim.net

#  Commercial - Rebates & Programs 
![EV Truck \(1\)](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=47484)
## Rebate Program
| 
## Description
| 
## Amounts  
---|---|---  
[**Public Access EV Charger Rebate**](https://www.anaheim.net/3312/) | Rebates for the installation of Level 2 or higher plug-in EV chargers that are made available for the general public to use.  |  Up to $3,500 per Level 2 charger for commercial sites, up to maximum of $25,000  
  
Up to $7,500 per charger for schools, affordable housing properties, and DC Fast Chargers, up to a maximum of $40,000  
  
 _Rebates are subject to fund availability._  
[**Private Use EV Charger Rebate**](https://www.anaheim.net/593/) | Rebates for the installation of EV chargers that are for private use at business sites.  | Up to $300 for the Level 2 charger and   
  
Up to $600 for the networked charger on a time-based rate  
  
 _Rebates are subject to fund availability._  
[**EV Fleet**](https://www.anaheim.net/5889/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate)[**Charger**](https://www.anaheim.net/5889/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate)[**Rebate**](https://www.anaheim.net/5889/EV-Fleet-Charger-Infrastructure-Rebate) | Rebates for fleet operators to electrify their fleets. | Up to $5,000 per charger and infrastructure costs for commercial fleet, up to a maximum of $75,000  
  
Up to $10,000 per charger and infrastructure costs for schools, up to a maximum of $75,000  
  
 _Rebates are subject to fund availability._  
[**Municipal EV Charger Assistance Program**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50016/Municipal-EV-Charger-Assistance-Program-Application--Fillable) | Rebates for City of Anaheim facilities that install EV chargers. Subject to application review.  | Up to $10,000 per charger and infrastructure costs for municipal customers, up to a maximum of $50,000  
  
 _Rebates are subject to fund availability._

  6. Residential EV Charging


#  Residential EV Charging Guide 
## EV Charger Types
Charging hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) and all-electric vehicles (EVs) requires plugging into an Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE).  
  
Charging times vary based on how empty the battery is, how much energy it holds, battery type, and the type of charging equipment used. EV Chargers may require power infrastructure upgrades.
There are two residential categories of EV chargers, based on the amount of power the charger can provide:
![120v Wall Outlet](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49235)
![Level 2 Charger](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49246)
## Level 1 - 120 volt outlet
  * Plug into a standard 120 volt (V) wall outlet; alternating current (AC) plug up to 15 amperes and 1.8 kW
  * Requires a dedicated circuit
  * Portable, does not require installation 
  * Cord is a standard 3-prong household plug with a connector to the vehicle
  * Slowest charging time between 8 - 20 hours
  * Standard outlet: NEM 5-15R


## Level 2 - 240 volt outlet
  * Uses 240 volt(V), alternating current (AC) plug up to 80 amperes and 19.2 kW
  * Requires installation
  * Most residential level 2 EVSE will operate at lower power, using 30 amperes and deliver 7.2 kW of power
  * Charging time take 3 - 8 hours and can be done overnight
  * Most common in US for residential homes and business use
  * Standard outlet: SAE J1442/3


## Equipment, Connectors & Plugs
###   

For a list of charging units and other accessories, view[ Plug Shares home charging equipment options.](https://plugstar.com/chargers)
  * Modern charging equipment and vehicles use a stand connector and plug receptacle based on the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) J1772 standard. Vehicles with this plug receptacle can use any Level 1 or Level 2 EVSE. Major vehicle and charging system manufacturers support this standard, which should eliminate drivers’ concerns about whether their vehicle is compatible with the infrastructure.
  * In the US, Combined Charging System (CCS) and CHAdeMO are the two standard connector types for Level 3 (DC fast charging) EVSE.
  * Tesla chargers deviate from these standards, but adapters are available so that Tesla drivers can use standard, non-Tesla connectors.


## Permitting & Electrical Steps
[Residential EV Charger Installation Checklist ](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/47969/EV-Charger-Checklist)for a list of permitting and electrical steps.
### Contact Us

#  Kristine Gaa, Property Development Consultant 
![Kristine Gaa 2asm](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=53671)
**Ms. Kristine Gaa** has over 15 years of high-level experience in Communications and Business Development. She has extensive experience with local, state, and federal grant and funding use. Ms. Gaa brings her strength in property analysis, project management, and program administration to the City’s Economic Development Department for real estate and business development projects as needed. She is well-versed in PowerPoint, Word, Excel, and InDesign, with experience in multi-media document and website creation. Ms. Gaa has excellent analytical, interpersonal, leadership, and communication skills.
Ms. Gaa has a Bachelor of Science Degree in Urban and Regional Planning.
Contact information:
Phone: (714) 765-5100 Ext. 4786  
Email: KGaa@anaheim.net
  
  

  1. [Stephen Stoewer, Senior Project Manager](https://www.anaheim.net/6321/Stephen-Stoewer-Senior-Project-Manager)
  2. [Kristine Gaa, Property Development Consultant](https://www.anaheim.net/6324/Kristine-Gaa-Property-Development-Consul)

![Storefront improvement header copy](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=47270)
# The Storefront Improvement Program has now concluded
We saw **strong interest** in the program, and we thank everyone for wanting to improve the look of businesses in Anaheim.
We will continue to **evaluate reopening the program should funding become available**. 
If you applied, you will receive an **email confirming your application**. We received many applications and will be thoroughly **reviewing all of them over the coming weeks**.
If you have **questions** , please email us at crehab@anaheim.net.
While the program has closed, you can find **full program guidelines below for reference.**

#  ADU Express Process 
[![ADUAnaheim Logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48517)](https://www.anaheim.net/6349/ADUheim-Landing-Page)
# ADU Express Process
# _A New ADU Review Experience_
##  STEP 1: VERIFY ELIGIBILITY
  * ### Check your property’s eligibility for ADU Express by searching your property address on the [**ADU Express Eligibility Tool**](https://gis.anaheim.net/portal/apps/instant/lookup/index.html?appid=70092ca40660457db6f60cfae2884600).
  * ### If you are eligible, congratulations! Go to Step #2 to Continue the process!
  * ### Not eligible for ADU Express? Questions about alternatives? Visit the [**ADU Anaheim landing page**](https://www.anaheim.net/6349/) for information on the standard Building Division permit review and approval process. If you have any questions, email us at **ADUAnaheim@anaheim.net**.


##  STEP 2: SELECT A PRE-APPROVED ADU PLAN 
  * ### All potential plans for ADU Express can be found on the ADU [**Pre-Approved Plan Catalogue Page**](https://www.anaheim.net/6351/). Review the various sizes, options, and images and decide what you believe will work best for you. 
  * ### The "Notes" column of the catalogue will provide you with important information about each plan set. 
  * ### Once you have selected a pre-approved plan, move on to Step 3. 


##  STEP 3: CONTACT CITY STAFF 
  * ###  **THIS IS IMPORTANT** : Once you pick your plan, **contact Anaheim Public Utilities at (714) 765-6847** to schedule a free “meter spot”. This process helps you plan for the additional electrical needs of your new ADU. When you call for a meter spot, please request the folowing: "**A 400 AMP, Multi-Meter Location**."
    * ### Please note: Staff cannot meet to create a site plan until you obtain this information from Anaheim Public Utilities. Usually, the meter spot site visit occurs within three business days of contacting Anaheim Public Utilities. 
  * ### Once you have received your meter spot report from Anaheim Public Utilities, please contact Planning and Building staff at **ADUAnaheim@Anaheim.net** to provide the Meter Spot and pre-approved plan number you wish to move forward with. Staff will then review and provide any additional documents to be completed.


##  STEP 4: MEET WITH CITY STAFF
  * ### Staff will continue to update you as the final review and approvals are completed. Once all aspects of the application receive preliminary approval, staff will schedule a meeting with you in City Hall to create your Site Plan, review construction requirements, and answer any questions you have. 


##  STEP 5: AS PART OF THE APPLICATION REVIEW
  * ### All ADU Express materials will be uploaded into the online portal. Staff will assist with this during our in-person meeting. It is through the online portal that you will download and print your construction documents. 


##  STEP 6: BUILDING DIVISION REVIEW AND APPROVAL
  * ### Following your in-person meeting, the Building Division will provide information on applicable development impact and/or school districts fees. All fees must be paid prior to building permit issuance. You will be provided information on which school district to pay (there are six in Anaheim) and where they are located.
  * ### Once you return from paying necessary school fees, staff should be able to issue your approved building permit, all within one day!


##  STEP 7: PERMITS AND INSPECTIONS
  * ### Inspections will be required throughout the construction process and at varying stages. Please visit [**www.Anaheim.net/Next**](http://www.Anaheim.net/Next) to utilize the City's automated system to schedule inspections.


  

###  To learn more about ADUs in Anaheim, visit  [www.Anaheim.net/ADUAnaheim](http://www.Anaheim.net/ADUAnaheim)
  

**Contact Us**
Send us an e-mail for any questions or comments.
  1. [ADU Anaheim Landing Page](https://www.anaheim.net/6349/Accessory-Dwelling-Units-ADUs)
  2. [Frequently Asked Questions](https://www.anaheim.net/6356/Frequently-Asked-Questions)
  3. [ADU Express Process](https://www.anaheim.net/6350/ADU-Express-Process)
  4. [ADU Express Application](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49140/)
  5. [Pre-Approved Plan Catalogue](https://www.anaheim.net/6351/Pre-Approved-Plan-Catalogue)

  5. Senior Safety Net Program


## Senior Safety Net Program
The Senior Safety Net Program offers Anaheim seniors facing hardships assistance with meeting housing costs.
The program offers case management, emergency assistance, and rental assistance to seniors who qualify for this program. Program participation is on a first come, first served basis. Funds for this program are limited and assistance is not guaranteed. For more information about these services, please call (714) 765-4300.
  * ### **Emergency Assistance**
    * Age Requirements - Must be at least 55 years old 
    * Program Information
      * [Prospective Client Guide](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57247/Anaheim-Senior-Safety-Net-Program-Emergency-Assistance---PROSPECTIVE-APPLICANT-GUIDE---English)
  * ### **Tenant Based Rental Assistance**
    * Age Requirements - Must be at least 62 years old
    * Program Information
      * [Prospective Client Guide](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57248/Anaheim-Senior-Safety-Net-Program-Tenant-Based-Rental-Assistance---PROSPECTIVE-APPLICANT-GUIDE----English)


##   

## Programa de Red de Seguridad para Personas Mayores
El Programa Senior Safety Net ofrece a las personas mayores de edad residentes de Anaheim que enfrentan dificultades asistencia para cubrir los costos de vivienda.
El programa ofrece administración de casos, asistencia de emergencia y alquiler basada en inquilinos a personas mayores que califican para este programa. La participación en el programa se otorga por orden de llegada. Los fondos para este programa son limitados y la asistencia no está garantizada. Para más información sobre estos servicios. Para obtener más información sobre estos servicios, llame al (714) 765-4300.
  * ### **Asistencia de Emergencia**
    * Requisitos de edad: debe tener al menos 55 años de edad.
    * Información del programa
      * [GuÍa para Solicantes](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57249/Anaheim-Senior-Safety-Net-Program-Emergency-Assistance---GUIA-PARA-SOLICITANTES---Spanish)
  * ### **Asistencia de Alquiler Basada en Inquilinos**
    * Requisitos de edad: debe tener al menos 62 años de edad.
    * Información del programa
      * [GuÍa para Solicantes](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/57250/Anaheim-Senior-Safety-Net-Program-Tenant-Based-Rental-Assistance---GUIA-PARA-SOLICITANTES---Spanish)

#  Pre-Approved Plan Catalogue 
[![ADUAnaheim Logo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=48517)](https://www.anaheim.net/6349/ADUheim-Landing-Page)
### Congratulations on deciding to move forward with an ADU Express project on your property! View the approved plans below to decide which option works for you. Sample elevations and floor plans for each plan type is below the table. 
###   

### Are you eligible for ADU Express? If so, please select your plan and email us at **ADUAnaheim@anaheim.net** to provide your address, your selected plan number, and a copy of your meter spot. From there, staff will conduct a screening process to verify eligibility, and if additional information is needed to move forward. This process can take a few weeks to complete, and we will update you throughout this process. 
###   

### Check back often, as we will continue to add additional plans.
###   

### *Please note that all plans include Building Code requirements that come into effect on July 1, 2024. 
Plan Reference Number | ADU Details | Link to View Full Plans | Additional Information  
---|---|---|---  
Plan 001 | Studio224 sf | [Link](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49352/) | Free  
Plan 002 | 1 bedroom1 bathroom 499 sf | [Link](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49353/) | Free  
Plan 003 | 2 bedrooms2 bathrooms 990 sf | [Link](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49354/) | Free  
Plan 004 | 3 bedrooms3 bathroom 1,199 sf | [Link](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/49355/) | Free  
###  Are you a **vendor **who would like to apply to add your plans to this list? Email us at **********ADUAnaheim@anaheim.net******for more information.

  6. Solar Energy and Net Metering


#  Solar Energy and Net Metering 
Installing solar panels is a big decision for a resident. Anaheim Public Utilities is here to help you go solar at your property. If you are planning to install solar panels at your residence, please ensure your system is properly permitted by the City’s Planning and Building Department and meets interconnection requirements from Anaheim Public Utilities. 
Our current [NEM 2.0](https://www.anaheim.net/5587) program is a wholesale-based rate NEM program. View our rates for excess energy [here](https://www.anaheim.net/5768). Anaheim is not going to NEM 3.0. 
**Important Note**  
Early NEM 1.0 programs in California were intended to encourage private investment in solar and to increase the amount of renewable energy used in California. While early NEM 1.0 programs were successful in meeting these goals, they also had a tendency to shift the cost of maintaining the local grid to non-solar customers. Anaheim Public Utilities is a not-for-profit municipal utility and as such, is required to recover our cost of maintaining the local grid and purchasing power on behalf of all customers. NEM 1.0 customers who are grandfathered will not be placed on NEM 2.0 unless they seek to expand their solar panels, at which time, customers will be placed solely on NEM 2.0 for their entire system.
### Here are the steps to installing solar for your Anaheim home:
  1. **Right size your PV system by ensuring your home is as energy efficient as possible.**
  2. **Know how much solar energy your home needs.**  
Obtain your annual energy usage by logging into your account online to check your past 12 months of energy needs. If you don’t have online billing, please call customer service at 714-765-3300.
  3. [**Understand how net energy metering (NEM) 2.0 works.**](https://www.anaheim.net/5587)  
Our 2.0 program is a wholesale-based program. [See the rate sheet for excess energy under NEM 2.0](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/34968/NEM-20-Excess-Energy-Rate-Sheet-?bidId=)
  4. **Decide on ways to install solar: Purchase, Lease, or Power Purchase Agreement.**  
Understand the different ways to go solar for your home.
  5. **Obtain contractor bids from at least three (3) solar contractors.**
  6. **Contractor will submit engineering plans to[Anaheim Planning & Building](https://www.anaheim.net/5252) online for review.**   
If under 10kW, the permit will be issued without review.
  7. **After building permit is issued, contractor will build the solar PV system.**
  8. ****Customer can operate the system after** Anaheim Planning & Building signs off on the building permit. **Anaheim Public Utilities does not issue the Permission to Operate.


![NEM](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=32678)
### Contact Us
  1. ## Solar Program 
  
714-765-4182 or Email

  1. [Home](https://www.anaheim.net/)
  2. [Departments](https://www.anaheim.net/148/Departments)
  3. [City Administration](https://www.anaheim.net/149/City-Administration)
  4. [Communications ](https://www.anaheim.net/151/Communications)
  5. City of Kindness


![kindness](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=14494)
City of Kindness is Anaheim's adopted motto and a way of creating a better quality of life in our city as well as resiliency in the face of natural disasters and other challenges.
kindness is a simple but powerful concept. It seeks to forge a stronger city by connecting people, neighbors and neighborhoods and by building a shared sense of community.
That can have a positive impact on the challenges our city and many others face, including crime, bullying, senior neglect and alcohol and drug abuse.
A city connected through kindness also is more resilient. At its core, kindness is a way of promoting community resiliency and partnership in the face of earthquakes, wildfires and other disasters.
Anaheim's City of Kindness motto has its roots in the tragic loss of a young resident.
Natasha Jaievsky lost her life in a 2002 car accident. After her passing, her family learned how Natasha constantly wrote and talked about kindness, leaving behind drawings and writings about kindness in her bedroom closet and drawers.
The family went on to honor Natasha's legacy with “Make Kindness Contagious” banners displayed around Anaheim.
In 2017, Anaheim's City Council adopted City of Kindness as a motto.

#  2025 Qualifying Income Levels 
Household Size | Household Income  
---|---  
**1** | **$94,750**  
**2** | **$108,300**  
**3** | **$121,850**  
**4** | **$135,350**  
**5** | **$146,200**  
**6** | **$157,050**  
**7** | **$167,850**  
**8** | **$178,700**  
### Information per Department of Housing & Urban Development. Based on 80 percent of the Orange County Median Family Income Level, which is adjusted annually.
