#  Link Labs 
![Makerspace_Website banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=65110)
## Welcome to the Link Labs
a collaborative environment designed to foster creativity, innovation, and exploration. Here, your Anaheim Public Library card connects you to our Makerspace, Media Lab, and Memory Lab—all equipped with advanced technology to help you develop new skills and bring your ideas to life.
## Free for Anaheim Public Library card holders 13 & up
[Visit any branch to sign up for a library card.](https://anaheim.net/903/Locations-Hours)
## All experience levels welcome
First-time visitors will receive an orientation to our space and equipment.
## Available by appointment only
Schedule your first orientation with staff.   
[![makerspace Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=65111)](https://www.anaheim.net/6668/Makerspace) [![Medialab Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=65112)](https://www.anaheim.net/6669/Media-Lab) [![memory lab Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=65113)](https://www.anaheim.net/6670/Memory-Lab)   

## [Schedule an Appointment](https://www.anaheim.net/6671/Request-a-Link-Labs-Appointment)
| 
## ![Asset 7linklabscircle](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=64682)
##   

## Link Labs
Haskett Branch  
2650 W Broadway  
Anaheim, CA 92804  
  
linklabs@anaheim.net  
(714) 765-5077   
  
---|---  
# FAQS
  1. [Who can use the Link Labs?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_0)


The Link Labs is open to all Anaheim Public Library cardholders 13 and up. Parent/guardian permission is required for cardholders ages 13-17. Children 12 and under must always be accompanied by a parent or guardian at the same station in the labs; children cannot use a station by themselves. The computers in this space are for Link Labs projects only; patrons needing computers for general use may utilize the Adult, Teen, and Children's computer labs as appropriate. 
  1. [Where is the Link Labs?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_1)


The Link Labs is located at Haskett Branch, 2650 W Broadway, Anaheim, CA 92804.
The Link Makerspace can be accessed through the double doors across from the Check-Out desk. The Link Media Lab is located to the right of the red tunnel leading to our Children's library. The Link Memory Lab is located inside the Link Media Lab.
  1. [How do I get started?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_2)


## Available by appointment only.
**Plan ahead.** [First-time users must complete the Orientation to the Link Labs as well as a corresponding Equipment Orientation](https://www.anaheim.net/6671/Request-a-Link-Labs-Appointment), regardless of skill level. In-person orientations are scheduled per staff availability and will be conducted during the first 15-30 minutes of a reservation. 
Once you are a member of the Link Labs, you can reserve any station for which you’ve completed an Equipment Orientation. Reservations may be made at least 48 hours in advance. You may reserve a station for two (2) hours per reservation per day. Contact staff for more information.
  1. [Do I need training or experience? ](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_3)


No experience is required. [Equipment Orientations](https://www.anaheim.net/6671/Request-a-Link-Labs-Appointment) include a basic introduction to the station and how to start your first project. If you would like more guidance, we will be offering classes soon: [Check out our branch calendar](https://anaheim.net/library). We also have many resources in our physical and digital collections. 
  1. [Will you have classes?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_4)


We offer drop-in programs bi-monthly on Saturdays from 11 AM to 3 PM. No orientation or registration is required. [Check out our branch calendar for more information. ](https://anaheim.libcal.com/calendar/haskett)
  1. [How much does it cost to use the Link Labs?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_5)


The Link Labs is available with an Anaheim Public Library card, which is free for California Residents. [Learn more about obtaining a library card here.](https://anaheim.net/1229/Get-Started)
  1. [Do you provide materials?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_6)


## Bring your own materials.
The Link Makerspace has a limited amount of materials available free to use for patrons. Monthly limits may apply. Otherwise, you may bring in your own materials that are on the staff-approved list. Other materials may be denied use in the lab unless or until staff can verify its safe handling. Please contact staff for a complete list of approved materials. 
  1. [How can I get updates for the Link Labs?](https://www.anaheim.net/6445/Link-Labs#tab95acc396-bc5b-46ce-8270-b0d67f0aaf89_7)


Sign up for our newsletter for all Anaheim Public Library updates, or follow our social media channels.
# About the Link Labs
## Our Mission 
The Link Labs is a hub for innovation, education, and collaboration. Our aim is to make advanced skills accessible by connecting library patrons to cutting-edge technology, as well as resources and education guided by experienced creators. We encourage learning from process and practice that becomes experience for developing new skills. Whether you are an aspiring hobbyist or a dedicated maker, all are welcome in this space to take creative risks, solve problems, and learn something new. 
## Our Vision 
With the help of the Link Labs, library patrons will feel empowered to utilize technology and seek information to reach their project goals. The open working space and its resources will provide new grounds for people to connect to technology, experienced creators, and each other. By dedicating a space to collaboration and creativity, new and diverse voices in the community can take a step forward to honor the past, shape the present, and envision the future.
[![Library Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42940)](https://www.anaheim.net/6100)

#  Commercial Rates 
Did you know that the time of day in which you recharge your electric vehicle (EV) can impact your electricity bill? Energy used during peak demand (4 p.m. to 9 p.m.) is the most expensive time for Anaheim Public Utilities to buy energy, because that's when most people get home from work or school and use electricity. Simple habit changes can help you reduce costs, and so can the right rate plan.   
  
So, when should you recharge your EV?  
  
**The best time to recharge your EV is during oﬀ-peak hours.**
Check out the video below to learn more how a Time-Of-Use (TOU) plan can help you lower costs.
![EV Clock](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49632)
  1. [Non-Domestic (commercial) ev time-of-use (tou) Rate (Option A)](https://www.anaheim.net/6440/Commercial-Rates#tab35de2a53-7a90-43f3-aafa-77923c802f8a_0)


This TOU rate plan is for commercial business who have unique electric vehicle charging infrastructure. This rate is for electric vehicle charging on a separate meter only. 
![Weekday Peaks](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49663)
  

  
| Summer (July 1 - October 31) | Winter (November 1 - June 30)  
---|---|---  
Monthly Customer Charge | $37.05 | $37.05  
Energy Charge (added to Customer Charge) |   
|   
  
All on-peak energy, per kWh  
| 30.88 ¢ | N/A  
Plus all mid-peak energy, per kWh | 13.75 ¢ | 14.52 ¢  
Plus all off-peak energy, per kWh | 9.91 ¢ | 9.91 ¢  
Minimum Charge | The minimum charge is the monthly Customer Charge.  
[*Developmental-Non-Domestic-Electric-Vehicles (anaheim.net)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/20547/Developmental-Non-Domestic-Electric-Vehicles)
  1. [NON-DOMESTIC (COMMERCIAL) EV time-of-use (TOU) Rate (OPTION B) ](https://www.anaheim.net/6440/Commercial-Rates#tab35de2a53-7a90-43f3-aafa-77923c802f8a_1)


This TOU rate plan is for commercial business who have unique electric vehicle charging infrastructure. This rate is for electric vehicle charging on a separate meter only. 
![Weekday Peaks](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49663)
  
| Summer (July 1 - October 31) | Winter (November 1 - June 30)  
---|---|---  
Monthly Customer Charge | $37.05 | $37.05  
Demand Charge (added to Customer Charge) |   
|   
  
Non-time related Maximum Demand, per kW | $8.00 | $8.00  
Plus all on-peak Billing Demand, per kW | $10.94 | N/A  
Plus all mid-peak Billing Demand, per kW | $5.06 | $6.07  
Plus all off-peak Billing Demand, per kW | N/A | N/A  
Energy Charge (added to Demand Charge) |   
|   
  
All on-peak energy, per kWh | 26.34 ¢ | N/A  
Plus all mid-peak energy, per kWh  
| 9.21 ¢ | 9.98 ¢  
Plus all off-peak energy, per kWh  
| 5.37 ¢ | 5.37 ¢  
Minimum charge   
| The minimum charge is the monthly non-time related Demand Charge and the monthly Customer Charge

#  Wire Theft and Vandalism 
![theft banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51087)
Wire theft and vandalism are on the rise. This is a serious issue impacting the community and Anaheim Public Utilities needs your help to identify theft and report illegal activity. 
It is important to note that non-operational streetlights may have exposed wires near the base, and nearby electrical box lids may be removed. Those involved in the theft of wire may be wearing construction clothing and operating unbranded vehicles. Beware of suspicious individuals in the act of removing materials and causing damage to the light.
**If you see suspicious activity or a theft in progress, DO NOT ENGAGE! Call 714-765-1900.**
To report damaged or non-working streetlights:
  * Request streetlight repair through the [My Anaheim app](https://www.anaheim.net/1409/My-Anaheim-App)
  * Submit a concern through the [Anaheim Anytime website](https://www.anaheim.net/2128)
  * Call 714-765-3300


We appreciate your patience during this time. Wiring repairs may take several weeks depending on the extent of the damage.
Anaheim Public Utilities is using different methods of reducing theft issues such as solar-powered lighting solutions, tamper-proof electrical boxes, utilizing wires made with non-copper materials, and collaborating with the Anaheim Police Department on theft attempt trends.
  1. ![theft 01](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51093)
  2. ![theft 02](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51094)
  3. ![theft 03](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51095)


  1. [ 1 theft 01 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)
  2. [ 2 theft 02 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)
  3. [ 3 theft 03 ](https://www.anaheim.net/6463/Wire-Theft-and-Vandalism#targetImage)


## How can you help?  
  

  * Call 714-765-1900 to report theft in progress – **DO NOT ENGAGE**
  * Report street light outages and vandalism through:
    * 714-765-3300
    * [My Anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/1409/My-Anaheim-App)
    * [Anaheim Anytime](https://www.anaheim.net/2128)

![Filmstrip banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49721)
# Brookhurst Corridor Study
We are working with our community to create a **better Brookhurst** Street. 
The 3.5-mile corridor through west Anaheim is a vibrant mix of restaurants and businesses serving a diverse population and distinct cultures.
Along with the businesses of Brookhurst, the street includes nearby neighborhoods, a community center, schools, parks and places of worship, with a mix of old and new. 
Brookhurst is also home to **Little Arabia** , a culturally unique area officially recognized by the Anaheim City Council.
Little Arabia is a collection of restaurants, shops and other businesses built by immigrants from the Arab world and the greater Middle East since the 1980s. Spanning roughly a mile of Brookhurst from Broadway to about Ball Road, Little Arabia is a favorite for Mediterranean food and other culturally unique businesses.
# Read our initial study recommendations
[![Brookhurst Corridor Study: initial report and recommendations Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=52556)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/52555/Brookhurst-Corridor-Study-initial-recommendations-12-12-23)
# About the study
From spring to late 2023, the study looked at how best to **advance Brookhurst for everyone** now and in the years to come.
The **first phase** covered issues and opportunities related to how land along Brookhurst is used, getting around, parking, signs and other ways to improve the street. The first phase also studied the recently designated Little Arabia district and looked at whether the district's initial boundaries should be updated.
We saw **extensive community input**. We set out to better understand community needs, goals and visions for the corridor. The feedback was used to develop recommendations for the city to consider related to land use regulations, public improvements, funding, identity and more.
A community working group was created and helped serve as the conduit between the residents and businesses along Brookhurst and the city.
A series of community events, such as pop-ups, walking tours and open houses were hosted to learn more about the study and offer feedback.
The first phase culminated in a workshop before the Anaheim City Council in December 2023.
# See the Brookhurst presentation
[![City Council presentation 12-12-23 Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=52557)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/52558/City-Council-presentation-12-12-23)
# Second Phase
The **second phase** of the Brookhurst Corridor Study will play out in 2024 with formal consideration of recommendations by the Anaheim City Council.
The City Council is expected to formally look at adopting recommendations in early 2024.
From there, the study will serve as a guide for how Brookhurst develops in the years and even decades to come.
# Questions
Pease email Nick Gomez or Stephen Stoewer on the Brookhurst team at the city of Anaheim.
[![workshop](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=52619)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xXHZidbKF38&list=PLuUnHMNg1Qf5XXA4YENQBNiGUSrs_4ZIX&index=1)

#  GHAD watering tips 
## About the Santiago Landslide and creation of the Geologic Hazard Abatement District
In 1993, following intense El Nino rains that started in late 1992, a bluff in east Anaheim slid prompting the evacuation of homes and resulting in property damage, emergency remediation work and litigation.
Since then, a largely unseen system of wells, pumps, drains and meters has helped keep land stable and protect homes in the area of the 1993 landslide.
Following litigation and installation of the wells, pumps and drains, the Santiago Geologic Hazard Abatement District, GHAD for short, was formed to oversee and manage the system. The district is a state entity and independent of the city of Anaheim.
It's anticipated that in early 2024, the GHAD will run out of funding to keep the wells, pumps and drains operational. In anticipation of the pumps not working, its recommended residents limit outdoor watering. 
## Tips to limit outdoor watering
![Turn off or adjust sprinklers](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49895) Turn off or adjust sprinkler controllers
  

  

  

![Water no more than 3 days](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49892) Water no more than 3 days a week _or less_
  

  

  

![low-water irrigation](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49893) Install low-water drip irrigation
  

  

  

![Low water landscaping](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=49894) Use low-water landscaping _  
_

  6. Commercial EV Charging


#  Commercial EV Charging Guide 
## EV Charger Types
Charging hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs) and all-electric vehicles (EVs) requires plugging into an Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment (EVSE).  
  
Charging times vary based on how empty the battery is, how much energy it holds, battery type, and the type of charging equipment used. EV Chargers may require power infrastructure upgrades.
There are three major categories of chargers, based on the amount of power the charger can provide:
![120v Wall Outlet](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49235)
![Level 2 Charger](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49246)
![Level 3 Fast Charger](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=49243)
## Level 1 - 120 volt outlet
  * Plug into a standard 120 volt (V) wall outlet; alternating current (AC) plug up to 15 amperes and 1.8 kW
  * Requires a dedicated circuit
  * Portable, does not require installation 
  * Cord is a standard 3-prong household plug with a connector to the vehicle
  * Slowest charging time between 8 - 20 hours
  * Standard outlet: NEM 5-15R


## Level 2 - 240 volt outlet
  * Uses 240 volt(V), alternating current (AC) plug up to 80 amperes and 19.2 kW
  * Requires installation
  * Most residential level 2 EVSE will operate at lower power, using 30 amperes and deliver 7.2 kW of power
  * Charging time take 3 - 8 hours and can be done overnight
  * Most common in US for residential homes and business use
  * Standard outlet: SAE J1442/3


## Level 3 - DC fast charger
  * DC fast chargers are signiﬁcantly faster charging 
  * Can take less than 30 minutes to reach a full charge
  * Ideal for commercial businesses, public locations and heavy-duty fleets
  * Standard outlet: CCS or CHAdeMO


## Equipment, Connectors & Plugs
###   

For a list of charging units and other accessories, view Plug In America's accessory graph. 
  * Modern charging equipment and vehicles use a stand connector and plug receptacle based on the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE) J1772 standard. Vehicles with this plug receptacle can use any Level 1 or Level 2 EVSE. Major vehicle and charging system manufacturers support this standard, which should eliminate drivers’ concerns about whether their vehicle is compatible with the infrastructure.
  * In the US, Combined Charging System (CCS) and CHAdeMO are the two standard connector types for Level 3 (DC fast charging) EVSE.
  * Tesla chargers deviate from these standards, but adapters are available so that Tesla drivers can use standard, non-Tesla connectors.

#  Emprendedor@s Program 
![Welcome to Empadores](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51161)
![emprendedores 2025](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63572)
![spanish emprendedores flyer 2025](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63574)
The Emprendedor@s Program is an 8-Week class offered to Spanish-speaking entrepreneurs that teaches vital business tools and information. Those who complete the class receive office certificates from the City, Orange County, and the State of California. All graduates showcase their future business endeavors through Business Exhibitions.
New Classes every Monday beginning April 21-June 23 from 5-7 p.m. For more or to register call (714) 640-6908.
  

El Programa Emprendedor@s es una clase de 8 semanas ofrecida a emprendedores de habla hispana que enseña información y herramientas comerciales vitales. Quienes completen la clase recibirán certificados de oficina de la ciudad, el condado de Orange y el estado de California. Todos los graduados muestran sus futuros proyectos comerciales a través de Exposiciones Empresariales.
Nuevas clases todos los lunes a partir del 21 de abril hasta 23 de junio de 5-7 de la tarde. Para más información o para registrarse llame al (714) 640-6908.

![A graphic with icons and text "Strategic Plan Anaheim"](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63310)
# 2024-34 Strategic Plan Anaheim: priorities that guide our city
Anaheim is a dynamic city that delivers exceptional services to provide a high quality of life for residents, a thriving environment for businesses and a welcoming atmosphere for visitors.
In looking to the future, Anaheim always strives to improve and promote growth for the betterment of our city.
Anaheim has a strategic plan of community priorities and guiding principles to lead our work on behalf of residents, businesses and visitors. A strategic plan is a chance to think big about larger issues that impact the daily life of our city today and in years to come.
The goal of a strategic plan is to outline high-level priorities, objectives and issues that can help guide policy decisions by our City Council and the work and planning of city departments.
We know many critical priorities for Anaheim include addressing homelessness, affordable housing, public safety and more. Our strategic plan includes those topics as well as others.
The goals and priorities outlined in the strategic plan are reflective of what we have heard from the community.
Anaheim’s top high-level priorities include addressing blight and improving major street corridors, programs and benefits for older adults and expanding affordable housing.
We spent months collecting input from everyone in our community. On Feb. 10, the Anaheim City Council held a workshop to hear the findings, additional community feedback and began collaborating on what the Strategic Plan would look like.
At the April 2 City Council meeting, the Anaheim City Council unanimously approved the Strategic Plan for 2024-2034.
[![A graphic showing the Vision, Values and Mission of the city in its Strategic Plan Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63300)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63303/Strategic-Plan-Vision-Values-Mission)
[![A graphic showing the multiyear goals of the city in its Strategic Plan Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63301)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63304/Strategic-Plan-Multiyear-Goals)
[![A graphic showing the top priorities of the city in its Strategic Plan Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63302)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63305/Strategic-Plan-Top-Priorities)
[![Graphic link to click to read Anaheim's strategic plan Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=54630)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63311/Anaheim_CitywideStrategicPlan_2023_StrategicPlanReport-Final)
[![Graphic link to click on to watch a video of a City Council workshop on Strategic Plan Anaheim Opens in new window](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=54631)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=phemXv3G1IY)

#  Anaheim Hills Festival Center 
Image does not exist 
![Site Aerial Anaheim Hills Festival](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=51810)
## Project Summary
The applicant, Shea Properties Management Company, Inc., proposes to amend the General Plan and the Festival Specific Plan. The applicant also proposes to develop a multiple-family apartment building on the existing movie theater site.
## Application Materials
To access the application materials submitted by the applicant, please click on the items below.
  * [Letter of Request](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/51811/)


## Environmental Analysis
  * [Environmental Documents](https://www.anaheim.net/876/) (DEV2023-00043)
  * [Scoping Meeting Presentation](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/53415/) (February 12, 2024)
  * [Festival Anaheim Hills DEIR Planning Commission Workshop](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/65697/) (July 14, 2025)

  6. Cap-and-Invest Program


#  Cap-and-Invest Program 
The Cap-and-Invest Program is one of the state’s key implementation tools intended to encourage reduction of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions as part of Assembly Bill 32 (AB 32), passed in 2007. Under the C&T Program , which is overseen by the California Air Resources Board (ARB), GHG emission reductions are accomplished through the use of economic pricing mechanisms that place a cost on the emissions of GHGs. Anaheim Public Utilities, like all other electric utilities in California, is a covered entity under the Cap-and-Invest Program and, as such, is required to participate in the C&T Program . 
As the Utilities generates electricity from its fossil fuel power plants, GHG emissions are also generated. In order to comply with the Cap-and-Invest Program, the Utilities must surrender allowances, essentially permits, equal in number to the total metric tons of GHG emissions from these electric generation facilities. The Cap-and-Invest Program gives incentives to the Utilities, and all covered entities, to reduce GHG emissions in order to reduce costs for procuring the allowances necessary to cover their GHG emissions.
### Contact Us
  1. ##  Cap-and-Invest Program 
  
Call: 714-765-4471 or Email
  

### Office:
Mon - Fri: 8 am - 5 pm
[Anaheim West Tower  
201 S. Anaheim Blvd.   
Anaheim, CA 92805](https://anaheim.net/6553/Map-Directions)
[Utilities Directory](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?did=86)


## Anaheim Public Utilities Compliance
It is the Utilities goal to minimize the costs necessary to comply with the Cap-and-Invest Program requirements by employing a variety of measures that include, but are not limited to, managing how we generate electricity and how we acquire allowances. At this time, the Cap-and-Invest Program is not expected to result in any additional costs to customers; however, it does have the potential to add additional costs of up to $5 to $10 million annually, based in part, upon the future costs and the availability of allowances in the market. 
The status of the Utilities compliance with the Cap-and-Invest Program requirements is provided to the Public Utilities Board on a regular basis. Additionally, the Cap-and-Invest Program is only one of many regulations that are impacting the Utilities as a result of AB 32. Other regulations include the [Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS)](https://www.anaheim.net/612/Renewables-Portfolio-Standard), which requires all California electric utilities to meet 60% of their customer needs with renewable energy resources by 2030, as well as energy efficiency and conservation requirements.

#  Billing and Payment Info 
Understanding Your Bill
## Understanding Your Bill
As a not-for-profit agency, Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) offersresidential rates that are based strictly on the cost of providing electric and water services. Additionally, other City of Anaheim departments or State agencies may utilize APU’s billing system for other services. Below is a breakdown of the key charges you will see on your bill. To compare a typical domestic bill in Anaheim to the surrounding community, [utilize the electric or water bill calculators.](https://anaheim.net/6535/Residential-Bill-Calculator)
Domestic Rate NEM 1.0 Rate NEM 2.0 Rate
Domestic TOU Rate NEM 2.0 TOU Rate

### **How often will I be billed?**
The timing of your bill depends on the billing cycle your service address is in. Most residential customers are billed bimonthly and can expect to receive a bill approximately every 60 days. Commercial customers are billed monthly and will receive a bill approximately every 30 days.   
  
Bills are due and payable upon receipt and become delinquent after 15 days.
### **Can I receive my bills monthly instead of bi-monthly?**
We now offer a monthly billing option to our customers who agree to the program’s guidelines. For more information on monthly billing please call customer service at 714-765-3300 or [complete the form online](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Monthly-Billing-Enrollment-Form-130).
* * *
  1. [PAYMENT METHODS ACCEPTED](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tabb1fe4384-4716-4336-8fbe-09715e915973_0)
  2. [HOW TO MAKE A PAYMENT](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tabb1fe4384-4716-4336-8fbe-09715e915973_1)


  1. [PAYMENT METHODS ACCEPTED](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tabb1fe4384-4716-4336-8fbe-09715e915973_0)


Anaheim Public Utilities offers several convenient options for customers to pay their utility bills*.
  * In-person / Cashier's office: 
    * Cash 
    * Cashier's check
    * Check
    * Debit/credit card (Visa, MasterCard, Discover) 
    * Money order 
  * Automated phone service: 
    * Automated Clearing House (ACH)
    * Debit/credit card (Visa, MasterCard, Discover)
  * Online:
    * Automated Clearing House (ACH)
    * Debit/credit card (Visa, MasterCard, Discover)
  * Mail
    * Check 
    * Money order


Note: Anaheim Public Utilities does **not** accept debit/credit card payments from medium or large-rate commercial customers.
*Digital wallets are not accepted at this time.
  1. [HOW TO MAKE A PAYMENT](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tabb1fe4384-4716-4336-8fbe-09715e915973_1)


For your convenience, we offer multiple methods for paying your utility bill:
  * **By Mail** : You may mail your payment using a check, money order, or cashier’s check to:
    * City of Anaheim  
P.O Box 3069  
Anaheim, CA 92803
  * **In-Person Payment** : 
    * Visit our cashier's office within the Anaheim West Tower located at 201 S. Anaheim Blvd. 
    * Alternatively, utilize the drop box located on the south side of Anaheim West Tower, to the left of the main entry doors, for dropping off checks, money orders, or cashier’s checks.
  * **Online** : Access our secure online system, [MY ACCOUNT](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150), 24/7 to pay using a credit card or ACH.  
  

  * **By Phone** : Utilize our automated phone system by calling 714-765-3300, **option 1** , to pay with a credit card or ACH, available 24 hours a day.


  1. [ENROLL IN PAPERLESS BILLING](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tab7d3c1254-e9e3-4784-9b5f-b4bb478e36db_0)
  2. [ENROLL IN RECURRING PAYMENTS](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tab7d3c1254-e9e3-4784-9b5f-b4bb478e36db_1)


  1. [ENROLL IN PAPERLESS BILLING](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tab7d3c1254-e9e3-4784-9b5f-b4bb478e36db_0)


Help us reduce the need for paper bills and SAVE! Visit [MY ACCOUNT](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150) to enroll and easily access your bills anytime, anywhere.
  1. To enroll in paperless billing, first log into your online account 
  2. On the left-hand side of the page, click “My Profile” 
  3. Then click on “Communication Preferences” 
  4. On this page, elect to have paperless / email notifications


  1. [ENROLL IN RECURRING PAYMENTS](https://www.anaheim.net/6523/Billing-and-Payment-Info#tab7d3c1254-e9e3-4784-9b5f-b4bb478e36db_1)


Simplify your bill management and never miss a payment! 
Visit [MY ACCOUNT](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150) to set up automatic, recurring payments and enjoy peace of mind.
To enroll in recurring payments, first log into your online account. Once you are logged into your account, select the following:
  1. On the left-hand side of the page, click “Payment Options”
  2. Click on “View Online Payments”
    1. If you have not previously saved a payment method, click “Add Credit Card” or “Add Bank Account” in the bottom right-hand corner. Once completed, click “Payments” on the top of the page.
  3. On the right-hand side of the “Payment History” page, click on “Recurring Payments”
  4. Next, click on “Add Recurring Payments” on the right-hand side
  5. Fill out the page, click “Submit”, then your autopay will be activated!


*Please note: If you have an active outstanding balance, you would be required to manually pay your balance in full. Autopay will begin on the next billing cycle.

  6. Level Pay Plan


#  Level Pay Plan 
The Level Pay Plan offers a solution for those seeking **consistent utility bills** year-round.
Based on your usage from the last 12 months, **an average will be applied to your bills** for 11 months. Differences between actual usage and estimated usage will be sorted out in the last month.
  * An adjustment, or true-up, will be applied in the final bill to ensure that the total paid matches the actual usage by refunding an excess amount or charging for any additional usage beyond what was estimated.


Please note that the Level Pay Plan _does not_ reduce the amount of your bill. Customers are still responsible for paying the full amount of energy used.
## Plan Details & Requirements  
  

  * The Level Pay Plan option is available for customers on a **monthly billing cycle**. If you would like to sign up for the Level Pay Plan but are currently billed every other month (bimonthly), we can switch you to monthly payments. 
  * Eligible customers will need to have a**minimum of 12 months' billing history** at their current address to enroll in the Level Pay Plan. The history helps calculate an average amount that takes into consideration seasonal differences in usage.
  * Your utility account must be **in good standing** and **clear of any remaining balance**.


Note: At this time, **solar accounts are not eligible** for the Level Pay Plan. All customers are eligible to receive their bills monthly, without the Level Pay Plan. 
  * Call 714-765-3300 to enroll.


* * *
## Level Pay Plan – FAQ’S  
  

**_How is my Level Pay Plan calculated?_**  
A: The Level Pay Plan is calculated based on your prior 12-month usage history. It is an average amount billed each month for the first 11 months, and then a true-up bill will occur the last month. There will be quarterly reviews where you might see a change in your average Level Pay amount.
**_My Level Pay amount due changed from the last bill, why?_**   
A: Accounts are quarterly reviewed and adjusted accordingly. 
**_My Level Pay Plan amount due is too high, can it be adjusted?_**  
A: Accounts are quarterly reviewed and adjusted accordingly. If you feel the amount is still too high, please contact customer service.
**_Can I be removed from the Level Pay Plan program?_**  
A: Yes, customers can elect to be removed from the Level Pay Plan at any time. Customers can re-enroll in the program, but all outstanding charges must be current.
**_Can I get a payment extension or promise to pay?_**  
A: Yes. However, this would require you to be removed from the Level Pay Plan program. Customers can re-enroll in the program, but all outstanding charges must be current. 
**_Can credit on the Level Pay Plan be used towards the payment due?_**  
A: Credits resulting from the billing of the account cannot be used towards the Level Pay amount. 
**_Can I make an overpayment?_**  
A: Yes, overpayments on your account will be applied towards your future Level Pay amount due. 
**_I am moving to another address in Anaheim, will I still be enrolled in Level Pay Plan?_**  
A: Customers need to be at their place of business for a minimum of 12 months in order to have enough usage history to calculate a level pay amount. Please contact us to re-enroll on the anniversary date of your move. We’d be happy to sign you up again.
**_I missed my payment, what happens with my Level Pay Plan?_**   
A: Customers who fail to submit full payment by the due date will be candidates for removal. Customers can re-enroll after settling their outstanding balance.

  6. Congregate Lunch Program


#  Congregate Lunch Program 
![Congregate Lunch Photo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57375)
### What is a congregate lunch program?
The Congregate lunch program is a community event designed to provide healthy, nutritious meals to older adults at the Brookhurst Community Center. This program aims to improve the nutritional intake and overall health of participants and offer opportunities for social interaction and engagement. This helps reduce isolation and promotes a sense of community. The lunch program provides an opportunity for staff to get to know participants and assist them in connecting with other support services and resources, contributing to their overall well-being and independence.
### Do I have to pay for congregate lunch?
Voluntary contributions are welcome but not required, with a suggested donation of $3.00 for those who are age 60 and over. Guests under 60 can participate with a donation of $5.00. The congregate lunch program, as part of an older adult nutrition initiative, aims to reduce hunger, food insecurity, and malnutrition while promoting social engagement and supporting the overall health and well-being of our older Anaheim residents.
### Who can attend congregate lunch?
The congregate lunch program is open to all adults aged 60 and older. 
### What are the benefits of congregate lunches?
**Improved nutrition:** The lunches provide older adults with nutritious, well-balanced meals, helping them obtain essential nutrients such as protein, calcium, and fiber. This can help prevent chronic conditions like heart disease, hypertension, and osteoporosis. 
**Dietary variety:** Congregate lunches allow older adults to enjoy a variety of meals they might not want to or be able to prepare themselves. A varied, interesting diet is not only healthy but also enhances overall well-being.
**Social connection:** Social isolation can lead to loneliness, depression, high blood pressure, and early-onset dementia. Regularly attending the lunch program provides older adults the opportunity to meet new people, form lasting friendships, and build a strong support network. This foundation for a thriving social life can make independent living easier.
**Greater food security:** Regular access to nutritious food is crucial for overall health. The congregate lunch program helps address this issue, with 54% of participants reporting that a congregate meal provides at least half of their daily food intake.
### Where is the congregate lunch program located?
The Brookhurst Community Center is located at 2271 W. Crescent Ave., Anaheim, CA. Monday through Friday from 11:00a.m. - 1:00p.m. Entertainment is available to enjoy on most Thursdays. For information regarding transportation to the congregate lunch site or other Older Adult and Senior services please call (714) 765-4511.

#  Go Digital and Save! 
Help reduce the need for paper bills and save! Earn up to $15 in one-time bill credits by enrolling in paperless billing and automatic payments using your checking account. Sign up for power outage notifications on Anaheim Alert, the city’s digital hub for emergency notifications for additional savings.*
**Paperless Billing + Automatic Bill Pay = $10  
Anaheim Alert = $5**
![Paperless Billing](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37902)
[Log in](https://myapu.anaheim.net/iwr/user/login.seam?cid=150) to your online account to enroll in paperless billing to receive your bills via email.
Within your online account, click “My Profile”, then click on “Communication Preferences”. On this page, select to have paperless billing.
![Auto Bill Pay](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37904)
While in your online account, enroll in automatic payments using your checking account and never miss a payment.
![Anaheim Alert ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=37903)
Sign up for [Anaheim Alert](https://www.anaheim.net/5387/Anaheim-Alert) to receive updates and notifications for outages and emergencies. 
Be sure to select "Yes" under "Sign Up for Electric Outage Notification" to receive bill credit.
_*Must be a new participant in each program. Credit is not recurring._ For additional questions on how to enroll in these programs, please give us a call at 714-765-3300.

#  Older Adult Special Events 
At the City of Anaheim's Older Adult Program, we believe that life is a celebration, and every moment matters. Our special events are designed to bring joy, connection, and creativity to our Older Adult community. Whether you're looking to meet new friends, explore new hobbies, or simply enjoy a fun day out, we have something for everyone. From engaging workshops and lively social gatherings to enriching educational sessions, we invite you to join us in making wonderful memories together. Check out our upcoming events and discover how you can participate in the joy of community!

#  Resource Links for Older Adults 
Aging gracefully is about staying informed, connected, and empowered. At our Anaheim's Older Adult Program resource page, we provide a wide range of tools, services, and information designed to help older adults and their families navigate the challenges and opportunities of aging. Whether you're looking for health and wellness resources, social activities, housing options, or caregiving support, we're here to guide you every step of the way. Explore our community of support and discover how you can live a vibrant, fulfilling life at every stage of your journey.
Most of these links are to third party organizations or agencies, therefore, City staff are not responsible for answering general or detailed questions about the resource. We encourage you to to click the link, if applicable, or call the associated number to speak to a subject matter expert of that resource. 
  

### GENERAL CITY, COUNTY, & STATE BASED RESOURCES
|

#  Anaheim's Older Adult Program Brookhurst Community Center 
  1. ![Brokhurst 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58659)
  2. ![Brookhurst 2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=58660)
  3. ![Seniors at a Table](https://www.anaheim.net/6567/Older-Adult-Program---Brookhurst-Communi)
  4. ![Senior Group Photo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57847)


Anaheim’s Older Adult Program offers a vibrant array of activities that cater to a wide range of interests and abilities for those age 55 and over. Each day at the Anaheim's Older Adult Hubs, you’ll find a dynamic environment where connections flourish and friendships are formed.
The Hubs provide something for everyone, whether you’re looking for gentle exercises, like classes for those with limited mobility, or more challenging activities, like longer walks. In addition to fitness options, we offer health lectures, information on health insurance, community resources, and seasonal workshops and luncheons.
Whether your goal is to stay active, learn something new, or simply enjoy the company of others, we invite you to join us.**For more information, please call 714-765-3415.**
[**Brookhurst Community Center Older Adults Program Printable Calendar**](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/50916/Brookhurst-Community-Center-Calendar)
****Special Events********![Spring](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=70244)****

2271 W. Crescent Ave.  
Anaheim, CA 92801  
  
Phone: 714-765-3415

#  Anaheim's Older Adult Program - East Anaheim Community Center & Gym 
  1. ![East Anaheim Building](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57855)
  2. ![East Anaheim Pickball 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57856)
  3. ![East Anaheim Pickleball 2](https://www.anaheim.net/6570/Older-Adult-Program---East-Anaheim-Commu)
  4. ![East Anahem Pickleball 3](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=57858)


Anaheim’s Older Adult Program offers a vibrant array of activities that cater to a wide range of interests and abilities for those age 55 and over. Each day at the Anaheim's Older Adult Hubs, you’ll find a dynamic environment where connections flourish and friendships are formed.
The Hubs provide something for everyone, whether you’re looking for gentle exercises, like classes for those with limited mobility, or more challenging activities, like longer walks. In addition to fitness options, we offer health lectures, information on health insurance, community resources, and seasonal workshops and luncheons.
Whether your goal is to stay active, learn something new, or simply enjoy the company of others, we invite you to join us. **For more information, please call 714-765-3912.**
[**East Anaheim Community Center Older Adult Program Printable Calendar**](https://anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/44849/East-Anaheim-Community-Center-Calendar)

Anaheim, CA 92808

  5. Ambulance Program


#  Ambulance Program 
![Honda - Ambulances cropped](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=55991)
The Anaheim Fire & Rescue Department (AF&R) is a proud and dedicated emergency medical services (EMS) provider. EMS incidents account for approximately 80% of AF&R's call volume. The goal of AF&R is to deliver the highest quality of medical care to members of the community with dignity, respect, and compassion.
Fees are charged for the emergency medical care provided patients. The City currently bills ambulance transport fees as well as a first responder fee. While the ambulance transport fee partially covers the cost of the transport, the first responder fee provides for the initial treatment and evaluation, regardless whether an ambulance transport occurs.
Even if a patient has insurance, their insurance may not cover the cost of the ambulance transport or may pay only a portion of the bill. This applies to Medicare, Medi-Cal, and private insurers. AF&R's billing contractor, Wittman Enterprises, will make every attempt to obtain payment from a patient's insurance carrier(s) but, if they are unable to verify insurance coverage or an insurance carrier either denies a claim or pays only a portion of the bill, then the patient is responsible for any unpaid charges resulting from the ambulance transport.
**Approved Ambulance Rates (effective July 1, 2025)**
[Paramedic Fees for Ambulance Program (Resolution 2025-057)](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/55994/FY-2024-25---Paramedic-Fees-for-Ambulance-Program-Resolution-2025-057)
**Ambulance Billing Questions**
Information regarding an ambulance bill may be obtained from Wittman Enterprises at [www.webillems.com](https://www.webillems.com) or (800) 906-6552. From the website, a patient or authorized representative can obtain the balance on the account, update the patient's insurance information, or make a payment.
Representatives from Wittman Enterprises are available Monday-Friday, 8:00am - 4:30pm PST. To protect patient privacy, representatives must speak directly with the patient or the minor patient's parent or legal guardian. Unless authorized by a patient to receive medical information and records, third-party callers may only update a patient's insurance information.
**FireMedics Program**
Members of the FireMedics Program are not responsible for the first responder fee or any out-of-pocket costs related to an ambulance transport (after Wittman Enterprises bills their insurance). Payments made by a patient's insurance will be accepted as payment in full, ensuring no out-of-pocket expenses for the FireMedics member. The cost to join the FireMedics program is $5 per month or, $60 per year. To learn more about the FireMedics program and join, please [click here](https://www.anaheim.net/615/Anaheim-Fire-Rescue-FireMedics-Program).
[![Anaheim Fire Patch Logo 275px](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=44704)](https://www.anaheim.net/6096)

  5. Water Quality


#  Water Quality 
Delivering high-quality water to Anaheim residents and businesses has been a core responsibility of Anaheim Public Utilities since 1879.
We closely monitor the quality of water we deliver to you and our other customers. Many of the issues that water providers are facing are a result of industrial and manufacturing processes that have made their way into the water table for decades. Each year, we conduct more than 44,000 tests on samples drawn from our own facilities and from customer locations throughout our service area. Our tests are in addition to thousands of tests conducted by our water suppliers. We test for over 100 possible substances in our drinking water, most of which are not detected at all in the water we deliver to you.  
  
All of our tests and behind-the-scenes efforts are aimed at one goal: making sure that your water consistently meets all the standards set for quality by California and federal regulations.  

## Water Quality Reports
View our [Water Quality Report](https://www.anaheim.net/2092/)
Available in English and Spanish.
The California Health and Safety Code, Section 116470(b), requires California water utilities serving more than 10,000 connections to compare their water every three years to California Public Health Goals and the United States Environmental Protection Agency (U.S. EPA) Maximum Contaminant Level Goals (MCLGs).
![Water_quality_testingtubes_inlab](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=13965)
  

[Spanish Video](https://youtu.be/RFFTVPOsn1Q)

Residents, businesses and schools can rest assured that—as your not-for-profit municipal utility—the city has its own water quality laboratory, which offers free water quality testing.
**APU NEVER PERFORMS UNSOLICITED WATER TESTING**
**APU NEVER SELLS WATER QUALITY TESTING KITS**

  5. Ethics


#  Ethics 
## **Ethics Division**
The Ethics Division was created in August 2024 and is a new division within the City Attorney’s Office primarily providing legal advice and counsel in the areas of ethics, conflicts of interest and permissible political activities to the city council, department heads, executive staff, boards and commissions, and other city staff. The division also provides training and information, advises on compliance with required filings related to financial interests, conducts ethics policy analysis, evaluates existing laws related to ethics and makes recommendations when appropriate, and monitors State and other legislation as it relates to ethics to ensure that the city is compliant. Through these efforts, the Ethics Division helps ensure that the City is committed to its transparency, accountability, and ethical mandates. 
##  **Ethics Division Personnel**
The Ethics Division of the City Attorney’s Office consists of an assistant city attorney who serves as the [Ethics Officer](https://www.anaheim.net/Directory.aspx?EID=885) for the City, manages the division, and reports to the City Attorney.

  7. Fluoridation


#  Fluoridation 
On April 27, 2015, the U.S. Public Health Service (PHS) published a recommendation that the optimal dosage for fluoride in water is 0.7 parts per million (ppm). Previously, the recommended dosage was expressed as a range from 0.7 to 1.2 ppm. PHS concludes that fluoridation at 0.7 ppm provides all the benefits for dental health with virtually no other health risks. High levels of fluoride can lead to fluorosis, a white mottling of tooth enamel.
Anaheim does not add fluoride to its water supply, but the Metropolitan Water District of Southern California (MWD) has been adding fluoride at its five water treatment plants since October 2007.
MWD treated water typically provides about 15% of Anaheim’s total water supply. MWD water is received at seven interconnection points in Anaheim, with the amount of water delivered dependent on consumer demand and the availability of Anaheim’s other supplies. Most of Anaheim’s customers receive some MWD treated water, but most of their water comes from Anaheim’s other sources.
MWD chose to fluoridate because it was requested by the County Health Officers of the six counties where MWD provides water. It is the position of the County Health Officers, the Centers for Disease Control, the California Dental Association, the State of California, and several other health organizations, that fluoride, in the proper dosage is a benefit to dental health. 
MWD adjusts the naturally occurring fluoride level in their water, which ranges from 0.1 to 0.4 parts per million (ppm), to the optimal range for dental health as determined by the California State Water Resources Control Board. Anaheim’s groundwater typically contains about 0.4 ppm of naturally occurring fluoride, but there are no plans to add fluoride.
More information is available about MWD's fluoridation project on its Web site at   
[MWD's Fluoridation Plan](http://www.mwdh2o.com/).   
  
To learn more about Anaheim Public Utilities' water quality check out our [Water Quality web page](https://www.anaheim.net/657/Water-Quality).   
  
  
Updated April 28, 2015

  5. Anaheim Public Art


![Anaheim Public Art photo collage of art works as an intro to a page about public art in Anaheim ](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=59042)
![Public art in Anaheim text header](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=59053)
Public art makes life better in Anaheim.
Murals, sculptures and other works across Anaheim build community, honor history, drive imagination, enhance well-being and make our city an even more fun place to visit.
We welcome and promote public art through the Anaheim Public Art Program, the result of a multiyear effort to develop and implement a public art plan.
See the Public Art Plan [here](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63298/Anaheim-Public-Art-Plan-FINAL).
Explore here to learn more about public art in Anaheim, including applying for murals.

  7. Income Qualified Third-Party Water Bill Rebate


#  Income Qualified Third-Party Water Bill Rebate 
## Program Overview 
  
Anaheim Public Utilities’ Income-Qualified Water Rebate Program provides**up to a $120 rebate per year** to qualifying customers who receive a third-party water bill.  
  

##  How it Works  
  

###  To be eligible, customers must:   
  

    * Receive their water bill from a third-party provider 
    * Be a senior (62 years of age or older), long-term disabled, or military veteran 
    * Meet the [income qualifications](https://anaheim.net/6423/Qualifying-Income-Levels)
    * Have an electric account with Anaheim Public Utilities that is in good standing 


  

### The application window to apply is from **January 1 – January 31** , and**June 1 – June 30**. 
###   

Submissions will be closed outside of these two periods. 
  

## How to Apply 
###    
Customers**already enrolled in our Utility Discount Program** require the following:   
  

  1. A completed Income Qualified Water Rebate Application ([English](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58586/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application-as-of-010925_)) ([Spanish](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58640/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application_Spanish))
  2. Up to the past 12 immediate months’ water bills from a third-party provider 


###  Customers**not enrolled in the Utility Discount Program** require the following:   
  

  1. A completed Income Qualified Water Rebate Application ([English](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58586/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application-as-of-010925_)) ([Spanish](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/58640/IQ-Water-Rebate-Application_Spanish))
  2. Proof of senior, disability, or veteran status 
     * _To be eligible as a senior_ , you must attach a copy of identification proving your age is at least 62 years old 
     * _If you are claiming a disability_ , you must attach a copy of your latest SSI statement or a letter from your physician, if you are claiming a disability 
     * T _o be eligible as a military veteran,_ you must attach a copy of either a DD-214, Veteran Heath Identification (VHIC), or Common Access Card 
  3. Up to the past 12 immediate months’ water bills from a third-party provider 


  
  

###  **Submit** your application and supporting documents to:   
  

**Email** : incentives@anaheim.net
**Mail** : 
201 S Anaheim Blvd., Suite #801,
* * *
##  Find out more by giving us a call at 714-765-4250.  
  

_The incentive offer is on a first-come, first-service basis and is effective until funding is expended, or the_ _program is discontinued by the City of Anaheim_.
[![Public Utilities Home Page](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=42943)](https://www.anaheim.net/6099)

#  87200 Filers 
In accordance with Government Code Section 87505 the following is a list of City officials identified as Section 87200 filers who are required to file Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700):
### City Council
  * Ashleigh E. Aitken, Mayor
  * Carlos A. Leon, City Council Member (Mayor Pro Tem, District 2)
  * Ryan Balius, City Council Member (District 1)
  * Natalie Rubalcava, City Council Member (District 3)
  * Norma Campos Kurtz, City Council Member (District 4)
  * Kristen M. Maahs, City Council Member (District 5)
  * Natalie Meeks, City Council Member (Council Member, District 6)


Copies of Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700) for City Council Members are available on the [City’s Transparency in City Government webpage](https://www.anaheim.net/1783/Transparency).
### Planning Commission
  * Christopher P. Walker, Planning Commissioner (Chairperson, District 5)
  * Jeanne Tran-Martin, Planning Commissioner (Vice-chairperson, District 2)
  * Abdulmageed Abdulrahman, Planning Commissioner (Vice-chairperson, District 1)
  * Michelle Lieberman, Planning Commissioner (District 3)
  * LuisAndres Perez, Planning Commissioner (District 4)
  * Amelia Castro, Planning Commissioner (District 6)
  * Deidre Kelly, Planning Commissioner (At-large)


### City Officials
  * Greg Garcia, Interim City Manager
  * Rober Fabela, City Attorney
  * Deborah Moreno, City Treasurer/Finance Director


Copies of the Statements of Economic Interests (Form 700) may be obtained by visiting the Office of the City Clerk:
City of Anaheim – Office of the City Clerk  
200 S. Anaheim Boulevard, Suite 217  
Anaheim, CA 2805  
(714) 765-5166
Statements of Economic Interest filed by certain State and Local Government agency elected officers are available in electronic format on the [Fair Political Practices Commission (FPPC) website](https://www.fppc.ca.gov/).

  5. Anaheim Chief Communications Officer: Mike Lyster


#  Anaheim Chief Communications Officer: Mike Lyster 
Mike Lyster serves as chief communications officer for the city of Anaheim, one of California's largest cities with 350,000 residents, 25 million yearly visitors and 20,000 businesses.
He joined as chief communications officer in 2015.
Lyster oversees city communications, including working with the media, social media, AnaheimTV, Anaheim magazine, newsletters, graphic design and other types of information sharing.
Lyster works directly with Anaheim's mayor, City Council and departments. He also works with residents, businesses and community groups in the city, all with a goal of informing people about Anaheim.
Anaheim Communications is part of the city manager's office and represents Anaheim Public Utilities, the city attorney's office and works with city departments across Anaheim. 
Before coming to Anaheim, Lyster served as vice president of corporate communications for the Irvine Company, one of the nation's largest real estate developers and owners.
He started his career as a journalist, including serving for more than a decade as editor of the Orange County Business Journal.
He grew up going to Angels games, Disneyland and riding his bike or taking the bus to Anaheim Plaza, among other many fond Anaheim memories.
![Mike Lyster 2025](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63314)
  1. [City Information and Alerts](https://www.anaheim.net/184/City-Information-and-Alerts) [](https://www.anaheim.net/6643/Anaheim-Chief-Communications-Officer-Mik "Expand City Information and Alerts Submenu")
    1. [Anaheim 311](https://www.anaheim.net/189/Anaheim-311) [](https://www.anaheim.net/6643/Anaheim-Chief-Communications-Officer-Mik "Expand Anaheim 311 Submenu")

#  Concerts in the Park 
We are excited to announce our 2026 Concerts in the Park dates!
Shows will be held on the following days, times and locations:
  * Thursday, June 11, 2026 at Modjeska Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, June 18, 2026 at Ponderosa Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, June 25, 2026 at Pioneer Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, July 9, 2026 at Ronald Reagan Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, July 16, 2026 at Ronald Reagan Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, July 23, 2026 at Ronald Reagan Park from 6:30pm-8pm
  * Thursday, July 30, 2026 at Ronald Reagan Park from 6:30pm-8pm


Mark your calendars! We will release our performance line up soon!

#  Vendor Outreach Day Registration 
Are you interested in doing business with the city? Join us for **Vendor Outreach Day** , an opportunity to meet with procurement staff, ask questions, and more. 
We are working on getting these scheduled on a quarterly basis. New dates to be published in the Fall.

  6. Electrical System Inspections


#  Electrical System Inspections 
Anaheim Public Utilities (APU) relies on inspections of electrical infrastructure to ensure the safety and reliability of service and for consistency with state guidelines as industry best practice. Inspections are critical for assessing the physical condition of infrastructure and for allowing APU to prioritize capital projects. Inspections also proactively identify equipment that may need immediate attention to avoid prolonged unplanned outages.
## OVERHEAD INSPECTION
![Overhead Inspection](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63334)
![Overhead Inspection](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63333)
![Powerlines](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63332)
### What to Expect:
  * Overhead inspections include utility poles, power lines, transformers, switches, capacitors, automation devices, and other utility equipment.
  * The city’s contractor, Intec Services Inc., will schedule an inspection time with customers.
  * Overhead inspections will last approximately 15-20 minutes, focusing on identifying any damage or signs of deterioration.
  * Access to private property may be necessary where utility poles are located along rear property lines.
  * Some minor excavation and drilling may take place around the utility poles.
  * There will be temporary noise associated with these activities.  
  



### How to Prepare:
  * Kindly ensure that gates are unlocked and pathways are clear for field personnel to access the utility pole.
  * Please secure any pets during the inspection period.
  * APU understands this may disrupt your normal activities. We appreciate your patience and cooperation.


### Questions?
See our frequently asked questions sheet: [Overhead Electrical Inspections FAQs](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63451/FAQs-Overhead-03-2025)
### Inspection Contacts:
  * **City’s Contractor (Intec Services Inc.):**  
Chris McMahon: 559-731-0534
  * **Anaheim Public Utilities Engineering:**  
John Vu: 714-765-4164


* * *
## UNDERGROUND INSPECTION
![UG-Inspection 1](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63452)
![UG-Inspection 2](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63453)
![UG-Inspection 3](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63454)
### What to Expect:
  * Underground inspections are conducted on electrical equipment such as transformers, cables and switches, and include an assessment of the structural integrity of underground structures.
  * Underground inspections will typically last approximately 30-45 minutes, depending on traffic control and access requirements to underground vaults.
  * Access to private properties, including apartments and commercial businesses, may be required where pad-mounted equipment is located. 
  * The city’s contractor, Henkels & McCoy West, LLC, will schedule an inspection time with affected customers.
  * There will be temporary parking restrictions, and “No Parking” signs will be posted 72 hours prior to inspection.  
  



### How to Prepare:
  * Kindly ensure a clear pathway for field personnel to access pad-mounted equipment and underground vaults.
  * Please adhere to the temporary “No Parking” signs and relocate vehicles as necessary. 
  * APU understands this may disrupt your normal activities. We appreciate your patience and cooperation.


### Questions?
See our frequently asked questions sheet: [Underground Electrical Inspections FAQs](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/63450/FAQs-Underground-03-2025)
### Inspection Contacts:
  * **City’s Contractor (Henkels & McCoy West, LLC): **  
Phil Schuster: 909-322-5067
  * **Anaheim Public Utilities Engineering:**  
John Vu: 714-765-4164

  5. Anaheim Bottled Water Donation Program


#  Anaheim Bottled Water Donation Program 
Anaheim Public Utilities is proud to support local non-profits and community organizations by providing free bottled water for Anaheim events open to the general public. 
To ensure your organization qualifies, please review the following guidelines:
### Program Guidelines:
  * **Advance Notice** : Requests must be submitted at least **10 business days** before the event.
  * **Location:** Events must be held **within Anaheim city limits** and **be free and open to the public**(sporting events are excluded).
  * **Usage:** Please ensure all donated water is distributed to the public free of charge and used solely for community benefit.
  * **Annual Limit:** Organizations are eligible for up to 10 cases per year* (24 bottles per case), subject to availability.


### How to Apply
Submit your request via the [Bottled Water Request Form](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/Anaheim-Bottled-Water-Donation-Request-325)
_* Case-maximum will transition to 5 cases per year starting July 1, 2026._
![Anaheim water bottles on display](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=63702)
Each case contains twenty-four 16.9-fluid-ounce bottles.

  6. Fire Hazard Severity Zones


#  Fire Hazard Severity Zones 
Living in California is great. But with the climate we have comes the risk of wildfires.
While not fun to think about, the threat of wildfires is real and always with us, particularly in east Anaheim.
State law requires Cal Fire to classify land into Fire Hazard Severity Zones. The three zones are: moderate, high and very high. These zones evaluate hazard, not risk, based on factors such as fire history, likelihood, terrain and probability.
Cal Fire updated the zones in Spring of 2025 and can be viewed below or by [clicking here](https://experience.arcgis.com/experience/5065c998b4b0462f9ec3c6c226c610a9/page/Compare-old-and-new-LRA-FHSZ). You can view the updated maps in person at Anaheim West Tower located at 201 S. Anaheim Blvd.
You can find additional, detailed information on Fire Hazard Severity Zones [here](https://osfm.fire.ca.gov/what-we-do/community-wildfire-preparedness-and-mitigation/fire-hazard-severity-zones).
If you have questions, contact Anaheim's Wildfire Mitigation Specialist Adrian Abel at (714) 765-4039 or aabel@anaheim.net.  
  

## **Additional Resources**
[Home hardening rebates](https://anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety)
Properties in or near a fire zone are at a higher risk for wildfire damage. Be energy smart and protect your home by taking advantage of rebates on fire-resistant measures, planting approved shade trees, and following any Anaheim Fire & Rescue recommendations to improve safety in wildfire zones.  
  
Learn more about rebates for home hardening [here](https://anaheim.net/5418/Fire-Safety).
  

[Be Ready Anaheim](https://www.anaheim.net/6102/Be-Ready-Anaheim-A-Hazard-Mitigation-Pla)
Every day, Anaheim's emergency response teams ― police, fire, public utilities and public works ― are ready to serve our city in times of need.
But community preparedness is more than that.
It is people, families, volunteers, neighborhoods, workplaces, community groups and organizations prepared for emergencies and resiliency.
Be Ready Anaheim is our partnership with the community to be ready for, to ride out and to recover from disasters and other challenges that confront our city.
Be Ready Anaheim is the power of preparedness. Learn more [here](https://www.anaheim.net/6102/Be-Ready-Anaheim-A-Hazard-Mitigation-Pla).
  1. [Know Your Way](https://www.anaheim.net/5204/Know-Your-Way) [](https://www.anaheim.net/6660/Fire-Hazard-Severity-Zones "Expand Know Your Way Submenu")

#  Pickleball Leagues 
The Anaheim Community Services Department offers pickleball leagues for players 18 years and older. The leagues operate 4 seasons annually, with all teams scheduled to play a minimum of 8 or 10 games. Every season is concluded with a single elimination playoff.
For more information and to register please visit www.teamsideline.com/anaheim
Seasons
Winter: January - March
Spring: April - June
Summer: July - September
Fall: September - December
Cost
Team Registration (2 players): $260
Weekly Official’s Fees: $30 (paid in cash at gym)
League Nights
Thursday at East Anaheim Gym
Sunday morning/afternoon at East Anaheim Gym Coming Soon

#  Downtown Anaheim Family Resource Center 
  1. ![Downtown Logo for Slideshow](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64338)
  2. ![Family in front of a celebration banner](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64647)
  3. ![Group of women holding flowers](https://www.anaheim.net/6662/Downtown-Family-Resource-Center)
  4. ![Woman smiling in a room](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentID=64649)


  

The Downtown Anaheim Family Resource Center is one of three welcoming, family-focused centers operated by the City of Anaheim. Designed as a community hub, the center brings together residents, public agencies, and local organizations to offer a wide range of supportive services that promote safety, stability, and healthy development for Anaheim families.
On-site staff provide needs assessments, coordinate human services and programs, and ensure comprehensive support for families. Bilingual (English/Spanish) staff are available at all three centers.
### **Case Management Services**
Our case management service can help you sustain your household by providing individualized case assessment, planning, and care to promote quality living,
  * Community Resource Information & Referrals
  * Transportation Resources
  * Diaper Distributions
  * Food Distributions
  * Utility Application Assistance
  * Form Translation & Completion Assistance
  * Enrollment Assistance
    * Medi-Cal, CalFresh, Covered California


  * [   
GET HELP NOW  
  
](https://www.anaheim.net/FormCenter/Community-Services-4/Downtown-Anaheim-FRC-DAFRC-Get-Help-Now--388)


For more information on the programs and services offered at our other Family Resource Centers, visit [Miraloma Park Family Resource Center](http://www.anaheim.net/2206/Miraloma-Park-Family-Resource-Center) or [Ponderosa Park Family Resource Center](http://www.anaheim.net/1136/Ponderosa-Park-Family-Resource-Center).
### Workshops and classes require registration

#  Anaheim Housing Authority 
  * [ APPLICANTS ](https://www.anaheim.net/675/Applicants)


  * [ Current program participants ](https://www.anaheim.net/733/Participants)


  * [ OWNERS & LANDLORDs ](https://www.anaheim.net/754/Owners)


**Apply to Project-Based Voucher (PBV) Waiting Lists Here:**
  * [**AHA's Project-Based Voucher (PBV) Application (Not Section 8/Housing Choice Voucher-HCV)**](https://anaheim.myhousing.com/)


  
**Important Updates:**
  

## AZURE APARTMENTS PBV WAITING LIST OPENING 
The Anaheim Housing Authority will begin accepting pre-application referrals for the AZURE APARTMENTS PBV project on Wednesday, July 2, 2025. Referrals must come from a Homeless Service Provider. Located at 1251 N. Harbor Blvd., Anaheim, CA 92801, the project includes 87 PBV units for individuals who are chronically homeless, homeless, at risk of homelessness, or eligible under the HOPWA program. [**Please visit this website for the full details, public notices, and information on waiting list preferences**](https://www.anaheim.net/1947/Public-Documents). 
## CA HOUSING RIGHTS 
California tenants are protected by a wide range of housing rights. For example, you may be protected from overly high rent increases and certain types of evictions. There are also laws protecting you from discrimination, retaliation, and lockouts where the landlord has not gotten a court order to evict you. To learn more about your rights as a tenant, visit the[**CA Attorney General's Office Landlord-Tenant pag****e**](https://oag.ca.gov/consumers/general/landlord-tenant-issues) or view the flyers below:
  * [**Know Your Rights as a California Tenant**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56619/Know-Your-Rights-Tenants-English)
  * **[Conozca sus derechos como inquilino de California](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56618/Know-Your-Rights-Tenants-Spanish)**
  * [**Biết Rõ Các Quyền của Quý Vị với tư cách là Người Thuê Nhà tại California**](https://www.anaheim.net/DocumentCenter/View/56617/Know-Your-Rights-Tenants-Vietnamese)


**Office Update:**  
The Anaheim Housing Authority is strongly encouraging email and telephone communication with our office. Staff continueto answer phones and emails Monday-Friday 8 a.m. - 5 p.m. For holiday closures[, please click here](https://www.anaheim.net/673/Holiday-Closures). 
  * Staff can provide forms via mail, fax, or email
    1. Common forms for applicants on the waiting list can be [found here](https://www.anaheim.net/675/Applicants)
    2. Common forms for existing program participants can be [found here](https://www.anaheim.net/733/Participants)
  * Current program participants can complete their annual recertification by mail. Your Housing Specialist will contact you if necessary.  Participants of the program are required to have an appointment to meet with a housing specialist.   

  * Program participants, vendors, and applicants submit documents via:
    * Drop-Box located on the first-floor lobby at: Anaheim City Hall - WEST Tower at 201 S. Anaheim Blvd, Anaheim, CA 92805
    * Mail: 201 S. Anaheim Blvd. Suite 201 Anaheim, CA 92805
    * Fax: (714) 765-4643
    * Email their Housing Specialist directly.  

  * Information about your application status cannot be provide by phone or in person. [Check the status of your application here](https://housingportal.anaheim.net/AHAStatusCheck/).


**Anaheim Housing Authority**  
The Anaheim Housing Authority administers federally-funded programs and grants allocated by the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) in the City of Anaheim, CA. The Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) program offers two housing options: tenant-based and project-based. The tenant-based program allows assisted households to use a rental assistance voucher in open rental market units. And the project-based program ties a voucher to a specific unit in which an assisted household can move into. More information regarding each program can be found below.
### **Housing Programs include:**
  * [Housing Choice Voucher (HCV) Program](https://www.anaheim.net/1989/Housing-Choice-Voucher-Program) - allows a participating family to move into a unit of their choice if the property owner has agreed to participate in the HCV Program.
  * [Project Based Voucher (PBV) Program](https://www.anaheim.net/761/Project-Based-Voucher-Waiting-Lists) - provides rental assistance at selected apartment complexes within the City. 
  * [Affordable Housing Lists](https://www.anaheim.net/770/Affordable-Housing) – The City has agreements with owners of specific units to keep rent levels below regular market rates. As affordable units become available, individuals on the Interest List will be notified by mail of the availability. **This is not a rental assistance program.**


###  **HUD Programs include:**  

  * Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) Program – The CDBG program provides funding to agencies and businesses that provide a benefit to low and moderate income persons, prevent or eliminate slums and blight, or meet needs having a particular urgency.
  * Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG) Program – The ESG program provides funding to nonprofit organizations and public entities sponsoring projects that’s provide a benefit to low-and moderate-income homeless persons, provides shelters for the homeless, but also aid in the transition of this population to permanent housing.
  * HOME Investments Partnerships (HOME) Program – The HOME program provides funds to local government’s to implement local housing strategies designed to increase the supply of affordable housing.
  * Housing Opportunity for Persons with AIDS (HOPWA) Program – The HOPWA program provides funding for housing and related support services of low–income persons living with human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS).

#  Trees for Turf 
Cut your lawn irrigation in half! Save water by planting trees in place of your turf removal area.
Participants can receive trees up to four (4) FREE trees to place in areas where turf was removed.
  * Participation in Turf Replacement program is not required. 
  * Must have removed your Turf within the past 12 months.
  * Over 40 available species to choose from.


If you have questions, please contact TreePower at 714-491-8733
  * [ SIGN UP ](https://anaheim.net/FormCenter/Public-Utilities-6/TreePower-Program-58)

#  Build More Homes Initiative 
The Build More Homes Initiative is Anaheim's way of helping more affordable housing communities get built.
Sometimes housing projects don't have enough money to move forward and this program helps fill some of the financial gap to make it happen. 
It's funded through Anaheim's Local Housing Trust Fund, which supports affordable housing. Our city can directly invest in projects and help more people find homes they can afford.
Every project is looked at individually to ensure it meets the rules and requirements in our Local Housing Trust Fund before any funding is given. 
For more email: affordablehousing@anaheim.net
  

![Build More Homes Initiative Photo](https://www.anaheim.net/ImageRepository/Document?documentId=69539)
